for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_is_abstract.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To view the inherited properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734490 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04531: (2026x) Working with Strategic Taxonomy table

- page_id: `272734490`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734490/2026x+Working+with+Strategic+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:57.304+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) UAF 1.3 > (2026x) Strategy > (2026x) Strategic Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Strategic Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button to add a Capability. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Capability dialog, select the desired Capabilities. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Strategic Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to add a Capability. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select the desired Capabilities. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734266 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04532: (2026x) Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram

- page_id: `272734266`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734266/2026x+Working+with+Strategic+Typical+Deployment+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:39.464+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) UAF 1.3 > (2026x) Strategy > (2026x) Strategic Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

545192467

100.00002%

545192470

545192464

**On this page**

545192469

##### Creating Strategic Typical Deployment table

The context of the Strategic Typical Deployment table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Strategic Structure' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Actual Responsible Resources, and the columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Phases***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a StrategicTypical Deployment table

To create an empty Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a Strategic Roadmap package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > Strategic Typical Deployment .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > Strategic Typical Deployment .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. A***Phases***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

To remove Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Typical Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Phases***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Typical Deployment table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627893252

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Logging background actions' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731798 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04533: (2026x) Working with StV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `272731798`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731798/2026x+Working+with+StV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:41.488+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating StV-3 diagram

When you choose to create StV-3 diagram, **StV-3 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a StV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click StV-3 Capability Phasing .
3. In the StV-3 Capability Phasing dialog, click Add . StV-3 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the wizard.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the StV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create StV-3”.

Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the **Environment Options** dialog go to **UAF** tab. In the General properties, set **Show CV-3/ StV-3 Creation Wizard each Time** 
**Creating New Chart** to false.

To create a StV-3 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click StV-3 Capability Phasing .
3. In the StV-3 Capability Phasing dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank StV-3 diagram is created.

##### Adding capabilities to StV-3 chart

To add a new capability

- On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add a new capability provision

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the Capability Provision Creation Wizard .
4. Click Finish , when you are done.

To add an existing capability

1. On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding sub capabilities to StV-3 chart

To add a new sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Sub Capability .
3. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add an existing sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add existing Sub Capability .
3. In the opened Select Capability dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

##### Removing capabilities and sub capabilities from StV-3 chart

To remove a capability or sub capability from the chart

1. Select a capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Remove From Chart .

To delete a capability or sub capability from the model

1. Select a capability or sub capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete .

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Display all sub capabilities in StV-3 chart

To display all sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.
2. In the StV-3 chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2ee3a39-6b3a-47c7-a1e3-49669876886b" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Creating StV-3 diagram</h3><p>When you choose to create StV-3 diagram, <strong>StV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a StV-3 diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog, click <strong>Add</strong>. <strong>StV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42bbac58-44fe-4e64-a652-17776450832f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the StV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create StV-3”.</p><p>Also you can disable the wizard. Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UAF</strong> tab. In the General properties, set <strong>Show CV-3/ StV-3 Creation Wizard each Time</strong><br /><strong>Creating New Chart</strong> to false.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a StV-3 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24a1c224-731a-4774-a2f3-d3c6c6e0fc28"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank StV-3 diagram is created.</li></ol><h3>Adding capabilities to StV-3 chart</h3><p>To add a new capability</p><hr /><ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a new capability provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong>Capability Provision Creation Wizard</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select</strong> element dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Adding sub capabilities to StV-3 chart</h3><p>To add a new sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add existing Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ed3d8cc-a0d7-48ed-96a2-17b53ea0b06a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Removing capabilities and sub capabilities from StV-3 chart</h3><p>To remove a capability or sub capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Remove From Chart</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete a capability or sub capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability or sub capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55f7247d-0bb5-4fe1-a13a-2815a70f9122"><ac:rich-text-body>The capability name becomes <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing Gantt chart properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[highlighted]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To display deleted Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click<strong> Select All</strong> to display all removed Sub Capabilities.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Display all sub capabilities in StV-3 chart</h3><p>To display all sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the StV-3 chart toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Editing Capability Provision</h3><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capability Provision</h3><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731807 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04534: (2026x) Working with StV-5 table

- page_id: `272731807`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731807/2026x+Working+with+StV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:42.237+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > (2026x) MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413145832

413145834

413145831

**On this page**

413145833

##### Creating StV-5 table

The context of the StV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating StV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the StV-5 table is Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the StV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the StV-5 table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a StV-5 table

To create an empty StV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click an StV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from an StV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from an StV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in an StV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from an StV-5 table

Toremovethe deployment of the Actual Resources from an StV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the StV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

229836933

The deployment is not removed.</p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the StV-5 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with StV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f60e36a-1abb-4c92-820c-fd1f8175c4d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229836933</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 04535: (2026x) Working with StV-5 typical table

627968609

627968608

##### Creating StV-5 typical table

The context of the StV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with StV-5 table' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the StV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the StV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the StV-5 typical table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a StV-5 typical table

To create an empty StV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a StV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more StV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a StV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a StV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a StV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the StV-5 typical table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627973640

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736575 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04536: (2026x) Working with Summary and Overview diagram

- page_id: `272736575`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736575/2026x+Working+with+Summary+and+Overview+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:29.943+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) UAF 1.3 > (2026x) Summary and Overview
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Summary and Overview** diagram shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. It serves as a planning guide in the initial phases of architecture development. Upon completion of architecture, it summarizes findings and any conducted analysis.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Summary and Overview diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Summary and Overview view specification package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Summary and Overview . [ATTACHMENT filename='summary_and_overview_new.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Summary and Overview , and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Architectural Description element, but the same is valid for other available elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Architectural Description (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='summary_and_overview_new_element_palette.png']
2. Name the element.

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability Configuration (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have an Architectural Description(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the Architectural Description will be connected to another Architectural Description using the Architectural Reference relationship.

To connect the elements with the Architectural Reference relationship

1. From the diagram palette, choose Architectural Reference.
2. On the diagram pane, click the source Architectural Description element and do one of the following:
  - Click the target Architectural Description if it is already drawn on the diagram pane.
  - Click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a new Architectural Description element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The <strong>Summary and Overview</strong> diagram shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. It serves as a planning guide in the initial phases of architecture development. Upon completion of architecture, it summarizes findings and any conducted analysis.</span></p><h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create a Summary and Overview diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Summary and Overview view specification package and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Summary and Overview</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="350" ac:width="310"><ri:attachment ri:filename="summary_and_overview_new.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Summary and Overview diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Summary and Overview</strong>,<strong> </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. An example is described using the Architectural Description element, but the same is valid for other available elements.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>(or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="summary_and_overview_new_element_palette.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Summary and Overview diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Configuration</span> (or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have an <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span> will be connected to another <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span> using the Architectural Reference relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Architectural Reference relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>From the diagram palette, choose Architectural Reference.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">On the diagram pane, click the source Architectural Description element and do one of the following:</span></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click the target Architectural Description if it is already drawn on the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a new Architectural Description element.</span></p></li></ul></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="summary_and_overview_connect.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Summary and Overview diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739860 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04537: (2026x) Working with SvcV-5 matrix

- page_id: `272739860`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739860/2026x+Working+with+SvcV-5+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:06.495+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-5-matrix', 'build-svcv-5-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix describes the mapping betweenServices and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.

To create a SvcV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Services maps to Operational Activities using theSupportsrelationship.

To map a Service to an Operational Activity

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix describes the mapping <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">between </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Service</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SvcV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Services).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services maps to Operational Activities using the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Supports </span>relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a Service to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734667 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04538: (2026x) Working with Symbol styles

- page_id: `272734667`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734667/2026x+Working+with+Symbol+styles
- version_number: 2

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

601916684

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

601916687

INLINE

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Before you begin working on symbol styles in a Teamwork Cloud project, you need to lock the symbols styles for edit first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the**Project Options** dialog, click the **Symbol Styles** option group, to expand the tree hierarchy of all the styles defined within the project. You may use as many of these styles as you wish.

**Shape** and **Path** trees have the inner structure to help you find the model element for which you want to change the symbol style. The right side of the dialog contains possible choices and the instruments to manage them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Before changing symbol styles, we advise you to make a copy of the default style so you can come back to it if needed. You can make a copy by cloning the default style, which will create a new style. You can make changes in any selected style.

To create a new style by cloning the existing one

1. In the Symbol styles group, select Default style and click the Clone button.
2. Enter a name for the new style.
3. Change options of the new style.

As stated previously, you can create several symbol styles. You can set the desired style to be your default at any time.

To make a selected style your default style

- In the Symbol styles options group, select the style to make the default and click the Make Default button.

You can change Default symbol styles using the **Set Select Symbol Style as Default** button in the diagram toolbar. If you do not see this button, you need to change **Perspective** to the **Full feature** or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing Perspectives' space=''].

You can select to apply one of the predefined styles for the existing diagrams.

To apply the selected style

- In the Symbol styles group, select the style you want to apply and click the Apply button.

You can also apply the desired options to selected diagram model elements. Click the **Apply** button in the specific element's pane.

You can move your earlier defined symbol styles between other projects or users. Simply export the defined style and pass it on for other projects or users to import.

To save the created style (export)

1. In the Symbol styles group, click the Export button. The Save dialog opens.
2. Select the location where you wish to export a style.

To import a project style

1. In the Symbol styles group, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens.
2. Select the style you wish to import (*.stl).

601916682

INLINE

**Related pages**

- Symbol Properties dialog
- Symbol styles option group
- Copy and paste symbol style
- Making your own default style for new projects

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732899 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04539: (2026x) Working with symbols of model elements

- page_id: `272732899`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732899/2026x+Working+with+symbols+of+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:25.513+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['resize', 'resize-shape', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Use the Sticky button

569530408

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Use the Sticky button

569530410

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Use the Sticky button

569530409

700349

To draw an element on the diagram pane

1. Select an element button on the diagram palette.
2. Click anywhere on the diagram pane.

To draw several elements of the same type on the diagram pane, do one of the following

#### EXPAND: Use the Shift key

Use the Shift key

1. Press and hold Shift.
2. Click an element button on the diagram palette.
3. Click the diagram pane. With each click, the new element of the same type is drawn.

#### EXPAND: Use the Sticky button

Use the Sticky button

1. On the diagram palette, under the Tools category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='sticky_button.png'] Sticky button or press Z.
2. From the diagram palette, select an element button.
3. Click the diagram pane. With each click, the new element of the same type is drawn.

To draw a symbol of the existing element

1. Activate a diagram on which you wish to draw a symbol.
2. From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] , select an item you wish to draw.
3. Drag the selected model element onto the diagram pane.

To specify a name of the element, do one of the following

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] , Name property specification cell, type the element name.
- Type the symbol name directly on the selected symbol on the diagram pane.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] , select an element and name it.

To draw several symbols for one element, do one of the following

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] , select an element and drag it to the diagram pane. Repeat it if needed.
- On the diagram pane, select a symbol, copy and then paste by pressing Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V.

To resize selected shapes to a preferred size

• Click the shape and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the corners of the symbol and drag to the preferred size.

To change text and compartments vertical position on shapes

- In the Symbol Properties dialog > Text Vertical Position , select one of the following:
  - Top
  - Center
  - Bottom

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To delete selected model elements, do one of the following

- From the Edit menu, select Delete .
- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='delete.png'] Delete button.
- Press Ctrl+D.
- In the Activity and Sequence diagrams, press Delete.

The model elements will be removed from the model with all their data.

To delete selected symbols, do one of the following

- From the Edit menu, select Remove from Diagram .
- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_from_diagram.png'] Remove from Diagram button.
- Press Delete.

569530407

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']

## PAGE 04540: (2026x) Working with Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `272738696`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738696/2026x+Working+with+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:28.825+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Measurements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1776767993

1776768014

1776768003

To create a Typical Measurements table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Add New button and from the drop-down list select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Property Set elements for measurement tables' space=''] . The new row in the table is created. Type the name of the Property Set and specify the Measurements of the selected Property Set.
  - Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Property Set to the table.
2. Click the Evaluate Measurements button to create the Actual Measurements table automatically.

In the Typical Measurements table, you can select one of the Display Modes:

- Owned Measurements - collects only owned measurements.
- All Measurements - collects all measurements hierarchically with primitive types.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The created instances are stored in the same location as the Typical Measurements table.

1776767992

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='']

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

86435654565

INLINE

2142654765

INLINE

1232455425

INLINE

To work with a User Interface Modeling diagram

Do one of the following:

- On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button and select User Interface Modeling Diagram . Press Enter.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] , click either the Package or the Model. From their shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Other Diagrams > User Interface Modeling Diagram .

After loading a project and creating a diagram, you can add UI modeling elements to the diagram pane.

To add new elements to the User Interface Modeling diagram pane

- Simply drag and drop the elements from the Text, Containers, Button, or Other toolbar.

To load a sample with an already created User Interface model

1. Open either the UI Modeling Samples.mdzip or UI Modeling in System Development.mdzip projects, found in <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\User Interface Modeling .
2. After loading the sample, select the diagrams from the Index or Overview d iagrams by clicking the hyperlinks.

605235642

INLINE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4279ced8-9e22-4ebe-b4c9-2cea99e4760b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">86435654565</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07b5830c-c028-4ee5-a711-407642dd91e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2142654765</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16c183ab-1d84-49a2-8ceb-a295c12b05da"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1232455425</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To work with a User Interface Modeling diagram</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button and select <strong>User Interface Modeling Diagram</strong>. Press Enter.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Model Browser" /></ac:link>, click either the Package or the Model. From their shortcut menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Other Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>User Interface Modeling Diagram</strong>.</li></ul><p>After loading a project and creating a diagram, you can add UI modeling elements to the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add new elements to the User Interface Modeling diagram pane</p><hr /><ul><li>Simply drag and drop the elements from the Text, Containers, Button, or Other toolbar.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To load a sample with an already created User Interface model</p><hr /><ol><li>Open either the <em>UI Modeling Samples.mdzip</em> or <em>UI Modeling in System Development.mdzip</em> projects, found in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\User Interface Modeling</em>.</li><li>After loading the sample, select the diagrams from the <em>Index </em>or <em>Overview d</em>iagrams by clicking the hyperlinks.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f0418b2-3050-474b-90bb-d8135f4bdcf4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">605235642</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UML diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extension diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extension diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737419 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04542: (2026x) Working with Web Publisher 2.0

- page_id: `272737419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737419/2026x+Working+with+Web+Publisher+2.0
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:42.424+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Generating Reports from Report Wizard > (2026x) Web Publisher 2.0 report
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1336380615

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1336380618

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1336380618

The Web Publisher 2.0 report template comes with predefined variables and their values. Before generating a report based on the Web Publisher 2.0 template, you can add more variables to it, edit the existing ones, or delete those you do not want to include in your report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report variables of Web Publisher 2.0.

The following table shows the variable names and values of the Web Publisher 2.0 template.

| Variable | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Author | The report's creator. |
| Title | The report's title. |
| HomeImage | Possible values:Leave it empty or unspecified to use the MagicDraw icon as the homepage image.Enter a diagram name (plain text) to display the selected diagram as the homepage image, for example, Software Development Process.Enter “mdel://” followed by a model element ID to display the element as the homepage image, for example, “mdel://_10_0EAPbeta2_8740266_1126593738250_35764_172”.Enter the absolute path of an image file on your computer to display the image as the homepage image, for example, file://d://picture//image.jpg.Enter the relative path of an image file on your computer to display the image as the homepage image. The relative path starts at the location of the Web Publisher 2.0 template: %APPDATA%\Local\.magicdraw\2021x\data\reports\Web Publisher 2.0\web (%APPDATA% is the location where the user's data are stored, for example, /images/homeimage.jpg).Enter the location of an image on the Web to display the image as the homepage image, for example, http://www.photobucket.com/image.jpg. |
| Diagram as the homepage image. |  |
| UseContextMenu | True: Shows a context menu when right-clicking a diagram element.False: Shows an element specification or opens an element, a diagram, or a page specified in any existing active hyperlink when right-clicking a diagram element. |
| SelectHistoryNode | True: Selects a node when you move backwards or forwards.False: Types "false" if you want it another way. |
| ExportLinkedFile | True: Copies a linked file from a model hyperlink into an output report folder.False: Keeps the link to an absolute path only. |
| ShowAppearsInPage | True: Shows data in a report, and opens the Appears in tab when clicking an element in the Containment tree or a diagram pane. False: Hides the Appears in tab in a generated report. |
| ShowAppliedStereotypes | True: Shows applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree.False: Does not show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. |
| AutoNavigateHyperlink | True: Automatically navigates the active hyperlink of each element. For example, if A has an active hyperlink to B and B has an active hyperlink to C, then C will be shown when you click A.False: The behavior of navigation to active hyperlink is the same as that of MagicDraw. For example, if A has an active hyperlink to B and B has an active hyperlink to C, then B will be shown when you click A. |
| DisplayTreeElementId | True: Shows the property ID of an element in the Containment tree.False: Types "false" if you want it another way. |
| SVGDiagramResizable | True: Resizes SVG images when the browser is minimized.False: Enables SVG image linkable capability, but the resizable capability would be disabled.If the "SVGDiagramResizable" is set to True, the SVG image would be resizable on web browser, but it would not be linkable. |
| SkipElementContext | True: Skips the iteration of all elements to create velocity context classified by customized stereotypes, e.g., $CustomizedStereotypeName.False: Does not skip the iteration of all elements to create velocity context classified by customized stereotypes. |
| GotoLinkByIcon | True: Both the icon and the label of the element in the Containment tree navigate to an active hyperlink.False: The icon element navigates to the element's Specification tab even if there is an active hyperlink. |
| ExportedImageSize | The percentage (from 10 to 1000) of an exported image size. |

WorkingwithWP

The following section contains the instructions on how to work with your Web Publisher report.

To generate a Web Publisher report

1. Open the Magic Library.mdzip sample project from the <modeling_tool_home>/samples/case studies directory.
2. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**. The**Report Wizard** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the Select Template pane, select **Default Template** > **Web Publisher 2.0**, and click**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.
4. You can either:
  - select the built-in report data or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new one. Type the new report name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the Select Report Data pane, you can create a new set of Report Data for the Web Publisher template. The Report Data is a container for a set of custom-defined fields in the template. It can be used to group different report versions.To edit or create a variable in the report data by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''], edit or delete information for predefined custom fields or create a new one, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] .
6. Select the element(s) from the model that you want to include in the report data, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Add_button.png'] . Select the scope of the report in the open package tree. Select the Model package if you want to have a web-based report of your entire project. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] .
8. Click the[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to locate the report file location.
9. Select the file location, type the report name, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. A generated web report will include a number of folders and files.
10. Select the report image format: **.png*, **.jpg* or**.svg*.
11. Select an option to display empty value information, either **Empty text** or **Custom text**. In some cases, the query may return an empty value that creates blank fields in the report. The **Display empty value as** option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.
12. Select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box to open the report document with the default editor.
13. After all options have been selected, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

To hide or show the Containment menu

- Click the CONTAINMENT menu to hide it, and click it again to show it.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Hiding the Containment Menu.

To expand or collapse the Containment tree

- Click the “ + ” button in the Containment tree to expand it, or click the “ - ” button to collapse it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The button to expand or collapse the Containment tree.

To reduce or increase the Containment tree width

- Drag the separator line between the containment and the content panel to reduce or increase the width of the Containment tree. The**Web Publisher 2.0**report interface uses ahorizontalscrollbar, which appears when the length of an element's name exceeds the current width of the Containment tree.The default width of the containment tree is**20%**of the**web browser**width.

To show or hide element contents

- Click and re-click the arrow button to show and hide the contents. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Showing and hiding element contents.

To show an element specification, active hyperlink, Submachine of state, or behavior of the Call Behavior action

- Click a diagram's element to show the shorcut menu for opening its specification, Active Hyperlink, Hyperlink, submachine of state, or behavior of the call behavior action. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Web Publisher 2.0 context menu.

To add an Active Hyperlink to a model

You can [add an active hyperlink to any symbol on a diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Defining+hyperlinks). There are three kinds of hyperlinks: Element, File, and URL. However, the Element type hyperlink is available for the Active Hyperlink column on the [Generic Table diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Working+with+Web+Publisher+2.0#WorkingwithWebPublisher2.0-generictable) image only.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The Web Publisher 2.0 report does not display the Generic Table diagram containing 10,000 elements or more due to the excessive size of the diagram image, making it unable to be rendered and empty.

You can navigate to the target if you double-click the hyperlink**on the diagram in the Web Publisher report. The following figure below shows the Submachine State hyperlink in the Web Publisher report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Submachine State hyperlink in the Web Publisher report.

If you double-click the Submachine State in the above example, it navigates to the target and opens it for you as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The hyperlink navigates to the target in the Web Publisher report.

Elements in the **Active Hyperlink** column in the **Generic Table** diagram link to the associate elements. If you click an element in this column, you will be navigated to the Specification page of the associated element.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only element type is available. File and URL address cannot navigate to the associated artifacts.

generictable

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Elements in the Active Hyperlink column in the Generic Table diagram links to the associated elements in the Specification page.

To display a property ID in the Containment tree

1. Click Tools > Report Wizard > Variable to open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Select the DisplayTreeElementId variable and enter the value true .
3. Click**OK**to show the requirement property IDs in the **Containment** tree. [IMAGE alt='' src='']The requirement property IDs also appear in the **Containment** tree of Web Publisher. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To go to the index page

- Click the Web Publisher 2.0 at the top-left corner of the screen to go to the index page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Shortcut to the index page.

To see the description of an element

- On the Specification tab, move your mouse over an element to see the description in a tooltip. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Showing an element description.

To show property visibility of your report

- In the **Specification** tab, select **Standard**, **Expert**, or **All** from the **Mode** drop-down menu. The mode appears in the property visibility depends upon the mode that you have selected in the modeling tool. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property visibility mode types.

To open an Activity, a State Machine, a Collaboration, or an Interaction sub-diagram

- Click an Activity, State Machine, Collaboration, or Interaction diagram to open a sub-diagram associated with an element. [ATTACHMENT filename='Clicking a Diagram Name to Open Its Subdiagrams.png']

###### Clicking a diagram name to open its subdiagrams.

To open the sub-diagrams of a state with Submachine

1. Double-click either a state with a Submachine or a call behavior action of which behavior is specified. For example, double-clicking the last Submachine State *Request Access : Connection* in the following figure.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
When you double-click it, the associated diagram opens (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src=''] State machine diagram viewed in web publisher interface.
2. Click the “**+**” button in the Containment tree to expand it, or click the “**-**” button to collapse it. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The button to expand or collapse the Containment tree.

1336380612

**Related page**

- 

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
