<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564872 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04575: Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard

- page_id: `249564872`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564872/Actual+Project+Milestone+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:22.828+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['actual-project-milestone-creation-wizard', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.

To open the **Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard**

1. Open an AcV-2 (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart' space=''] (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] (DoDAF 2.0), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects Roadmap' space=''] (UAF).
2. Select a project.
3. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
4. Select one of the available milestone kinds: The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other

The wizard consists of five steps:

truenone

##### Actual Project Milestone name, kind, and date specification

In this step, you can specify the name of a new Actual Project Milestone you are creating.

You can select one of the Actual Project Milestone kinds, which are:

- In Service
- Deployed
- No Longer Used
- Out Of Service
- Other

Also, you can set the Actual Project Milestone date and time that indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information about the date and time, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Actual Project Milestone type specification

You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:

- DLOD Milestone
- DOTMLPF Milestone

You can create your own milestone type as well using the **Creation Mode** and selecting the **Create** button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Version Released specification

You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Released property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Version Withdrawn specification

You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Withdrawn property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Project status specification at a particular Actual Project Milestone

After creating a new Actual Project Milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.

|  | DLOD Milestone type | DOTMLPF Milestone type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Project threads | EquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTraining | DoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilities |
| Status indicators | DLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical Issues | Materiel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & Support |

You can also define your own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.

To create a project thread and status indicator

1. In the Project Thread area, click the Add button.
2. Specify the thread name.
3. In the Status Indicator area, click the Add button.
4. Specify the indicator name.
5. Click the Fill Color button.
6. In the Color dialog select the color and click OK . The project thread and status indicator are created.

To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread select the **Creation Mode,** and click the **Clone** button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.</p><p>To open the <strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open an AcV-2 (MODAF),<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Lr Lines of Development]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="PV-2 Project Timelines" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF 2.0), or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Projects Roadmap" /></ac:link> (UAF).</li><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The wizard consists of five steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97f66a82-51e3-46dd-aea3-309376400102"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Actual Project Milestone name, kind, and date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the name of a new Actual Project Milestone you are creating.</p><p>You can select one of the Actual Project Milestone kinds, which are:</p><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul><p>Also, you can set the Actual Project Milestone date and time that indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information about the date and time, see<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_I.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Actual Project Milestone type specification</h3><p>You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:</p><ul><li>DLOD Milestone</li><li>DOTMLPF Milestone</li></ul><p>You can create your own milestone type as well using the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> and selecting the <strong>Create</strong> button.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_II.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Version Released specification</h3><p>You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Released property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_III.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Version Withdrawn specification</h3><p>You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Withdrawn property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_IV.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Project status specification at a particular Actual Project Milestone</h3><p>After creating a new Actual Project Milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>DLOD Milestone type</th><th>DOTMLPF Milestone type</th></tr><tr><td>Project threads</td><td><ul><li>Equipment</li><li>Logistics</li><li>Infrastructure</li><li>Organization</li><li>Doctrine/Concepts</li><li>Information</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Training</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Doctrine</li><li>Organization</li><li>Training</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Leadership</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Facilities</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Status indicators</td><td><ul><li>DLOD Absent</li><li>Not Required</li><li>No Outstanding Issues</li><li>Manageable Issues</li><li>Critical Issues</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Materiel Solution Analysis</li><li>Technology Development</li><li>Engineering and Manufacturing Development</li><li>Production &amp; Deployment</li><li>Operations &amp; Support</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p>You can also define your own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.</p><p>To create a project thread and status indicator</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Project Thread area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Specify the thread name.</li><li>In the Status Indicator area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Specify the indicator name.</li><li>Click the <strong>Fill Color</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Color</strong> dialog select the color and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The project thread and status indicator are created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e561acb7-083d-467f-9335-74eb922f2c5a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread select the <strong>Creation Mode,</strong> and click the <strong>Clone</strong> button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_V.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

## PAGE 04576: Actual Resources

832335041

832335043

832335042

The Actual Resources (Ar) domain shows the analysis.e.g. evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. It illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations and actual relationships between them.

832335040

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564779 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04577: Actual Resources Connectivity

- page_id: `249564779`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564779/Actual+Resources+Connectivity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:19.673+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Actual Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832380838

832380840

832380839

**Description**

An Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain shows the communication of actual resource. It illustrates the actual resource configurations and actual relationships between them.

An Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency) shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time.

The intended usage of the actual form of the Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain includes:

- 
  - Identify architecture stakeholders.
  - Identify process owners.
  - Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Resources Connectivity diagram' space=''].

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

832380836

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organizational Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Person' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resource Relationship' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Responsibility' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Responsible Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fielded Capability' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564879 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04578: Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard

- page_id: `249564879`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564879/Actual+Resources+Deployment+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:23.276+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment', 'wizard', 'actual-resources', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This wizard is used to create the following tables:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping' space=''] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space=''] (MODAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying NCV-5 table' space=''] (NAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space=''] (UAF)

To open the **Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard**

- Do one of the following :
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram, and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard opens.

If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create <diagram name>** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show St-Rm Actual Deployment/CV-5/StV-5/NCV-5 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

The wizard consists of six steps:

truenone

##### Specifying the name and owning package

In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting Actual Enterprise Phase

In this step, select the existing or create a new Actual Enterprise Phase which will be represented as a key element in the table.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the **Type** radio button and choose Enterprise Phase, the Actual Enterprise Phase is created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase

1. In the Select Actual Enterprise Phase step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Enterprise Phase, type its name.

##### Specifying start date

In this step, specify the start date for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.

When you select the Actual Enterprise Phase with the defined Start and End dates, those dates are selected in the calendar. Although, the Actual Enterprise Phase might not have any dates defined, or defined only the Start date. If you specified the End date for the Actual Enterprise Phase, you need to define the Start date also.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specifying end date

In this step, specify the end date for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.

If the Actual Enterprise Phase has the End date defined, this date is selected in the calendar. The End date cannot be earlier than the Start date.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting Capabilities

In this step, select existing or create new Capabilities which will be represented as columns in the table.

Once Capabilities are added, the Phases relationships between Actual Enterprise Phases and Capabilities are created.

If the Actual Enterprise Phases already have Phases relationships with Capabilities, they will be displayed in the **Selected Elements** section.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Capability

1. In the Select Capabilities step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a Capability.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Capability, type its name.

##### Selecting Actual Responsible Resources

In this step, select existing or create new Actual Responsible Resources which will be represented as rows of the table.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the **Type** radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and added in the **Selected Elements** section (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Responsible Resource

1. In the Select Actual Responsible Resources step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create an Actual Responsible Resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. Select the needed Actual Responsible Resource.
5. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Responsible Resource, type its name.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This wizard is used to create the following tables:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF)</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with StV-5 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (MODAF)</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying NCV-5 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NCV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF)</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Actual Deployment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (UAF)</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ec1910d5-cca1-4817-94d2-fb944feb98c1">following</ac:inline-comment-marker>:<ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4e53e16e-6d15-45cd-832f-1c57571b2a2a">Press Ctrl+N.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the <strong>&lt;view name&gt;</strong> Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>&lt;diagram name&gt;</strong>.</li><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Create Diagram,</strong> and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram.<br />The <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Creation </strong><strong>Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e743b31-08c5-4ba8-8111-15b730e83500"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create &lt;diagram name&gt;</strong> check box.</p><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> Diagram Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e4862187-0820-4aec-9a6f-164717a11b3e">St-Rm Actual Deployment/CV-5/StV-5</ac:inline-comment-marker>/NCV-5 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The wizard consists of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="af367bc8-7c1a-4f3b-be49-572a383e48c3">six</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fd05aeb8-2c5d-41fb-9e05-aea73f603dd0">steps</ac:inline-comment-marker>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d5e6e6e-d138-4abd-91b1-dcfbcbb88775"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Specifying the name and owning package</h3><p>In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_actual_resource_deployment_creation_wizard_1.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Selecting Actual Enterprise Phase</h3><p>I<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3cc55805-b5ff-4163-a650-fb346b29f95d">n this step, select the e</ac:inline-comment-marker>xisting or create a new Actual Enterprise Phase which will be represented as a key element in the table.</p><p>You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the <strong>Type</strong> radio button and choose Enterprise Phase, the Actual Enterprise Phase is created automatically.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_actual_resource_deployment_creation_wizard_2.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Enterprise Phase</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Enterprise Phase </strong>step, click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Enterprise Phase, type its name.</li></ol><h3>Specifying start date</h3><p>In this step, specify the start date <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8a188576-856d-4f06-809f-c84080d9bc19">for</ac:inline-comment-marker> the s<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e65ec6aa-8dcf-4c1d-a279-137f717eb72c">elected Actual Enterprise</ac:inline-comment-marker> Phase.</p><p>When you select the Actual Enterprise Phase with the defined Start and End dates, those dates are selected in the calendar. Although, the Actual Enterprise Phase might not have any dates defined, or defined only the Start date. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f494d71-6f48-4c1d-8945-107de59e3a2e">If you specified the End date for the Actual Enterprise Phase, you need to define the Start date also.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_actual_resource_deployment_creation_wizard_3.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Specifying end date</h3><p>In this step, specify the end date for the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c21bb758-46f8-4eb6-bb23-2db6c6021f12">selected</ac:inline-comment-marker> Actual Enterprise Phase.</p><p>If the Actual Enterprise Phase has the End date defined, this date is selected in the calendar. The End date cannot be earlier than the Start date.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_actual_resource_deployment_creation_wizard_4.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Selecting Capabilities</h3><p>In this step, select existing or create new Capabilities which will be represented as columns in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ae2ab780-fb2f-46f9-b5d2-0811a7108143">table</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p>Once Capabilities are added, the Phases <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ad41a05d-53c6-4777-9920-63b8b69c3672">relationships</ac:inline-comment-marker> between Actual Enterprise Phases and Capabilities are <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="155a6df6-b556-4332-a8a7-4093bc5458d6">created</ac:inline-comment-marker>. </p><p>If the Actual Enterprise Phases already have Phases relationships with Capabilities, they will be displayed in the <strong>Selected Elements</strong> section. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_actual_resource_deployment_creation_wizard_5.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Capabilities </strong>step, click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a Capability.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created Capability, type its name.</li></ol><h3>Selecting Actual Responsible Resources</h3><p>In this step, select existing or create <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2edda4df-6848-46dc-b358-a3c8320f310a">new</ac:inline-comment-marker> Actual Responsible Resources which will be represented as rows of the table.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the <strong>Type</strong> radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and added in the <strong>Selected Elements</strong> section (see the following figure).</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_actual_resource_deployment_creation_wizard_6.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Responsible Resource</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Responsible Resources </strong>step, click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create an Actual Responsible Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>Select the needed Actual Responsible Resource.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Responsible Resource, type its name.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564887 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04579: Actual Resources Deployment Wizard

- page_id: `249564887`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564887/Actual+Resources+Deployment+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:23.737+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment-wizard', 'actual-resources-deployment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** is to deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.

To open the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space=''] (DoDAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space=''] (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space=''] (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space=''] (UAF) table.
2. Double-click a cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Select Actual Resources

In this step, select the Actual Resourcesrequired by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing ActualResources or create new ones.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the**Type**radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and displayed in blue in the**Selected Elements**section (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Deployment date specification

In this step, you can specify the Deployment date and time for the selected Actual Resource configuration to be deployed and used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default, the Today date is selected.

If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase) and the date selection steps are not available.

The Deployment date for the Actual Resource usage can not be later than the No Longer Used date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard only if the start date is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### No longer used date specification

In this step, you can specify the No Longer Used date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be no longer used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.

The No longer Used date for the Actual Resourceusage can not be earlier than the Deployment date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard if the end date is not specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Elements that can beActual Resources are listed in the following table.

| Domain | Actual Resources | Types of Actual Resources |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Security | Actual ResourceActual Resource | Security EnclaveResource Mitigation |
| Resources | Actual ResourceActual ResourceActual ResourceActual ResourceFielded CapabilityActual ResourceActual Resource | Natural ResourceTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactCapability ConfigurationSystemResource Architecture |
| Projects | Actual Project | Project |
| Personnel | Actual ResponsibilityActual PersonActual PostActual Organization | ResponsibilityPersonPostOrganization |
| Operational | Actual Resource | Known Resource |
| Actual Resources | Actual PostActual PersonActual OrganizationActual ResponsibilityFielded CapabilityActual Resource | Actual PostActual PersonActual OrganizationActual ResponsibilityFielded CapabilityActual Resource |

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

760209065

760209076

760209075

**Description**

The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space=''].
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space=''] .
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564786 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04581: Actual Resources Structure

- page_id: `249564786`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564786/Actual+Resources+Structure
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:20.605+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Actual Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832612334

832612336

832612335

**Description**

An Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain describes the analysis, e.g. evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations as it provides a means to capture different solution architectures. The detailed analysis (trade-off, what-if etc.) is carried out using the Resource Constraints view. It illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations required to meet an operational need.

An Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain is divided in two forms:

- Typical (e.g., a typical brigade command structure). It shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. The intended usage of the typical form of the Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) view includes:
  - Organizational analysis.
  - Definition of human roles.
  - Operational analysis.
- Actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency). It shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time. The intended usage of the actual form of the Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain includes:
  - Identify architecture stakeholders.
  - Identify process owners.
  - Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Resources Structure diagram' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Resources Structure diagram

832612333

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Person' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Responsibility' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Responsible Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Competence' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fills Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organizational Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Person' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Responsible For' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='']

## PAGE 04582: Actual Resources Taxonomy

- page_id: `249564791`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564791/Actual+Resources+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:26.062+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Actual Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1476440482

1476440483

1476440483

**Description**

The Actual Resources Taxonomy (Ar-Tx) domain illustrates the actual organizational structure and relationships among actual organizations, actual posts, and actual persons filling those actual post, that are the key players in the architecture.

This domain describes the hierarchy of control within actual organizations, between actual posts and actual persons filling those actual posts that affect the architecture and how it is used. It is the instance version of the personnel structure which defines the types of organizations and post, etc.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Taconomy (Ar-Tx) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix' space=''].

1476440482

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564892 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04583: Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `249564892`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564892/Actual+Strategic+Phase+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:24.227+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['assign-actual-person', 'actual-post', 'personnel-availability', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

276394815

INLINE

276394817

INLINE

276394816

INLINE

The **Actual Strategic Phase Wizard** is used to assign an Actual Strategic Phase.

To open the **Actual Strategic Phase****Assignment Wizard**

1. Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space=''] .
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the chart toolbar, click the Add New button and choose Add New Enterprise Mission , Add New Actual Enduring Task , Add New Whole Life Enterprise , Add New Actual Enterprise Phase , or Add New Value Stream .
  - In the chart toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
  - In the chart, right-click a selected Actual Strategic Phase and choose Add New Sub Actual Strategic Phase or Add Existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase .
  - The Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### **Strategic Phase**selection

In this step, you can select a Strategic Phase in three available ways:

- Select an existing Strategic Phase as a type from the suggested list .
- Create a new Strategic Phase as a type by clicking the Creation Mode button.
- Create an instance without a type, choose UNSPECIFIED.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Select Strategic Phase dialog, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a Strategic Phase.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Strategic Phase, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the Actual Strategic Phase starts.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the Actual Strategic Phase ends.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1587085761

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973771 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04585: Adding a Macro and editing Macro information

- page_id: `243973771`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973771/Adding+a+Macro+and+editing+Macro+information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T19:18:21.343+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

851898320

851898333

851898317

**On this page:**

851898322

You can add a new macro and enter all necessary information about it by following the steps described in****Opening Macro Information Dialog and**** Adding a Macro and Its Information below.

##### Opening Macro Information Dialog

You can add or modify macro information such as the macro name and description, in the **Macro Information** dialog. To open the **Macro Information** dialog, you need to open the **Organize Macros** dialog first.

To open the **Organize Macros** dialog

- Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros on the main menu. The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figures below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Macro Information** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros on the main menu to open the Organize Macros dialog, see above figure.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Macro Information dialog will open.

##### Adding a Macro and Its Information

Use the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** or **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** button in the **Organize Macros** dialog to add or edit a macro and its information in the **Macro Information** dialog. You can also press the mnemonic keys to add or edit a macro, see****Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys.

To add a macro and enter macro information in the **Macro Information** dialog

1. Open the Organize Macros dialog (click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros on the main menu).
2. Click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. The **Macro Information** dialog will open, see figure below. The **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**, **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**, **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**, and **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** buttons in the **Organize Macros** dialog will be disabled if there is no macro in the macro table or if you do not select any macro from the table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Type the macro name in the Name box.
4. The default macro language you have previously selected (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting a default macro language' space=''] ) will appear in the Macro Language box.
5. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='button-1.png'] button, to locate a macro file. The Open file dialog will open, see figure below.
6. Select the file and its type (there are 5 types of file filter: *.bsh , *.groovy , *.rb , *.js , or *.py ), see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='open-dialog.png']
7. Click Open, see figure above. The selected pathname will appear in the File box in the Macro Information dialog, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='fig 8.jpg'] [ATTACHMENT filename='macro-information.png']
8. Select either (i) the **Use path variables** check box or (ii) the **Add macro to model** check box. If you have specified the file or network path in the **Environment Options** dialog by clicking **Options** > **Environment** > **Path** **Variables** and selected the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box in **Macro Information** dialog, the <Path Variable name> will show in front of the file pathname. This field is the [Required] field, for example, *<mypath>/<macro_name>.js.*If you select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box, the full pathname will not be saved.If you select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box, your source code will be imported from the file to the model. The location to keep the model is Data::MacroEngine.You can open the **Macro Information** by using Ctrl+Alt+m as a shortcut key.
9. Type the macro description in the **Description** box, see figure above. A macro name must be unique and cannot be duplicated.
10. The function of the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box is to allow you to run the default values automatically. If you select the check box, the system will not open a dialog to prompt you to input the value. If you select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box, you need to enter the valid default value of each parameter.
11. Click **Add** to specify the arguments of the macro. The arguments specified in the **Arguments** table will be the global variables of a specific macro. A parameter type can be a String, Integer, Long, Double, Date, or ElementPath.If the **Null** check box is selected, you cannot enter the default value of that particular parameter.An empty value in the **Default Value** column does not necessarily mean a null value, for example an empty string value is an empty string.If you input an invalid default value or you do not enter the argument name, the system will display the following error message when you run the script: **The following argument(s) are invalid: <List of the invalid argument>**.
  - Name column contains the name of a parameter
  - Type column contains the type of a parameter
  - Array check box is to identify if an array is the parameter
  - Null check box is to identify if null is the parameter value
  - Default Value column contains an initial value to run the macro
12. Type a keyboard shortcut that will be used to run the macro in the [ATTACHMENT filename='press-new-shortcut-key.png'] box and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The newly assigned keyboard shortcut will appear in the Current keys box.
13. Click **OK** . The **Organize Macros** dialog will open, showing the newly added macro name, description, and keyboard shortcut. Macro names, filenames, and languages are required.Macro description and keyboard shortcuts are optional.If any of the required fields are not entered or duplicate macro name is entered, the following message will open: **The following field(s) are invalid: <List of the problems>**.If the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box is selected and at least one value is empty or not valid, then the following error message will open: **The following value(s) are invalid. <List of the invalid value>**If the****[IMAGE alt='' src='']**** check box is selected, all variables must have valid values or are set to null.
14. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to close the Organize Macros dialog.

##### Editing Macro Information

You can see macro information such as names and descriptions, as well as the macro keyboard shortcuts in the **Organize Macros** dialog.

To edit macro information

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros . The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. Select a macro from the table and either click **Edit** or press **Alt + E**. The **Macro Information** dialog will open.

3. Edit the macro information.

4. Click **OK** to save it. You will see the altered information in the **Organize Macros** dialog.

5. Click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

##### Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys

Macro Engine provides mnemonic keys for you to perform some operations, for example, highlight a textbox and click a specific button in the **Macro Information** dialog. The table below lists the**Macro Information** dialog mnemonic keys and their function. Below is a table listing of Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys.

| Mnemonic keys | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + N | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + M | To highlight the box. |
| Alt + F | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + . | To open the File dialog. |
| Alt + D | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + A | To add an argument in the Arguments box. |
| Alt + l | To delete an argument in the Arguments area. |
| Alt + U | To highlight the Current keys box. |
| Alt + G | To click the button. |
| Alt + R | To click the button. |
| Alt + E | To click the button. |
| Alt + P | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + O | To click the button. |
| Alt + C | To click the button. |
| Alt + H | To click the button. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd962017-cb63-46fc-bb5f-e354bf29d296"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851898320</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c05a776-977e-4402-8169-0ec946bb9520"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851898333</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="333acb62-37f2-4613-aff0-e3140e410420"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851898317</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39c3e0c3-099e-4bd5-98d1-e3a99f31e009" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99159ac6-757b-47fb-a4b0-6897c2eff916"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">851898322</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add a new macro and enter all necessary information about it by following the steps described in<strong> </strong>Opening Macro Information Dialog and<strong> </strong> <span class="confluence-link">Adding a Macro and Its Information</span> below.</p><h3>Opening Macro Information Dialog</h3><p>You can add or modify macro information such as the macro name and description, in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog. To open the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog, you need to open the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog first.</p><p>To open the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Organize Macros</strong> on the main menu. The <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog will open, see figures below.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Organize Macros Menu" ac:alt="Organize Macros Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fig 4.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Organize macros dialog" ac:alt="Organize macros dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="organize-macros.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Organize Macros</strong> on the main menu to open the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog, see above figure.</li><li>Click <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong>. The <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog will open.</li></ol><h3>Adding a Macro and Its Information</h3><p>Use the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong> or <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong> button in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog to add or edit a macro and its information in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog. You can also press the mnemonic keys to add or edit a macro, see<strong>  </strong>Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a macro and enter macro information in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog (click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Organize Macros</strong>  on the main menu).</li><li><p>Click <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong>. The <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog will open, see figure below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91153c0f-f327-447c-a56b-2286fb4be311"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong>, <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong>, <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit-code.png" /></ac:image> </strong>, and <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="run-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong> buttons in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog will be disabled if there is no macro in the macro table or if you do not select any macro from the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Macro Information Dialog" ac:alt="The Macro Information Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add-macro.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Type the macro name in the <strong>Name</strong> box.</li><li>The default macro language you have previously selected (see<strong> </strong> <em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting a default macro language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Selecting a Default Macro Language]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </em>) will appear in the <strong>Macro Language</strong> box.</li><li>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png" /></ac:image>button, to locate a macro file. The <strong>Open file</strong> dialog will open, see figure below.</li><li>Select the file and its type (there are 5 types of file filter: <em>*.bsh</em>, <em>*.groovy</em>, <em>*.rb</em>, <em>*.js</em>, or <em>*.py</em>), see figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Use Path Variables Checkbox" ac:alt="Use Path Variables Checkbox"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open-dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Open, </strong>see figure above. The selected pathname will appear in the <strong>File</strong> box in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog, see figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Types of File Filter" ac:alt="Types of File Filter"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fig 8.jpg" /></ac:image> <br /><span> <br /></span> <br /><ac:image ac:title="Macro Information Dialog" ac:alt="Macro Information Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="macro-information.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p><span>Select either (i) the </span> <strong>Use path variables</strong> <span> check box or (ii) the </span> <strong>Add macro to model</strong> <span> check box.</span> <span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5416dd1-742d-4ab3-b82c-babe0c89e2ad"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you have specified the file or network path in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog by clicking <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>Path</strong> <strong>Variables</strong> and selected the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use-path-variables.png" /></ac:image> </strong> check box in <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog, the &lt;Path Variable name&gt; will show in front of the file pathname. This field is the [Required] field, for example, <em>&lt;mypath&gt;/&lt;macro_name&gt;.js.</em></li><li>If you select the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use-path-variables.png" /></ac:image> </strong> check box, the full pathname will not be saved.</li><li>If you select the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add-macro-to-model.png" /></ac:image> </strong> check box, your source code will be imported from the file to the model. The location to keep the model is Data::MacroEngine.</li><li>You can open the <strong>Macro Information</strong> by using Ctrl+Alt+m as a shortcut key.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span>Type the macro description in the </span> <strong>Description</strong> <span> box, see figure above.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86396671-f89d-4b01-b34b-49dc07693d2d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A macro name must be unique and cannot be duplicated.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span>The function of the </span> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png" /></ac:image> </strong> <span> check box is to allow you to run the default values automatically. If you select the check box, the system will not open a dialog to prompt you to input the value.</span> <br /><span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dddf5cb2-6c91-46b2-a606-b7bf9a4c85f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you select the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png" /></ac:image> </strong> check box, you need to enter the valid default value of each parameter.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span>Click </span> <strong>Add</strong> <span> to specify the arguments of the macro. The arguments specified in the </span> <strong>Arguments</strong> <span> table will be the global variables of a specific macro.</span></p><ul><li><strong>Name</strong> column contains the name of a parameter</li><li><strong>Type</strong> column contains the type of a parameter</li><li><strong>Array</strong> check box is to identify if an array is the parameter</li><li><strong>Null</strong> check box is to identify if null is the parameter value</li><li><strong>Default Value</strong> column contains an initial value to run the macro</li></ul><p><span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cf0edb8-a556-4ad8-bb80-2c0f70a2dc3a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>A parameter type can be a String, Integer, Long, Double, Date, or ElementPath.</li><li>If the <strong>Null</strong> check box is selected, you cannot enter the default value of that particular parameter.</li><li>An empty value in the <strong>Default Value</strong> column does not necessarily mean a null value, for example an empty string value is an empty string.</li><li>If you input an invalid default value or you do not enter the argument name, the system will display the following error message when you run the script: <strong>The following argument(s) are invalid: &lt;List of the invalid argument&gt;</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>Type a keyboard shortcut that will be used to run the macro in the</span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png" /></ac:image> <span> box and click </span> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong> <span>. The newly assigned keyboard shortcut will appear in the Current keys box.</span></li><li><p><span>Click </span> <strong>OK</strong> <span>. The </span> <strong>Organize Macros</strong> <span> dialog will open, showing the newly added macro name, description, and keyboard shortcut.</span> <span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38da4285-f9d0-4826-8812-5566b56beb29"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Macro names, filenames, and languages are required.</li><li>Macro description and keyboard shortcuts are optional.</li><li>If any of the required fields are not entered or duplicate macro name is entered, the following message will open: <strong>The following field(s) are invalid: &lt;List of the problems&gt;</strong>.</li><li>If the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png" /></ac:image> </strong> check box is selected and at least one value is empty or not valid, then the following error message will open: <strong>The following value(s) are invalid. &lt;List of the invalid value&gt;</strong></li><li>If the<strong> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png" /></ac:image> </strong> </strong> check box is selected, all variables must have valid values or are set to null.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>Click </span> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="close-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong> <span> to close the </span> <strong>Organize Macros</strong> <span> dialog.</span></li></ol><h3>Editing Macro Information</h3><p>You can see macro information such as names and descriptions, as well as the macro keyboard shortcuts in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit macro information</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Organize Macros</strong>. The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figure below.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Editing a Macro" ac:alt="Editing a Macro"><ri:attachment ri:filename="organize-macros.png" /></ac:image></p><p>      2.  Select a macro from the table and either click <strong>Edit</strong> or press <strong>Alt + E</strong>. The <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog will open.</p><p>      3.  Edit the macro information.</p><p>      4.  Click <strong>OK</strong> to save it. You will see the altered information in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog.</p><p>      5.  Click <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="close-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong>.</p><h3>Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys</h3><p>Macro Engine provides mnemonic keys for you to perform some operations, for example, highlight a textbox and click a specific button in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog. The table below lists the<strong> Macro Information</strong> dialog mnemonic keys and their function. Below is a table listing of Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup> <col /> <col /> </colgroup><thead style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th><p>Mnemonic keys</p></th><th><p> Function</p></th></tr></thead><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><strong>Alt + N</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To place the pointer in the <span>box.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="name-box.png" /></ac:image> </strong> </p></div></td></tr><tr pointer in the<strong> </strong>box.<br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="file-box.png" /></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + l</strong></td><td colspan="1">To delete an argument in the <strong>Arguments</strong> area.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + U</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To highlight the <strong>Current keys </strong>box.<strong> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current-keys-area.png" /></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + G</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To click the <span>button.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + R</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To click the <span>button.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + E</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To click the <span>button.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ok-btn.png" /></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + C</strong></td><td <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968639 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 04586: Adding a new Metric Suite

- page_id: `243968639`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968639/Adding+a+new+Metric+Suite
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:05.327+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

189812560

189812562

189812561

There are two ways to add a new Metric Suite to your model:

- Create a Metric Suite using the BaseMetricSuite (recommended).
- Create a Metric Suite from scratch.

The *BaseMetricSuite* is a predefined Metric Suite stored in the *DSL Customization* profile of the *UML Standard Profile*.

You are free to choose either way, but we highly recommend the first one. It aids the creation of a new Metric Suite, which inherits the *BaseMetricSuite*attributes that are necessary for calculating metrics (e.g., the context parameter definition and the metric definition for date tracking). This way you don’t have to specify them on your own.

To create a Metric Suite using the *BaseMetricSuite*

1. Make sure that your project uses the UML Standard Profile . If not, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space=''] by going to Options > Project Usages .
2. Create a Package to store all the Metric Suites of your model.
3. In the Package, create a Profile Diagram. If you can't find it in the list of available diagrams, enable the Expert mode.
4. Press Ctrl+Alt+F and in the **Quick Find** dialog type “BMS”. Make sure you clear the **Apply Filter** check box.
5. Click the *BaseMetricSuite* in the list of search results. The *BaseMetricSuite* is selected in the Containment tree.
6. Drag the *BaseMetricSuite* from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. Its shape appears on the diagram.
7. In the diagram palette, click **MetricSuite**, and then click a free space on the diagram. A new metric suite is created and its shape appears on the diagram.
8. Create a Generalization relationship from the newly created Metric Suite to the *BaseMetricSuite.*

Now the newly created Metric Suite inherits all the parameter and metric definitions of the *BaseMetricSuite.*

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Metric Suite from scratch

1. Create a Package to store all the Metric Suites of your model.
2. In the Package, create a Profile Diagram. If you can't find it in the list of available diagrams, enable the Expert mode.
3. On the diagram palette, click MetricSuite , and then click a free space on the diagram.

A new Metric Suite is created and its shape appears on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify the description of a Metric Suite

1. Double-click the shape of a Metric Suite to open its Specification window.
2. Click Documentation/Hyperlinks on the left side of the window.
3. In the text box on the right side of the window, type the description.
4. Click Close .

189812558

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric Suites' space='']

This way you don’t have to specify them on your own.<br /> </p><p>To create a Metric Suite using the <em>BaseMetricSuite</em></p><hr /><ol><li>Make sure that your project uses the <em>UML Standard Profile</em>. If not, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use it]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by going to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>Create a Package to store all the Metric Suites of your model.</li><li>In the Package, create a Profile Diagram. If you can't find it in the list of available diagrams, enable the <strong>Expert</strong> mode.</li><li><p>Press Ctrl+Alt+F and in the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog type “BMS”.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="716d272f-7194-4097-9ec2-3c9640933c0c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure you clear the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Click the <em>BaseMetricSuite</em> in the list of search results.
