Its shape appears on the diagram.</p></li><li><p>In the diagram palette, click <strong>MetricSuite</strong>, and then click a free space on the diagram. A new metric suite is created and its shape appears on the diagram.</p></li><li><p>Create a Generalization relationship from the newly created Metric Suite to the <em>BaseMetricSuite.</em></p></li></ol><p>Now the newly created Metric Suite inherits all the parameter and metric definitions of the <em>BaseMetricSuite.</em><br /> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_metric_suite.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Metric Suite from scratch</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Package to store all the Metric Suites of your model.</li><li>In the Package, create a Profile Diagram. If you can't find it in the list of available diagrams, enable the <strong>Expert</strong> mode.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <strong>MetricSuite</strong>, and then click a free space on the diagram.</li></ol><p>A new Metric Suite is created and its shape appears on the diagram.<br /> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_metric_suite.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the description of a Metric Suite</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the shape of a Metric Suite to open its Specification window.</li><li>Click <strong>Documentation/Hyperlinks</strong> on the left side of the window.</li><li>In the text box on the right side of the window, type the description.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be351d3f-f0c0-4156-a9a2-4100f77fdee7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">189812558</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metric Suites" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specify the target for the Metric Suite" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying parameter definitions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building metric definitions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building validation-based metric definitions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specific use cases" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973853 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04587: Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool

- page_id: `243973853`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973853/Adding+a+scripting+language+to+the+modeling+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T19:18:22.690+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Macro engine > Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add a new scripting language that supports JSR-223 to the modeling tool by editing the Macro Engine's *plugin.xml* file.

To add a new scripting language

1. Open the plugin.xml file in <modeling tool name>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton/plugin.xml .
2. Add the following jar file tags to the runtime section and save the file.

```text
"/>]]>
```

The path to the jar file is relative to *<modeling tool name>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton* or the absolute pathname can be used, for example, if you want to add Sleep programming language, add the following tag to the runtime section:

```text
]]>
```

A slash (/) is used as a directory separator for all platforms, including Windows. The new language will be displayed in most of the Macro Engine dialogs, for example, in the Macros section in the **Environment Options** dialog, the**Create Macro** dialog, and in the**Macro Information** dialog. The language name is automatically configured from the information inside the jar file. This version of Macro Engine does not allow you to modify the language name.

##### Script Filename Extension Filter

When you open an **Open** file dialog to browse for a script file in Macro Engine, you can find the filename extension for the script file in the dialog file filter. If you have added a new scripting language to the modeling tool, for example, Sleep programming language, Macro Engine will add the filename extension (**.sl*)” in the**Open** file dialog.

The filename extensions are derived from jar files. There can be more than one filename extension for each engine. However, the current Macro Engine version only shows the first filename extension that is queried from the engine jar file.

You can remove a scripting language from Macro Engine by removing the associated library tag in the *plugin.xml* file. Once the tag has been removed, the language will be removed from all of the dialogs in Macro Engine. However, the script configured to be used with the language will not be removed from the modeling tool. The language of the script will be reset to the default language, which has been previously configured in the modeling tool**Environment Options**dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add a new scripting language that supports JSR-223 to the modeling tool by editing the Macro Engine's <em>plugin.xml</em> file.</p><p>To add a new scripting language</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>plugin.xml</em> file in <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton/plugin.xml</em>.</li><li>Add the following jar file tags to the runtime section and save the file.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fbf9693-33ba-409d-9d78-9127521855df"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[... <library name="<path to jar file>"/>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The path to the jar file is relative to <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton</em> or the absolute pathname can be used, for example, if you want to add Sleep programming language, add the following tag to the runtime section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6dcab4db-4170-4d46-b884-f6717061987a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[... <library name="c:/sleep_2.1.jar"/>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>A slash (/) is used as a directory separator for all platforms, including Windows. The new language will be displayed in most of the Macro Engine dialogs, for example,  in the Macros section in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, the<strong> Create Macro</strong> dialog, and in the<strong> Macro Information</strong> dialog. The language name is automatically configured from the information inside the jar file. This version of Macro Engine does not allow you to modify the language name.</p><h3>Script Filename Extension Filter</h3><p>When you open an <strong>Open</strong> file dialog to browse for a script file in Macro Engine, you can find the filename extension for the script file in the dialog file filter. If you have added a new scripting language to the modeling tool, for example, Sleep programming language, Macro Engine will add the filename extension (<em>*.sl</em>)” in the<strong> Open</strong> file dialog.</p><p>The filename extensions are derived from jar files. There can be more than one filename extension for each engine. However, the current Macro Engine version only shows the first filename extension that is queried from the engine jar file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0065b927-4b15-4e6a-85a2-56407b6d0c49"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can remove a scripting language from Macro Engine by removing the associated library tag in the <em>plugin.xml</em> file. Once the tag has been removed, the language will be removed from all of the dialogs in Macro Engine. However, the script configured to be used with the language will not be removed from the modeling tool. The language of the script will be reset to the default language, which has been previously configured in the modeling tool </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> dialog.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563555 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04588: Adding Actual Project Milestones in Lr diagram

- page_id: `249563555`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563555/Adding+Actual+Project+Milestones+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:14.945+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['add-milestone-in-lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add an Actual Project Milestone in Lr diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > **Actual Project** Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add an Actual Project Milestone in Lr diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Actual Project</strong> </span><strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563557 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04589: Adding Actual Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `249563557`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563557/Adding+Actual+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:58.599+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['add-project-in-lr', 'add-sub-project-in-lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select a Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Lr diagra m toolbar, click **Add New** > **Actual Sub Project**.
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Project**,**choose**Add new Actual Sub Project.******
3. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Specify a time frame for the Actual Sub Project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Actual</span> Project</strong> dialog, select a Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Actual S</span>ub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Lr diagra<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">m toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the shortcut menu of the selected Project<strong>, </strong>choose<strong> Add new Actual Sub Project.</strong><strong><br /></strong></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify a time frame for the Actual Sub Project.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563194 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04590: Adding actual projects to NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563194`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563194/Adding+actual+projects+to+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:19.293+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add a new or an existing actual project to the NPV-1 diagram.

To add a new actual project

1. On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.
2. Specify a time frame for the actual project.

To add a new actual sub project

1. Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.

To add an existing actual project

1. On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add a new or an existing actual project to the NPV-1 diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual sub project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click <strong>Add new Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966866 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04591: Adding and removing rows

- page_id: `243966866`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966866/Adding+and+removing+rows
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:11.267+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Working with rows
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Adding a new row

You can create a new element in the model by adding a new row in the table. Each type of table requires a different procedure to add new row(s).

To add a new row in the Generic and Glossary tables

1. On the table toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new.png'] button.

#### INFO: More information

More information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Glossary table' space='']]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='']]]>

To add a new row in the Instance Table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table.png'] > [ATTACHMENT filename='button_create.png']

#### INFO: More information

More information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='']]]>

To add a new row in the Metric Table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calculate_metrics.png'] > [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_metric_with_different_parameters.png']

#### INFO: More information

More information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric table' space='']]]>

To add an existing element as a row in the Generic, Instance, Glossary, Requirement, and Metric tables

1. In the table toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add_existing.png'] button.
2. Select the element existing in the model and click OK .

##### Removing a row from a table

To remove a row from a table

1. Select the whole row or select one cell of the row you want to remove.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Remove From Table button on the table toolbar.
  - Press Ctrl+Delete on the keyboard.

##### Removing an element from a model

You can delete an element from the model when removing the row from a table.

To remove an element from a table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting an entire row' space=''] or select one cell of the row you want to remove.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Delete button on the table toolbar.
  - Press Ctrl+D on the keyboard.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563140 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04592: Adding capabilities to NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `249563140`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563140/Adding+capabilities+to+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:03.452+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new capability

- On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add a new capability provision

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] .
4. Click Finish , when you are done.

To add an existing capability

1. On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new capability</p><hr /><ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a new capability provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ad34cb3-d429-4779-b639-b6814c5255ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565246 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04593: Adding custom columns in tables

- page_id: `249565246`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565246/Adding+custom+columns+in+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:35.733+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Managing tables
- labels: ['add-custom-columns-in-tables', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add any desired column to your table by clicking**Show Columns** button.

When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.

To add a custom column

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns > Select Columns .
2. In the Select Columns dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add any desired column to your table by clicking<strong> Show Columns</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca5d35fe-1fbe-4d0e-8d88-8ad29bae7b56"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To add a custom column</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Show</strong><strong> Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong> dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.</li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_kind_and_confirms_to_columns_added.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc0393f3-60e1-4ab4-a6ef-1695f9a65e27"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563358 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04594: Adding Existing Resource Exchanges to NSV-6 table

- page_id: `249563358`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563358/Adding+Existing+Resource+Exchanges+to+NSV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:24.008+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to add existing Resource Exchanges to fill in an NSV-6 table.

To add existing Resource Exchanges

1. Click the Add Existing and select the Resource Exchange Kind:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
2. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an NSV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can remove the Rows (Resource Exchanges) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to add existing Resource Exchanges to fill in an NSV-6 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add existing Resource Exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> and select the  Resource Exchange Kind:<ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="018e2a36-4f7f-41d9-bae8-ea1ae22a91b2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange identifier, Resource <span>Exchange </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.</li><li>You may use an NSV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.</li><li>You can remove the Rows (Resource Exchanges) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

- Resource Exchange identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an SV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973851 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04597: Adding JRuby and installing Gems

- page_id: `243973851`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973851/Adding+JRuby+and+installing+Gems
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T19:18:22.535+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Macro engine > Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

147042186

147042197

147042185

**On this page:**

147042196

By default, JRuby is not included in the modeling tool, however, you can add it manually. In addition, you can also install Gems for JRuby.

##### Adding JRuby to the modeling tool

To add JRuby to the modeling tool

1. Copy the JRuby engine jar file (e.g. jruby-complete-9.2.14.0.jar ) to <*tool_installation_directory*>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib.
2. Add the entry below to the file *<tool_installation_directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\enginedescriptor.xml.* rb
 
 JRuby
]]>
3. Append a library entry in file *<*tool_installation_directory*>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\plugin.xml.* ]]>

##### Installing Gems for JRuby

To install a gem for Ruby engine in the modeling tool

1. Open the command line.
2. Go to the folder <*tool_installation_directory*>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib.
3. Enter the following command to install a gem.

```text
.jar -S gem install [--user-install]   ...]]>
```

- <version> is the JRuby version, e.g. 9.2.14.0.
- <gem name> is the name of a gem.
- The three dots (...) mean that you can type a list of gem names there.

Once the gem has been installed, you can use it in the Macro Engine. The example below shows how to create a macro that can generate a unique ID by using a gem.

```text

```

To install a gem for the existing Ruby environment on your machine and use it in the Macro Engine

1. Add the following properties to specify the home and library of JRuby in <**tool_installation_directory**>/bin/mduml.properties under JAVA_ARGS section (assuming you have JRuby installed in C:/jruby-9.2.14.0 on your machine).

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

2. Change the JRuby library path to navigate to your *jruby.jar* in *<***tool_installation_directory***>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton/plugin.xml*.

```text
]]>
```

1. The command in this section should run in <****tool_installation_directory****>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton/engine .
2. If you have a whitespace in the property file path, you need to type a double quote to wrap both the property and its value, for each property, for example: “ -Djruby.home=C:/my white space/jruby-*9.2.14.0* " “ -Djruby.lib=C:/my white space/jruby-*9.2.14.0*/lib ”

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7952164a-d50b-40e1-b7e8-780e1ddcae2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042186</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5510481d-508a-4e41-8e15-8c1e88940b0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042197</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60dbe2fc-87e5-41e8-8e2f-b941f68ccbfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042185</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="acfb9d0a-77f5-4fbb-ac3e-6150c37672dc" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1957e301-6d12-45bf-ab73-a0ef2d6693a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042196</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, JRuby is not included in the modeling tool, however, you can add it manually. In addition, you can also install Gems for JRuby.  </p><h3>Adding JRuby to the modeling tool</h3><p>To add JRuby to the modeling tool</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy the JRuby engine <em>jar</em> file (e.g. <em>jruby-complete-9.2.14.0.jar</em>) to <em>&lt;<em>tool_installation_directory</em>&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Add the entry below to the file <em>&lt;tool_installation_directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\enginedescriptor.xml.</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="922f581c-6995-4cd3-b2bd-f55a677b2495"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<void method="add">

			<string>JRuby</string>
		</void>
	</object>
</void>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Append a library entry in file <em>&lt;<em>tool_installation_directory</em>&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\plugin.xml.</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a16e952e-af3e-4799-9626-f0d532a95518"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<library name="lib/jruby-complete-9.2.14.0.jar"/>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Installing Gems for JRuby</h3><p>To install a gem for Ruby engine in the modeling tool</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the command line.</li><li>Go to the folder <em>&lt;<em>tool_installation_directory</em>&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib.</em></li><li>Enter the following command to install a gem.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e484a77-ecae-4168-a38b-26100dd55d04"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -jar jruby-complete-<version>.jar -S gem install [--user-install] <gem name1> <gem name2> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9533940-40a5-4b95-aebc-1f9b5eae7999"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>&lt;version&gt; is the <span>JRuby </span>version, e.g. 9.2.14.0.</li><li>&lt;gem name&gt; is the name of a gem.</li><li>The three dots (...) mean that you can type a list of gem names there.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Once the gem has been installed, you can use it in the Macro Engine. The example below shows how to create a macro that can generate a unique ID by using a gem.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77be0e79-ded9-490d-9b06-d32a81c58189"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[require 'java'
require 'rubygems'
require 'uuid'
Application = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application
uuid = UUID.new Application.getInstance().getGUILog().log(uuid.generate);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To install a gem for the existing Ruby environment on your machine and use it in the Macro Engine</p><hr /><ol><li>Add the following properties to specify the home and library of JRuby in <em>&lt;<em><em>tool_installation_directory</em></em>&gt;/bin/mduml.properties</em> under JAVA_ARGS section (assuming you have JRuby installed in <em>C:/jruby-9.2.14.0</em> on your machine).<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="444f4bbe-f1fc-4c54-9c42-f3f9481c8468"><ac:parameter <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563201 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04598: Adding Milestones in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563201`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563201/Adding+Milestones+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:19.515+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a milestone

1. Select an actual project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone
4. The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
5. Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul></li><li>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971164 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04599: Adding more report data directories

- page_id: `243971164`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971164/Adding+more+report+data+directories
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:06.795+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

267209495

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

267209497

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

267209496

The default report data directories for operating systems are as follows:

- For Windows: text\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\\data\reports]]>
- For Linux: text/./< modeling tool version number>]]>

Report Wizard provides a configuration to add more report data directories by creating a **report.properties** file in the Report Wizard plugin directory, *<modeling tool name>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard*.

You can declare the **template_loc** parameter in the **report.properties** file to specify another report data directory as follows:

```text

```

You can also specify the **template_loc** parameter for multiple report data directories by using a semicolon (;) to separate each of the directories.

```text

```

All of templates in the default report data directory and other locations specified in the **report.properties** file are available for generating in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard dialog' space='']**Report Wizard** dialog and can be called through the **-template** parameter in command line report generation.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The template names defined in the **template.xml** file in each report data directory must be unique.

267209494

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuring Report Wizard' space=''] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuring Report Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard Environment options' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Windows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a28ec22e-0f85-40e6-bebd-e1a4b8876d64"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\<version>\data\reports]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p
