ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<user.home>/.<modeling tool name>/< modeling tool version number>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p>Report Wizard provides a configuration to add more report data directories by creating a <strong>report.properties</strong> file in the Report Wizard plugin directory, <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard</em>.</p><p><br />You can declare the <strong>template_loc</strong> parameter in the <strong>report.properties</strong> file to specify another report data directory as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51e6feb7-8bce-4f41-a6f5-28c87018c62b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template_loc=C:/Users/MD_user/AppData/Local/.magicdraw/new Location]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>You can also specify the <strong>template_loc</strong> parameter for multiple report data directories by using a semicolon (;) to separate each of the directories.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29ffda9b-ba60-4eba-a498-1c26974a6010"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template_loc=//template_server/MagicReport/;C:/Users/MD_user/AppData/Local/.magicdraw/new Location]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>All of templates in the default report data directory and other locations specified in the <strong>report.properties</strong> file are available for generating in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and can be called through the <strong>-template</strong> parameter in command line report generation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14238e77-8e36-45c5-b275-31b394f131be"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The template names defined in the <strong>template.xml</strong> file in each report data directory must be unique.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e953e83d-035d-4301-89e4-3da381fb43b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">267209494</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuring Report Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configuring Report]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuring Report Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard Environment options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563356 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04600: Adding New Resource Exchanges to NSV-6 table

- page_id: `249563356`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563356/Adding+New+Resource+Exchanges+to+NSV-6+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:07.954+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add new Resource Exchanges

1. Click Add New . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created resource interactions are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add new Resource Exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Add New</strong>. <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done. The rows with created resource interactions are added to the table.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966947 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04601: Adding or removing element types

- page_id: `243966947`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966947/Adding+or+removing+element+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:13.304+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Generic table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add or remove element types

1. In the Criteria area, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button next to the Element Type box. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='']**Select Element Type** dialog with the element and stereotype list opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='criteria_element_type.png']
2. Do one of the following:

- To add the element types, select the appropriate check boxes in the list. Properties corresponding to the selected types will be added to the list of available columns.
- To remove the element types, click to clear the appropriate check boxes in the list. Properties corresponding to the deselected types will be removed from the list of available columns. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Select the Include subtypes check box to display the subtypes of the selected element types.

- Select the Include Custom Types check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.

To add an element type, you can also drag a selected element to the **Element Type** box directly from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''].

- To select adjacent elements, select a single element and hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.
- To select nonadjacent elements, select a single element and hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.

The new value of the **Element Type** box replaces the old value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add or remove element types</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Criteria</strong> area, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png" /></ac:image> button next to the <strong>Element Type</strong> box. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Element Type</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog with the element and stereotype list opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="criteria_element_type.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li><p>To add the element types, select the appropriate check boxes in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="190523af-47bd-4ba9-855b-72006d24be89"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Properties corresponding to the selected types will be added to the list of available columns.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>To remove the element types, click to clear the appropriate check boxes in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3310dc5-b68f-4eb4-baa3-0b243a15fde5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Properties corresponding to the deselected types will be removed from the list of available columns.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_type.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb05b02-bf43-4df6-8bcc-bcb5fb812b3d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>Select the </span><strong>Include subtypes</strong><span> check box to display the subtypes of the selected element types.</span></li></ul><ul><li><span>Select the </span><strong>Include Custom Types</strong><span> check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To add an element type, you can also drag a selected element to the <strong>Element Type</strong> box directly from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</p><ul><li>To select <em>adjacent</em> elements, select a single element and hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.</li><li>To select <em>nonadjacent</em> elements, select a single element and hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.</li></ul><p>The new value of the <strong>Element Type</strong> box replaces the old value.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_element_type_generic_table.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563142 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04602: Adding sub capabilities to NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `249563142`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563142/Adding+sub+capabilities+to+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:17.941+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Sub Capability .
3. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add an existing sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click A dd existing Sub Capability .
3. In the opened Select Capability dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click A<strong>dd existing Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da049ff8-52ce-4793-997c-e889f70f4ec3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966970 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04603: Adding terms in the Glossary Table

- page_id: `243966970`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966970/Adding+terms+in+the+Glossary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:14.254+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Glossary table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add terms in the Glossary Table in the following ways: 
43

##### Directly from the diagram pane

To add a word or phrase to a glossary directly from the diagram pane

1. Select a word or phrase you want to be a term. Even if you mark only a part of a word, the entire word will be added to the glossary as a term.
2. Right-click it and select Add To <Glossary Table name>. The word or phrase is converted to a term and added to the Glossary Table. [ATTACHMENT filename='glossary_add.png']

##### Using the Generic Table toolbar

To add a term in the Glossary Table using a toolbar

- Click the Add New button to create a new row for a term in the table.
- Click the Add Existing button and from the Select Element dialog. Choose the element you wish to be a term.

##### By dragging any element

To drag an element to the Glossary Table

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you wish to be a term.
2. Drag it to the Glossary Table.

##### By pasting from another resource

To create terms in the table by pasting data from another resource

1. Copy a table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Glossary Table.The data types of columns in the Glossary table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Glossary Table where you want to paste the copied data. The cells must be editable in the Glossary Table.
3. Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
Each row displays one term with its description. If the term already exists in the model, the information updates.If terms have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted terms in the Glossary Table. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='']**Element Numbering** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Adding synonyms of the terms

You can add one or more synonyms for every term. After that, the synonyms are available in the suggestion list. When you use a synonym, the original term is displayed in the description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To add a synonym

- In the term's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']**Specification** window , Synonym property specification cell, type the synonyms for the term. [ATTACHMENT filename='synonyms.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add terms in the Glossary Table in the following ways:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6684ea6d-ca59-4c0f-815c-f50bde5487b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Directly from the diagram pane</h3><p>To add a word or phrase to a glossary directly from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select a word or phrase you want to be a term.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcebc2ff-42e8-463b-b08f-c5d19c16e556"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Even if you mark only a part of a word, the entire word will be added to the glossary as a term.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Right-click it and select<strong> Add To &lt;Glossary Table name&gt;.</strong> <br />The word or phrase is converted to a term and added to the Glossary Table.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="glossary_add.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Using the Generic Table toolbar</h3><p>To add a term in the Glossary Table using a toolbar</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to create a new row for a term in the table.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and from the<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog. Choose the element you wish to be a term.</li></ul><h3>By dragging any element</h3><p>To drag an element to the Glossary Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select an element you wish to be a term.</li><li>Drag it to the Glossary Table.</li></ol><h3>By pasting from another resource</h3><p>To create terms in the table by pasting data from another resource</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Copy a table from another resource.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5799968b-3b43-4cd1-8c33-87d572954aec"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Glossary Table.</li><li>The data types of columns in the Glossary table must be compatible with copied information.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open the Glossary Table where you want to paste the copied data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e77cc25f-6962-4a18-bd50-6ae2f1e0c262"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The cells must be editable in the Glossary Table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X).<br />Each row displays one term with its description.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aa3ecfe-f5e2-4189-8710-ee308ed4e62d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the term already exists in the model, the information updates.</li><li>If terms have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted terms in the Glossary Table. Manage ID prefixes in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="paste_table_in_glossary_table.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Adding synonyms of the terms</h3><p>You can add one or more synonyms for every term. After that, the synonyms are available in the suggestion list. When you use a synonym, the original term is displayed in the description.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synonym_example.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To add a synonym</p><hr /><ul><li>In the term's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:link-body><strong>Specification</strong> window</ac:link-body></ac:link>, Synonym property specification cell, type the synonyms for the term.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synonyms.png" /></ac:image></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970865 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04604: Adding variables into an output report filename

- page_id: `243970865`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970865/Adding+variables+into+an+output+report+filename
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:00.894+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the following variables and include them in an output report filename.

- Date variable
- Template variable
- Random number
- Project attribute

#### Date variable

A date variable is a variable for the current date and time. (See section **$date** in the previous chapter). The date variable consists of:

- A date variable with the default format.
- A date variable with a customized date format.

##### Date variables with default format

Using a date variable without an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a default format.

The syntax: **${date}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html*”.

##### Date variable with custom format

Using a date variable with an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a specific format.

The syntax: **${date.get('format')}** or **${date.get("format")}**

For example:

```text

```

The output filename would be “*index_20111221-115544.html*”.

#### Template variable

A template variable is a variable for a template that is created in Report Wizard. It allows you to add all template variables into an output report filename (see **).

##### Including template variables in an output report filename

When you include template variables created in an output report filename, the output filename which consists of the variables will be converted into value. This section uses the following template variables as examples, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Adding a value of a top-level variable

You can retrieve a top-level variable in an output report filename by using the following syntax.

The syntax: **${Parent}**

For example:

```text

```

The report output filename would be “*index_parent value.html*”.

##### Adding a value of a child variable

You can retrieve any of the following child variable values in an output report filename.

1. Getting a child variable at an index position: The syntax : ${Parent.get(0)}
2. Getting a child variable by child names: The syntax : ${Parent.get('Child')} or ${Parent.get("Child")}
3. Getting a child value by referencing its name (in this case “Child”): The syntax : ${Parent.Child}

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_child value.html*”.

#### Random number

Report Wizard supports only pseudorandom numbers in an output report filename. The random number consists of:

- A random number with the default distributed int value.
- A random number with a distributed int value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive).

##### Random number with the default distributed integer value

You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value.

**The syntax: ${random}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_21547.html*”.

##### Random number with a distributed integer value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive)

You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value between 0 and a specific value.

**The syntax: ${random.nextInt(n)}**

The Parameters:

“n” - the bound on the random number to be returned. The “n” value must be a positive integer.

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_457.html*”.

#### Project attribute

You can add a project attribute or information about a project into an output report filename.

The project attribute consists of:

- Project name
- Project version

##### Project name

You can retrieve the name of a project by using the following syntax.

**The syntax: ${project.name}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_myProject.html*”.

##### Project version

You can retrieve a project version by using the following syntax.

**The syntax: ${project.version}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_1234.html*”.

#### Exception flow

You may accidentally specify an invalid output report filename such as:

- An invalid syntax variable.
- A variable that does not exist in a template variable.
- An invalid parameter.

If this happens, Report Wizard will use this variable as a filename and a warning message will open in a log file (*\.modeling tool\version\md.log*).

For example:

```text

```

A single quote after “yyyyMMdd-hhmmss” was missing. Therefore, the output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss)}.html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax.

For example:

```text

```

“${Parent}” was not in the report template variable. Therefore, the output filename would be "index_${Parent}.html" and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable.

For example:

```text

```

In this example, ‘yyyy-MM-dd qq’ is an invalid parameter. Therefore, output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyy-MM-dd qq').html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter.

- All variables should be able to be combined together. For example: C:\OutputReport\index_${project.name}_${date}.html The output filename would be “ index_myProject_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html ”.
- The template variable is the highest priority of all other variables. For example: C:\OutputReport\index_${random}.html

If you specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the variable value will be used as the filename.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The output filename would be “*index_myRandomNumber.html*”.

If you do not specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the random number with the default distributed integer value will be used as the filename.

The output filename would be “*index_12345.html*”.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can use the following variables and include them in an output report filename.</p><ul><li>Date variable</li><li>Template variable</li><li>Random number</li><li>Project attribute</li></ul><h2>Date variable</h2><p>A date variable is a variable for the current date and time. (See section <strong>$date</strong> in the previous chapter). The date variable consists of:</p><ul><li>A date variable with the default format.</li><li>A date variable with a customized date format.</li></ul><h3>Date variables with default format</h3><p>Using a date variable without an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a default format.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${date}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4218d084-8f1e-410b-9f13-b52437369ab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html</em>”.</p><h3>Date variable with custom format</h3><p>Using a date variable with an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a specific format.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${date.get('format')}</strong> or <strong>${date.get(&quot;format&quot;)}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aecf2d93-fb72-44e3-8f95-783788c2373d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss')}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output filename would be “<em>index_20111221-115544.html</em>”.</p><h2>Template variable</h2><p>A template variable is a variable for a template that is created in Report Wizard. It allows you to add all template variables into an output report filename (see <em><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Including Variables in a Template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>).</p><h3>Including template variables in an output report filename</h3><p>When you include template variables created in an output report filename, the output filename which consists of the variables will be converted into value. This section uses the following template variables as examples, see figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Sample of Report Variables" ac:alt="Sample of Report Variables"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Variable Sample.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h3>Adding a value of a top-level variable</h3><p>You can retrieve a top-level variable in an output report filename by using the following syntax.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${Parent}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30eb8db7-f2a3-4920-814d-2147940b9d15"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${Parent}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The report output filename would be “<em>index_parent value.html</em>”.</p><h3>Adding a value of a child variable</h3><p>You can retrieve any of the following child variable values in an output report filename.</p><ol><li>Getting a child variable at an index position:<br /><strong>The syntax</strong>: <strong>${Parent.get(0)}</strong></li><li>Getting a child variable by child names: <br /><strong>The syntax</strong>: <strong>${Parent.get('Child')} or ${Parent.get(&quot;Child&quot;)}</strong></li><li>Getting a child value by referencing its name (in this case “Child”): <br /><strong>The syntax</strong>: <strong>${Parent.Child} </strong></li></ol><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3da87559-1e46-41b0-b0e2-b94f207df042"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${Parent.get('Child')}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_child value.html</em>”.</p><h2>Random number</h2><p>Report Wizard supports only pseudorandom numbers in an output report filename. The random number consists of:</p><ul><li>A random number with the default distributed int value.</li><li>A random number with a distributed int value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive).</li></ul><h3>Random number with the default distributed integer value</h3><p>You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${random}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed4ac6b9-5e5c-49e6-8e3e-0c5f83ce9ec7"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${random}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_21547.html</em>”.</p><h3>Random number with a distributed integer value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive)</h3><p>You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value between 0 and a specific value.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${random.nextInt(n)}</strong></p><p>The Parameters:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">“n” - the bound on the random number to be returned. The “n” value must be a positive integer.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1aaf3977-17dd-4ccb-86a8-7c7e1d032ff6"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${random.nextInt(1000)}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_457.html</em>”.</p><h2>Project attribute</h2><p>You can add a project attribute or information about a project into an output report filename.</p><p>The project attribute consists of:</p><ul><li>Project name</li><li>Project version</li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Project name</span></h3><p>You can retrieve the name of a project by using the following syntax.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${project.name}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10dd6bfc-808f-42de-ad91-6d6f2165748f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${project.name}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_myProject.html</em>”. </p><h3>Project version</h3><p>You can retrieve a project version by using the following syntax.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${project.version}</strong></p><p>For example: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e48ef7f-500e-4db7-8925-8d9ab6340600"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${project.version}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_1234.html</em>”.</p><h2>Exception flow</h2><p>You may accidentally specify an invalid output report filename such as:</p><ul><li>An invalid syntax variable.</li><li>A variable that does not exist in a template variable.</li><li>An invalid parameter.</li></ul><p>If this happens, Report Wizard will use this variable as a filename and a warning message will open in a log file (<em>\.modeling tool\version\md.log</em>).</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f339326a-7d57-476c-9f59-1c36c425156f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss)}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A single quote after “yyyyMMdd-hhmmss” was missing. Therefore, the output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss)}.html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax" ac:alt="A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Invalid Variable.PNG" /></ac:image><br />A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax.</h6><p>For example: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="554bea4d-a169-4398-9642-ffb25427a090"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${Parent}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>“${Parent}” was not in the report template variable. Therefore, the output filename would be &quot;index_${Parent}.html&quot; and a warning message would open, see figure below.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable" ac:alt="A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specified Variable Not Existing.PNG" /></ac:image><br />A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable.</h6><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ce18582-9311-47cd-b2bd-363dad75699e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date.get('yyyy-MM-dd qq')}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In this example, ‘yyyy-MM-dd qq’ is an invalid parameter. Therefore, output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyy-MM-dd qq').html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter" ac:alt="A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Invalid Parameter.PNG" /></ac:image><br />A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52cfdfa9-f690-4409-b467-39a9e6cba383"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>All variables should be able to be combined together. For example:<br /><em>C:\OutputReport\index_${<span style="line-height: 1.42857;">project.name</span>}_${date}.html</em><br />The output filename would be “<em>index_myProject_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html</em>”.</li><li>The template variable is the highest priority of all other variables. For example:<br /><em>C:\OutputReport\index_${random}.html</em></li></ul><p>If you specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the variable value will be used as the filename.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Template Variable Name at Random " ac:alt="Template Variable Name at Random "><ri:attachment ri:filename="Random Template Variable Name.PNG" /></ac:image></p><p>The output filename would be “<em>index_myRandomNumber.html</em>”.</p><p>If you do not specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the random number with the default distributed integer value will be used as the filename.</p><p>The output filename would be “<em>index_12345.html</em>”.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563373 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04605: Adding version of configurations to NSV-8 chart

- page_id: `249563373`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563373/Adding+version+of+configurations+to+NSV-8+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:24.433+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-8 System Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a version of configuration

1. On the NSV-8 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Version of Configuration . On the diagram pane, an empty line appears.
2. Name the created Version of Configuration.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a version of configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NSV-8 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Version of Configuration</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line appears.</li><li>Name the created Version of Configuration.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968447 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04606: Adding, removing, and changing order of favorites

- page_id: `243968447`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968447/Adding+removing+and+changing+order+of+favorites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:28.524+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Favorites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1539458257

1539458269

1539458259

There are a few ways to add an element to favorites:

- Using the Add Selected to Favorites command from the Favorites menu. This way is especially handy for quick additions to favorites.
- Using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space=''] dialog. Choose this way when you need to add and then perform other manipulations with favorites.

To quickly add an element to favorites

1. Select the element in the Model Browser and click [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] on the toolbar of the open tab. [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_quick_add.png']
2. Click Add Selected to Favorites on the menu.

To add an element to favorites in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='']** dialog

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space=''] dialog.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space=''] dialog, select the element and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] button, which is between two lists of the dialog. The element appears in the list of favorites.
3. Click OK to confirm the addition and close the dialog.

Once the element is added to favorites, the following changes occur in your project:

- The element becomes marked as a favorite in all trees and lists of the model.
- You can easily navigate to the element in the Model Browser. The element name is added to the Go To Favorites command group on the Favorites menu that opens in the Model Browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_goto_favorites.png']

To remove an element from favorites

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space=''] dialog.
2. In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click the element you need to remove and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove.png'] button.
3. Click OK to confirm the removal and close the dialog.

Once the element is removed from favorites, it is no longer marked. Commands for navigation to the element and for searching in it are no longer available on the Favorites menu.

The following procedure describes how to change the order of favorite elements on the Favorites menu. To change the order of favorites on the Favorites menu, you need to make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='']** dialog.

To change the order of favorites

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='']**Manage Favorites** dialog.
2. In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click an element whose order on the Favorites menu you need to change and then do either of the following:
  - If you need to move the element up, click the Up button. Click again if you need to move the element up by one more position and so on.
  - If you need to move the element down, click the Down button. Click again if you need to move the element down by one more position and so on.
3. Click OK to confirm the reorder and close the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Moving the element up in the Favorites menu.

1539458256

**Related pages**

- Favorites

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The element appears in the list of favorites.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the addition and close the dialog.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Once the element is added to favorites, the following changes occur in your project:</p><ul><li>The element becomes marked as a favorite in all trees and lists of the model.</li><li>You can easily navigate to the element in the Model Browser. The element name is added to the <strong>Go To Favorites</strong> command group on the Favorites menu that opens in the Model Browser.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorites_goto_favorites.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></li></ul><p>To remove an element from favorites</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manage favorites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage Favorites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</li><li>In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click the element you need to remove and then click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove.png" /></ac:image>button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the removal and close the dialog.</li></ol><p>Once the element is removed from favorites, it is no longer marked. Commands for navigation to the element and for searching in it are no longer available on the Favorites menu.</p><p>The following procedure describes how to change the order of favorite elements on the Favorites menu. To change the order of favorites on the Favorites menu, you need to make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manage favorites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage Favorites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the order of favorites</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manage favorites" /><ac:link-body><strong> Manage Favorites</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>dialog.</li><li>In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click an element whose order on the Favorites menu you need to change and then do either of the following:<ul><li>If you need to move the element up, click the <strong>Up</strong> button. Click again if you need to move the element up by one more position and so on.</li><li>If you need to move the element down, click the <strong>Down</strong> button. Click again if you need to move the element down by one more position and so on.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the reorder and close the dialog.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_favorites_order.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Moving the element up in the Favorites menu. </h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc16ea9c-7887-48dd-8eeb-70b574a427d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1539458256</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Favorites">Favorites</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968368 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 04607: Address tab

- page_id: `243968368`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968368/Address+tab
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-11T19:46:49.301+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks > Edit Hyperlink dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Address** tab is dedicated to adding, editing, or deleteing hyperlinks to webpages and other external resources.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of the Address tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog. The text "units" links to the unit description in the NoMagic documentation.

All boxes and buttons of the **Address** tab are described in the table below.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text to display box | Shows the text that will be displayed as a hyperlink. If you select the text before opening this dialog, you will see that text in this box. If you change the displayed text in this box, it will be changed in the HTML text field too. If this field is empty, you can type a text and it will be added in the HTML text field.It is available only when adding a hyperlink on text. |
| Type the address box | Allows you to type or paste the hyperlink address. |
| Select from list box | Displays all previously used hyperlinks. You can select them to fill in the Type the address box. |
|  | Opens the hyperlink in the web browser specified in the Text to display box. |
| The Clear button | Deletes all previously used hyperlinks from the Select from list box. |
| The Active check box | Activates the hyperlink if selected. It is available only when adding a hyperlink on element or entire diagram.Active check box ruleOnly one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. |

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The<strong> Address</strong> tab is dedicated to adding, editing, or deleteing hyperlinks to webpages and other external resources<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>.<br /></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_hyperlink_dialog_address_tab.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of the Address tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog. The text &quot;units&quot; links to <span class="confluence-link">the unit description in the NoMagic documentation.</span></h6><p>All boxes and buttons of the <strong>Address</strong> tab are described in the table below.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Text to display</strong> box</td><td><p>Shows the text that will be displayed as a hyperlink. If you select the text before opening this dialog, you will see that text in this box. If you change the displayed text in this box, it will be changed in the HTML text field too. If this field is empty, you can type a text and it will be added in the HTML text field.</p><p>It is available only when adding a hyperlink on text.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Type the address</strong> box</td><td>Allows you to type or paste the hyperlink address.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Select from list</strong> box</td><td>Displays all previously used hyperlinks. You can select them to fill in the <strong>Type the address</strong> box.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="27"><ri:attachment ri:filename="go_to_hyperlink_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Opens the hyperlink in the web browser specified in the <strong>Text to display</strong> box.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>The <strong>Clear</strong> button</td><td>Deletes all previously used hyperlinks from the <strong>Select from list</strong> box.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>The <strong>Active</strong> check box</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Activates the hyperlink if selected. It is available only when adding a hyperlink on element or entire diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="537cde56-2816-42b2-8c43-f80368ba6e20"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Active check box rule</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="75da945f-2d56-4601-884b-fef2d838d72f"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li data-uuid="96d34c7e-4564-4d78-bae1-cec242d169ff"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DEVG/?contextKey=managing-hyperlinks&amp;version=latest">Managing hyperlinks</a></li><li data-uuid="672ada12-25e7-4b14-893f-482d74d1b2bd"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=linking-model-elements-to-oslc-resources&amp;version=latest">Linking model elements to OSLC resources</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566517 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04608: AdHoc SubProcess

- page_id: `249566517`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566517/AdHoc+SubProcess
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:06.201+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram > Activities > SubProcesses
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487248268

1487248269

1487248269

**Description**

An AdHoc SubProcess is a specialized type of SubProcess, which is a group of activities that have no required sequence relationships. A set of activities can be defined for the process, but the sequence and number of performances for the activities are determined by the performers of the activities.

**Notation**

- Expanded [ATTACHMENT filename='expanded_adhoc.png']
- Collapsed [ATTACHMENT filename='suppressed_adhoc.png']

1487248267

**Related elements**

- Activities
- SubProcess
- Event SubProcess
- Transaction

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using SubProcesses

A set of activities can be defined for the process, but the sequence and number of performances for the activities are determined by the performers of the activities.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><ul><li>Expanded<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expanded_adhoc.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Collapsed<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="suppressed_adhoc.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0e3dc63-0e29-41d3-bfc9-b905d72c70d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487248267</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Activities">Activities</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/SubProcess">SubProcess</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Event+SubProcess">Event SubProcess</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Transaction">Transaction</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+and+Using+SubProcesses">Creating and Using SubProcesses</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968480 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04609: Advanced HTML Editor dialog

- page_id: `243968480`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968480/Advanced+HTML+Editor+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:29.738+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > HTML editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Advanced HTML Editor allows you to change text style, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inserting images into HTML text' space=''], insert symbols and tables, and perform other formatting actions.

To open the **Advanced HTML Editor** dialog

- Click the Advanced HTML Editor [ATTACHMENT filename='advanced_html_editor_button.png'] button on the HTML editor toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='html_editor_toolbar.png']

For more information, please see how to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space=''] in a dialog.

Click the**HTML source** tab to view the HTML source. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Advanced HTML Editor dialog

To insert an image into HTML text

1. Click the **Insert image** button, as shown in the following picture. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the Picture Properties dialog, specify the image location. Do one of the following:
  - Type the path to the image location.
  - Click **Browse** and select the image you want to insert. If you are working on a server project, make sure that the path to the image location is accessible from any computer that has an Internet connection. Otherwise, the image will not display when the project opens on another computer. To insert an image as an attached file, simply paste its URL to the **Picture Source** box by pressing Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inserting images into HTML text' space=''].
3. Click OK when you are done.

The image is inserted into HTML text.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image in HTML text.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565099 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04611: Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors

- page_id: `249565099`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565099/Allocate+Activities+to+State+Internal+Behaviors
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:03:40.511+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['activities-allocation', 'states', 'internal-behavior', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

890462394

890462398

890462397

You can allocate Activities to States in all state diagrams. Activities allocation in the UAF profile works in a similar way as in SysML v1.

To allocate an Activity to a State

1. Select an Activity, drag and hold it over the State shape in the diagram.
2. When the tool-tip appears, release the mouse button. The context menu appears.
3. Select one of the following:
  - Entry
  - Do Activity
  - Exit

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The context menu appears.</li><li>Select one of the following:<ul><li>Entry</li><li>Do Activity</li><li>Exit</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561722 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 04612: Allocation Mode

- page_id: `249561722`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561722/Allocation+Mode
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-08T17:06:11.472+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Functional analysis > Functional Allocations
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Allocation Matrix' space=''] or new swimlane in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space=''], you can select the kind **Definition** or **Usage** allocation mode. The definition elements are Activity and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']; usage elements are Action and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'].

When we talk about the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Allocation Matrix' space=''], the rows in the matrix represent either the Activity or Action that are the clients of the Allocate relationship. Columnsin the matrix represent either the Block or Part Property that are the suppliers of the Allocate relationship.

When creating a SysML Allocation Matrix in a SysML project for the first time, the**Create SysML Allocation Matrix** dialog appears, shown in the figure below. The usage allocation matrix is created by default when creating a new SysML Allocation Matrix in a MagicGrid project.You can change that by specifying the **Allocation Mode** option value in the **Project Options**dialog.>]]>

The **Allocation Mode** project option is global and will be applied for the SysML Allocation matrix and SysML Activity diagram (swimlanes).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The****Create SysML Allocation Matrix dialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.

The choice you made will be remembered and applied for the following matrices you will create in the project. The same applies to the swimlanes.

When you create a swimlane for the first time, the **Create Swimlane** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose one of the allocation modes and the choice will be remembered. The dialog will not be displayed if the Allocation Mode is already chosen when creating a SysML Allocation matrix.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Create Swimlane dialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.

The examples of Usage and Definition modes are illustrated below. Figures are created by using the [distiller model.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/distiller%20model.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1505483436734&api=v2) sample model that comes only with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v1 modeling' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The definition and usage modes in the SysML Allocation matrices.

##### Changing the Allocation Mode

Changing the Allocation Mode in the Project Options dialog applies the change for the SysML Allocation matrix and swimlanes.

change_modeTo change the default Allocation Mode

1. From the main menu, select **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the **Project Options**dialog, expand the **General** node and select **SysML**.
3. Select the **Allocation Mode**option value: 
 - Definition (Activity allocated to Block) 
 - Usage (Action allocated to Part) 
 - Ask and Remember
4. Click **OK**. The default Allocation Mode is selected and will be applied each time you create a new SysML Allocation matrix or new swimlanes in the SysML Activity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Allocation Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969402 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04616: Analyzing dependencies among elements

- page_id: `243969402`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969402/Analyzing+dependencies+among+elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T14:59:33.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Identifying Package Dependencies
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before exporting or sharing a package as an independent used project, you need to identify and solve package dependencies first. More specifically, you have to make sure that the packagedoes not depend on any external elements (except other used projects). Furthermore, cyclic dependencies between several used projects are not allowed.

##### relationshipPackage dependencies by relationship

The used project depends on the external elements which have relationships with the model elements from the used project packages.

external

###### The used project depends on external elements

If a used project's element has a relationship with an external element and that relationship is contained in the used project package, an error message appears when exporting the used package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the used project dependency on an external element

Such dependencies on external elements are displayed in the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Package depends on an external element

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Error in the Package Dependencies panel

In this situation, a modeling tool can suggest moving the relationship into the parent package of this external element. For example, the package *Items* is a parent of a class *Book*, so the relationship can be moved from the *Items* into the package *Library*:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resolved package dependency on an external element

Some movements can be achieved by clicking **Solve** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='']**Package Dependencies** panel.

###### The used project depends on an external element, but can be exported (with warning)

Even though the used project element has a relationship with an external element, that relationship is contained in an external package:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a “legal” used project dependency on an external element

In this case, the dependency on an external element is displayed in the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### “Legal” used project dependency on an external element in the Model Browser

The package can be exported as the used project because the relationship is contained in an external package.

###### The used project does not depend on an external element

If the used project element has a relationship with an external element it is irrelevant in the context of UML (for instance, the external model uses the used project, but not vice versa) and that relationship is contained in an external model, the package can be exported into an independent used project:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a relationship when the used project does not depend on an external element

##### referenceDependencies by reference

The used project depends on the external elements which have references to the model elements from the used project packages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of a dependency by reference

In this case, the package *Items* cannot be exported to an independent project because this package has reference to the *Library* package.

##### dependenciesDiagram dependencies

The diagram depends on all model elements displayed within it.

If the diagram is contained in the package *Items* and depends on external elements, this package cannot be exported to a separate project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the relationship when a diagram depends on an external element

For more information about the package dependencies on external elements, see .

In this case, if the diagram is not important to the used project, it can be moved from the used project package into any external package by dragging-and-dropping it within the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram is moved from the package *Items* to the package *Library*

**

The package*Items* can now be exported into an independent project.

Teamwork Cloud is an ideal solution for a collaborative work on the same project. For more information about using a modeling tool in the collaborative environment, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation' space='CM'].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Unresolved dependencies' space='']


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005681 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04617: Analyzing dependencies in dependency matrix

- page_id: `249005681`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005681/Analyzing+dependencies+in+dependency+matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:23:11.748+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management > Analyzing Requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

830972946

830972948

830972947

You can create, analyze, and modify requirement relations in the matrix, which provides a compact visual view for analysis. Using a dependency matrix, you can also edit relations between a selected requirement and model element (i.e., add or remove a desired relation).

The Dependency Matrix enables:

- The visualization of more than one element relations from different diagrams.
- The capability to create domain-specific matrices and their templates.
- Ready-to-use matrix templates: traceability of elements and behavior diagrams, traceability of requirements, traceability of requirements and elements refining, satisfying, verifying them, and others.
- Faster creation of traceability links between elements (for example, between requirements and the architecture or requirements and test cases). This improvement saves a huge amount of time in comparison to linking elements in diagrams.

To create a dependency matrix

1. From a requirements specification package shortcut menu in the Containment tree, select **Create****Diagram** and then select one of the following:
  - Derive Requirement Matrix to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify derivation relations between a derived requirement and a source requirement.
  - Refine Requirement Matrix to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify refined relations between a requirement and a model element or a set of elements refining the requirement.
  - Satisfy Requirement Matrix to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify satisfaction relations between requirements and model elements fulfilling the requirements.
  - **Verify Requirement Matrix** to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, or modify verification relations between requirements and named elements that can determine whether the systems fulfill the requirements.
2. Type a diagram name.
3. Select criteria and a scope to be represented in the matrix. You can specify requirements to be represented as rows and class elements to be represented as columns.
4. Click the **Refresh** button. 
The following figure shows how particular requirements satisfy particular model class elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a dependency matrix.

You can also remove redundant or create missing dependencies using a dependency matrix. For detailed instructions on how to use dependency matrixes, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space=''].

830972945

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement matrices' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Using a dependency matrix, you can also edit relations between a selected requirement and model element (i.e., add or remove a desired relation).</p><p>The Dependency Matrix enables:</p><ul><li>The visualization of more than one element relations from different diagrams.</li><li>The capability to create domain-specific matrices and their templates. </li><li>Ready-to-use matrix templates: traceability of elements and behavior diagrams, traceability of requirements, traceability of requirements and elements refining, satisfying, verifying them, and others.</li><li>Faster creation of traceability links between elements (for example, between requirements and the architecture or requirements and test cases). This improvement saves a huge amount of time in comparison to linking elements in diagrams.</li></ul><p>To create a dependency matrix</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From a requirements specification package shortcut menu in the Containment tree, select <strong>Create</strong><strong> Diagram</strong> and then select one of the following:<br /></span></span><ul><li><strong>Derive</strong><strong> Requirement</strong><strong> Matrix</strong> to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify derivation relations between a derived requirement and a source requirement.</li><li><strong>Refine </strong><strong>Requirement </strong><strong>Matrix</strong> to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify refined relations between a requirement and a model element or a set of elements refining the requirement.</li><li><strong>Satisfy Requirement Matrix</strong> to create a Dependency Matrix to ac:macro-id="11eae72e-474b-44bb-a0cd-f3e032497cc0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can specify requirements to be represented as rows and class elements to be represented <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005685 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04618: Analyzing dependencies in relation map

- page_id: `249005685`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005685/Analyzing+dependencies+in+relation+map
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:07.738+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management > Analyzing Requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

831044545

831044548

831044547

A relation map allows visual analysis among multiple levels of abstraction, such as how requirements are satisfied by other model elements. The relation map enables you to show existing relationships, visualize multilevel relationships, observe traceability from requirements to implementation, or any other analysis you need.

The relation map is useful for:

- Making fast analysis and overview of a model.
- Observing traceability from requirements to implementation all the way through different levels of abstraction (such as analysis, design, etc.).
- Discovering the requirements structure.

You are not allowed to edit relations in the relation map diagram.

To create a relation map

1. From a package or requirement shortcut menu in the Containment tree, select **Create Diagram** and then select one of the following:
  - Requirement Containment Map .
  - Requirement Derivation Map .
2. Type a diagram name.
3. Specify a relation criterion, element type, and scope.
4. Click the **Refresh** button. 
The following figure shows how particular requirements satisfy particular model class elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of relation map

For detailed instructions on how to use relation maps, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space=''].

831044543

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005642 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04619: Analyzing Requirements

- page_id: `249005642`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005642/Analyzing+Requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-17T23:11:23.416+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The requirements analysis encompasses those tasks that determine the needs or conditions a new or altered product or project must meet. It considers the possibly conflicting requirements of the various stakeholders by analyzing, documenting, validating and managing software or system requirements. Requirements analysis is critical to the success or failure of a systems or software project.

You can analyze requirements using the following tools:

- Usages and Dependencies . Discovers the Requirements usage and dependencies on other model parts: traceability, change impact analysis.
- Dependency Matrix . Checks the Gap analysis, Change Impact analysis, Requirements completeness and correctness.
- Model validation . Checks Requirements completeness and correctness based on build in or custom validation suites.
- Traceability . Tracks, visualizes, navigates, and analyzes the elements involved in traceability relations.
- Relation Map . Visualizes the relations of multilevel requirements: traceability, coverage analysis.
- Generic and Requirements Tables . Makes the coverage analysis in a compact format.
- Report Wizard . Analysis (e.g. coverage) and estimation (e.g. functional points method) based on built in or custom documentation generation templates.

The following sections describe the procedures for analyzing requirements:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It considers the possibly conflicting requirements of the various stakeholders by analyzing, documenting, validating and managing software or system requirements. Requirements analysis is critical to the success or failure of a systems or software project.</p><p>You can analyze requirements using the following tools:</p><ul><li><strong>Usages and Dependencies</strong>. Discovers the Requirements usage and dependencies on other model parts: traceability, change impact analysis.</li><li><strong>Dependency Matrix</strong>. Checks the Gap analysis, Change Impact analysis, Requirements completeness and correctness.</li><li><strong>Model validation</strong>. Checks Requirements completeness and correctness based on build in or custom validation suites.</li><li><strong>Traceability</strong>. Tracks, visualizes, navigates, and analyzes the elements involved in traceability relations.</li><li><strong>Relation Map</strong>. Visualizes the relations of multilevel requirements: traceability, coverage analysis.</li><li><strong>Generic and Requirements Tables</strong>. Makes the coverage analysis in a compact format.</li><li><strong>Report Wizard</strong>. Analysis (e.g. coverage) and estimation (e.g. functional points method) based on built in or custom documentation generation templates.</li></ul><p>The following sections describe the procedures for analyzing requirements:</p><p class="atl-forced-newline"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="32f67e56-5911-42ce-9969-8b7c7a5e3994" /><br /><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968906 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04620: Analyzing traceability relations

- page_id: `243968906`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968906/Analyzing+traceability+relations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:47.978+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1977687758

1977687761

1977687760

You can perform the impact and/or gap analysis in your project using the Dependency Matrix feature, which is a powerful way for representing traceability relations between multiple elements from different packages, levels of abstraction, views, or other relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, for example, relations through UML tags.You can create your own dependency matrices or even custom dependency matrix types for visualization of various traceability relations. You only need to define a relevant traceability property as **dependency criteria** for this.

The following figure depicts an example of traceability relations between several model elements.

###### Example of traceability relations

These relations can be represented by the following traceability properties:

- Realizing relations (use cases classes):
  - Realizing Class
  - Realizing Element
  - All Realizing Elements
- Specifying relations (classes use cases):
  - Specifying Use Case
  - Specifying Element
  - All Specifying Elements

You can create a dependency matrix to visualize these traceability relations.The following steps will show you how to create a dependency matrix for the Realizing Class predefined property.

To create a dependency matrix for the Realizing Class predefined property:

1. Create a Dependency Matrix diagram.
2. Define row and column type element types as follows:
  1. For the Row Element Type, select UseCase
  2. For the Column Element Type, select Class
3. Define row and column scopes as follows:
  1. For the Row Scope, click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] > Requirements > MagicTest and select Administration .
  2. For the column scope, click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] > Design > MagicTest and select UI .
4. Define the Realizing Class property as Dependency Criteria.
5. Rebuild the matrix (click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_button.png'] in the diagram toolbar). [IMAGE alt='' src='']Defining criteria for visualization of traceability relationships that can be represented by Realizing Class predefined property

The following figure depicts the created Dependency Matrix showing the traceability relationships between use cases and design classes they realize:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visualization of Realizing Class traceability relations on Dependency Matrix

1977687757

**Related Pages**

- Traceability

- 
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules
  - Custom traceability rules
