<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968830 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04621: Analyzing usages and dependencies

- page_id: `243968830`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968830/Analyzing+usages+and+dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:11.957+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

525949422

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

525949425

525960400

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

525949424

No Magic modeling tools provide a way to track and analyze element usages and dependencies in models, understand the relationships between used and dependent elements, and find the diagrams where these elements are represented. You can do all of this using the commands **Used By** and **Depends On**, as described below.

##### Used By command

The **Used By** command allows you to find all the elements that use a selected element (e.g., as property, tag etc.). This also includes usage in diagrams.

#### TIP: Example

Example

In the figure below, the *Computer* Propertyhas its type set to the *WorkStation* Class.**In other words, the *Computer* Property uses the *WorkStation* Class as its type. If you execute the **Used By** command for the *WorkStation* Class, you will get the*Computer* Property as one of the results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window showing that the *WorkStation* Class is used by the *Computer* Property.

Note that containing (owning) other elements is not considered usage. For example, when a Package contains a Class, this does not mean that the Package uses the Class. The Package is only a container of the Class.

##### Depends On command

Use the **Depends On** command to find all elements used by a selected element (e.g., properties, tags etc.).

#### TIP: Example

Example

Let's say you have a model with the content tree shown in the figure below. Execute the **Depends On** command for the*Workplace Infrastructure*Package to see how its content depends on the outer elements (not owned by the Package) of the model. One of the search results shows that the*Workplace Infrastructure*Package depends on the *Power Socket* Class, which is the type of several Ports owned by this package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The content tree of a sample model with the *Workplace Infrastructure*Package having dependencies to the outer elements of the model.

Note that****in this particular situation you must select the **Search recursively** check box in the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog. This allows you to search for dependencies of the Package content rather than the Package itself. Also, select the **Ignore inner interrelations** checkbox to eliminate the dependencies between the elements contained by the package from the search results.

##### Searching for usages and dependencies

You can find usages and dependencies of a particular model element by opening its shortcut menu from the model browser or a diagram.

To search for usages/dependencies of a model element

1. In the model browser or a diagram right-click the element, the usages or dependencies of which you want to find.
2. In the shortcut menu of the element click Related Elements , and select Used By or Depends On (depending on what you want to find).
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog' space='']**Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog , select the search options you want to use.
4. Click OK to start the search.

The search results are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and dependencies search results window' space=''] at the bottom of the modeling tool. The search results window also provides additional information, such as element type and how a particular element is used by/in the element for which you perform the search.

525949420

**Related pages**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model analysis' space='']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d31b9b07-9d1f-4173-9d3b-f9e7eb75b0f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949422</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07d951e4-c5ea-4647-b07e-a2f62aee41a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949425</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17e0fa6d-3b88-4943-8f41-b718cbd6f418"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525960400</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a67174c8-0758-4cb1-9217-9866c29b4a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7cace291-24eb-4e5a-99ec-db11ed9663fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949424</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>No Magic modeling tools provide a way to track and analyze element usages and dependencies in models, understand the relationships between used and dependent elements, and find the diagrams where these elements are represented. You can do all of this using the commands <strong>Used By</strong> and <strong>Depends On</strong>, as described below.</p><h3><br />Used By command</h3><p>The <strong>Used By</strong> command allows you to find all the elements that use a selected element (e.g., as property, tag etc.). This also includes usage in diagrams.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27671e0f-2ee2-4192-9030-d88960cb29e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the figure below, the <em>Computer</em> <span>Property </span>has its type set to the <em>WorkStation</em> Class.<em> </em>In other words, the <em>Computer</em> Property uses the <em>WorkStation</em> Class as its type. If you execute the <strong>Used By</strong> command for the <em>WorkStation</em> Class, you will get <span>the </span><em>Computer</em> Property as one of the results.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_by_sample.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: left;">The Specification window showing that the <em>WorkStation</em> Class is used by the <em>Computer</em> Property.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />Note that containing (owning) other elements is not considered usage. For example, when a Package contains a Class, this does not mean that the Package uses the Class. The Package is only a container of the Class.</p><h3><br />Depends On command</h3><p>Use the <strong>Depends On</strong> command to find all elements used by a selected element (e.g., properties, tags etc.).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c71748e-acd0-4f14-aae5-133bad652a62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Let's say you have a model with the content tree shown in the figure below. Execute the <strong>Depends On</strong> command for <span>the </span><em>Workplace Infrastructure</em><span> Package</span> to see how its content depends on the outer elements <span>(not owned by the Package)</span> of the model. One of the search results shows that <span>the </span><em>Workplace Infrastructure</em><span> Package depends on the <em>Power Socket</em> Class, which is the type of several Ports owned by this package.</span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="depends_on_sample.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: left;"><span>The content tree of a sample model with the <em>Workplace Infrastructure</em><span> Package having dependencies to the outer elements of the model.</span></span></h6><p style="text-align: left;"><span>Note that<strong> </strong>in this particular situation you must select the <strong>Search recursively</strong> check box in the <strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong> dialog. This allows you to search for dependencies of the Package content rather than the Package itself. Also, select the <strong>Ignore inner interrelations</strong> checkbox to eliminate the dependencies between the elements contained by the package from the search results.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><br />Searching for usages and dependencies</h3><p>You can find usages and dependencies of a particular model element by opening its shortcut menu from the model browser or a diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for usages/dependencies of a model element</p><hr /><ol><li>In the model browser or a diagram right-click the element, the usages or dependencies of which you want to find.</li><li>In the shortcut menu of the element click <strong>Related Elements</strong>, and select <strong>Used By</strong> or <strong>Depends On</strong> (depending on what you want to find).</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the search options you want to use.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to start the search.</li></ol><p><br />The search results are listed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Usages and dependencies search results window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[usages/dependencies search results window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> at the bottom of the modeling tool. The search results window also provides additional information, such as element type and how a particular element is used by/in the element for which you perform the search.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07e1ba14-ca1f-4b39-ab88-405f62c9ceeb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949420</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong class="confluence-link">Related pages</strong></p><p><strong class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b1f7fc5f-ff6e-420c-939d-92549e7c25e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model analysis" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567546 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04623: Annotation

- page_id: `249567546`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567546/Annotation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:02.738+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Comment with or without the stereotype XSDannotation. The documentation content maps to the UML Comment body (name).

“documentation” maps as a UML comment, as outlined below:

- the “content” value - the comment name.

- the “xml:lang” value – the tag “xml:lang” value.

- the source value – the tag ”source” value.

“appinfo” maps as a tag value with the name “appInfoSource”:

- the “source” value will be the tag value.
- the “content” will be documentation for the tagged value.

When several annotation nodes appear on one element node, mapping shall be done as follows:

- the “documentation” text shall be merged into one UML comment with merged content, but the “content” and “xml:lang” tag values shall represent only the first matched values.
- “appInfo” shall have “content” merged into one tag “appInfoSource” comment, but the tag value shall represent the “appinfo” matched first.

#### PANEL: annotation XML representation summary

annotation XML representation summary

```text
<annotation
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace …}>
```

```text
Content: (anninfn I dorumpnrarioWl.
```

```text
</annotation>
```

```text
<appinfo
```

```text
source = anyURI>
```

```text
Content: ({any})*
```

```text
</appinfo>
```

```text
<documentation
```

```text
source = anyURI
```

```text
xml:lang = language>
```

```text
Content: ({any})*
```

```text
</documentation>
```

#### PANEL: XML representations of three kinds of annotation

XML representations of three kinds of annotation

```text
<xs:simpleType fn:note="special">
```

```text
<xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:documentation>A type for experts only</xs:documentation>		<xs:appinfo>
```

```text
<fn:specialHandling>checkForPrimes</fn:specialHandling>		</xs:appinfo>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### annotation UML model example.

#### PANEL: annotation XML code sample

annotation XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com">	<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:appinfo source = "infoSource">infoContent</xs:appinfo>		<xs:documentation source = "documentation source" xml:lang = "EN">the documentation for this schema</xs:documentation>	</xs:annotation>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567564 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04624: Any and anyAttribute

- page_id: `249567564`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567564/Any+and+anyAttribute
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:03.071+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDany or XSDanyAttribute.

maxOccurs - to multiplicity upper range. The unbounded value maps to an asterisk in UML.

minOccurs – to multiplicity lower range.

annotation maps to Attribute documentation.

Other properties map to TaggedValues.

#### PANEL: any and anyAttribute XML representation summary

any and anyAttribute XML representation summary

```text
<any
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
maxOccurs = (nonNegativeInteger | unbounded) : 1
```

```text
minOccurs = nonNegativeInteger : 1
```

```text
namespace = ((##any | ##other)| List of (anyURI | (##targetNamespace |
```

```text
##local)) ) : ##any 	processContents = (lax | skip | strict) : strict
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</any>
```

```text
<anyAttribute
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
namespace = ((##any | ##other) | List of (anyURI | (##targetNamespace |
```

```text
##local)) ) : ##any
```

```text
processContents = (lax | skip | strict) : strict
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)</anyAttribute>
```

#### PANEL: XML representations of the four basic types of wildcard

XML representations of the four basic types of wildcard

```text
<xs:any processContents = "skip"/>
```

```text
<xs:any namespace = "##other" processContents="lax"/>
```

```text
<xs:any namespace = "http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"/>
```

```text
<xs:any namespace = "##targetNamespace"/>
```

```text
<xs:anyAttribute namespace = "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"/>
```



```text

```

```text
</xs:annotation>			</xs:any>
```

```text
</xs:choice>
```

```text
</xs:group>
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "attr_group">
```

```text
<xs:anyAttribute id = "anyID" namespace = "http:\bla.bla.bla" processContents = "skip">
```

```text
<xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:documentation>any attribute documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

```text
</xs:anyAttribute>
```

```text
</xs:attributeGroup>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

````html
<p>Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDany or XSDanyAttribute.</p><p>maxOccurs - to multiplicity upper range. xmlns:xs = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</a>&quot; targetNamespace = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:group name = &quot;my_type&quot; &gt;<br />		&lt;xs:choice&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:any id = &quot;anyID&quot; namespace = &quot;<a href="http://bla">http://bla</a>&quot; processContents = &quot;strict&quot; minOccurs = &quot;0&quot; maxOccurs = &quot;1&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:annotation&gt;<br />					&lt;xs:documentation&gt;any documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;<br />			&lt;/xs:any&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:choice&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:group&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;attr_group&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:anyAttribute id = &quot;anyID&quot; namespace = &quot;http:\bla.bla.bla&quot; processContents = &quot;skip&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:documentation&gt;any attribute documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:anyAttribute&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971392 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04625: Appendix A: report extensions

- page_id: `243971392`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971392/Appendix+A+report+extensions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:12.912+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352447286

1352447286

1352447286

Report Wizard has been enhanced with an extension that provides additional functionalities (for example, a Custom Tool that encapsulates Java functions in context). The report extensions are Java class and related resources collected into JAR archives, which are merely ZIP files that contain a specially formatted “manifest” file describing the contents of the archive.

To install the extension, copy the *.jar* file into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. The archives stored within subdirectories will not be loaded in Report Wizard, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure.

The extension deployed under the global extensions directory will affect all templates, which means you can use the deployed Custom Tool in all templates. Extensions deployed under a template package will affect only the specific template.

1352447285

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94c1861f-b6e3-47a9-85ca-41d8a92d7191"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352447286</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8150f4b-896b-40ee-96bb-adb667c7e139"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352447286</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f7e3fa8-287f-4fa1-bbad-575ddcff00a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352447286</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Report Wizard has been enhanced with an extension that provides additional functionalities (for example, a Custom Tool that encapsulates Java functions in context). The report extensions are Java class and related resources collected into JAR archives, which are merely ZIP files that contain a specially formatted “manifest” file describing the contents of the archive.</p><p>To install the extension, copy the <em>.jar</em> file into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. The archives stored within subdirectories will not be loaded in Report Wizard, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure" ac:alt="Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extensions Deployment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Appendix A: report extensions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure.</h6><p>The extension deployed under the global extensions directory will affect all templates, which means you can use the deployed Custom Tool in all templates. Extensions deployed under a template package will affect only the specific template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d608ca77-9395-42b0-8788-59f20f350bfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352447285</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5e747ab4-111f-402e-a497-b2836dc5f39c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971403 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04626: Appendix B: office open XML format template

- page_id: `243971403`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971403/Appendix+B+office+open+XML+format+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:13.512+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1353187275

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1353187276

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1353187276

Office Open XML (also referred to as OOXML or Open XML) refers to the standardized file formats of spreadsheets, charts, presentations, and word processing documents for Microsoft Office. These standardized file formats become free and open as ECMA-376 Office Open XML File Formats - 2nd edition (December 2008) and ISO/IEC 29500:2008.

The most common Open XML file formats that Report Wizard supports include:

- DOCX - for word processing (text) documents
- XLSX - for spreadsheets
- PPTX - for presentations

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Support Office Open XML (DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX) and Microsoft Office 2007 and newer versions.

1353187274

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971449 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04627: Appendix C: OpenDocument format template

- page_id: `243971449`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971449/Appendix+C+OpenDocument+format+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:14.170+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355361738

1355361739

1355361739

The OpenDocument format (*ODF*) is an open file format for office documents, such as spreadsheets, presentations, and word processing documents. The standard was developed by the Open Office *XML* technical committee of the Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards (OASIS) consortium.

The version 1.0 manifestation was published as an ISO/IEC International Standard, ISO/IEC 26300:2006 Open Document Format for Office Applications (OpenDocument) v1.0.

The most common files supported by Report Wizard are:

- ODT for word processing (text) documents
- ODS for spreadsheets
- ODP for presentations

1355361738

**Related pages**

The standard was developed by the Open Office <em>XML</em> technical committee of the Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards (OASIS) consortium.</p><p>The version 1.0 manifestation was published as an ISO/IEC International Standard, ISO/IEC 26300:2006 Open Document Format for Office Applications (OpenDocument) v1.0.</p><p>The most common files supported by Report Wizard are:</p><ul><li><em>ODT</em> <br />for word processing (text) documents</li><li><em>ODS</em> <br />for spreadsheets</li><li><em>ODP</em> <br />for presentations</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="019e42de-51d9-4046-a350-f1f7095ec904"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355361738</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bdc85593-179d-4d8d-8bc5-7696d304540a" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

Report Wizard supports *HTML* code conversion to *RTF, ODF,* and *OOXML* file formats. The *HTML* code is created from the Element Documentation pane, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation Pane.

The element documentation can also be retrieved by the following *VTL* code:

```text

```

Whenever the report engine encounters *HTML* content, it will automatically convert the content into a valid output format style.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971547 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04629: Appendix E: DocBook Support

- page_id: `243971547`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971547/Appendix+E+DocBook+Support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:16.646+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1357340520

1357340520

1357340520

DocBook was built as an application to write technical documents related to computer hardware and software. It is a semantic markup language that was extended from XML language. As an open source application, DocBook has been adopted as a standard to write other types of documentation as well. The current version of DocBook, published by O'Reilly Media and XML Press, is 5.1.

The Report Wizard’s DocBook engine enables you to input the *VTL* codes inside DocBook’s document content. It also allows you to convert HTML into DocBook tags, rearrange tags, and escape special characters. Report Wizard uses “*dbk*” and “*docbook*” file extensions to save DocBook files. The figure below shows the DocBook’s document content.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1357340519

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565287 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04630: Applying Actual Measurements

- page_id: `249565287`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565287/Applying+Actual+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:36.902+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['make-element-measurable', 'set-default-value-for-actual-property-set', 'dv', 'apply-actual-measurement', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actual Measurements can be applied to Measurable Elements and Property Sets. 
 
To make a Measurable Element measurable

1. Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least. For a Property Set, it is sufficient to define measurements as an owned property.
2. Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.
3. Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the Measurement Set property.

You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser onto the element.

As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set, and you can use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the **Default Value** button in the element's Specification window. 
 
To set the default value for the Actual Property Set

1. Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.
2. In the Actual <measurement type name> property group, click the **dv** button to assign the default value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.
- When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Actual Measurements can be applied to Measurable Elements and Property Sets. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To make a Measurable Element measurable</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d59253d8-7cf4-4b58-8f11-d7a60bbcb293"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a Property Set, it is sufficient to define measurements as an owned property.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.</li><li>Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the <strong>Measurement Set </strong>property.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad1bdd46-a069-43f5-9ae1-491dc7d1eb93"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser onto the element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Applying Actual Measurements on Property Set.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set, and you can use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the <strong>Default Value</strong> button in the element's Specification window. </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To set the default value for the Actual Property Set<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the Actual &lt;measurement type name&gt; property group, click the <strong>dv</strong> button to assign the default value.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="208651de-8398-4fae-8b88-43d30065bbd0"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.</li><li>When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563205 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04631: Applying actual project status in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563205`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563205/Applying+actual+project+status+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:19.661+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To apply the actual project status to a new milestone

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the actual project status by following steps of the Project Status Application Wizard .

To edit the actual project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the Project Status Application Wizard .

Specify Project Status

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To apply the actual project status to a new milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Apply Status</strong>.</li><li>Specify the actual project status by following steps of the <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit the actual project status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d13ec7f-596d-4186-95e6-648e27b2c7f7"><ac:rich-text-body>If two or more milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Specify Project Status</strong> and then select the milestone you want to edit the status for.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005583 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04632: Applying automated post-processing scripts after ReqIF file import

- page_id: `249005583`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005583/Applying+automated+post-processing+scripts+after+ReqIF+file+import
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:23:11.496+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management > Importing requirements > Importing ReqIF files
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the ReqIF file import, you can apply an automated post-processing script to your project in order to modify requirements and create custom mapping upon requirements interchange through ReqIF.

In order to see the scripts and execute them, you must be using ReqIF Profile. To set the profile, go to **File** > **Use Project**, then scroll to select *ReqIF Profile.mdzip* and click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To apply the post-processing rules

1. From the Options menu, select Project .
2. In the options group list, select General , then scroll down to the ReqIF options group.
3. Under the ReqIF Import category, find Post-processing rules and select the one you want to apply.
4. Click OK .

| Post-processing Rule | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Fill Empty Requirement Name | When applied to a requirement with an unspecified Name property, the rule picks the data from Element ID and uses it as the element name. |
| Set CustomID as ID | When applied to a requirement with an empty Element ID, the rule sets the specified CustomID as an element ID. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>After the ReqIF file import, you can apply an automated post-processing script to your project in order to modify requirements and create custom mapping upon requirements interchange through ReqIF.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d942ed88-5d32-4e2f-ac22-050f019816a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to see the scripts and execute them, you must be using ReqIF Profile. To set the profile, go to <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Project</strong>, then scroll to select <em>ReqIF Profile.mdzip</em> and click <strong>Finish</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="140" ac:width="581"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reqif_profile_used.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying automated pre-processing scripts before ReqIF file export" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To apply the post-processing rules</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the options group list, select <strong>General</strong>, then scroll down to the <strong>ReqIF</strong> options group.</li><li>Under the<strong> ReqIF Import</strong> category, find <strong>Post-processing rules</strong> and select the one you want to apply.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><table><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: left;">Post-processing Rule</th><th style="text-align: center;">Description</th></tr><tr><td>Fill Empty Requirement Name</td><td>When applied to a requirement with an unspecified <strong>Name</strong> property, the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e6ad2d6-0dac-458f-b39e-ef25257112c2">r</ac:inline-comment-marker>ule picks the data from <strong>Element ID</strong> and uses it as the element name.</td></tr><tr><td>Set CustomID as ID</td><td>When applied to a requirement with an empty <strong>Element ID</strong>, the rule sets the specified <strong>CustomID</strong> as an element ID.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005599 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04633: Applying automated pre-processing scripts before ReqIF file export

- page_id: `249005599`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005599/Applying+automated+pre-processing+scripts+before+ReqIF+file+export
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-17T23:11:21.462+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management > Exporting requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Prior to the ReqIF file export, you can apply an automated pre-processing script to your project in order to modify requirements and create custom mapping upon requirements interchange through ReqIF.

In order to see the scripts and execute them, you must be using ReqIF Profile. To set the profile, go to**File**>**Use Project**, then scroll to select *ReqIF Profile.mdzip* and click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To apply the pre-processing rule

1. From the Options menu, select Project .
2. In the options group list, select General , then scroll down to the ReqIF options group.
3. Under **ReqIF Export**, find **Pre-processing rules** and select the one you want to apply.
4. Click OK .

| Pre-processing Rule | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restore Empty Requirement Name | When applied to a requirement with a specified Name property, the rule removes the element name upon export. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Prior to the ReqIF file export, you can apply an automated pre-processing script to your project in order to modify requirements and create custom mapping upon requirements interchange through ReqIF.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d942ed88-5d32-4e2f-ac22-050f019816a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0459773-8b83-4869-aa7b-f597a39c4b0a">In order to see the scripts and execute them, you must be using ReqIF Profile. To set the profile, go to </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0459773-8b83-4869-aa7b-f597a39c4b0a">File</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0459773-8b83-4869-aa7b-f597a39c4b0a"> &gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0459773-8b83-4869-aa7b-f597a39c4b0a">Use Project</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0459773-8b83-4869-aa7b-f597a39c4b0a">, then scroll to select <em>ReqIF Profile.mdzip</em> and click <strong>Finish</strong>.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="140" ac:width="581"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reqif_profile_used.jpg" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To apply the pre-processing rule</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the options group list, select <strong>General</strong>, then scroll down to the <strong>ReqIF</strong> options group.</li><li><p>Under <strong>ReqIF Export</strong>, find <strong>Pre-processing rules</strong> and select the one you want to apply.</p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><table><tbody><tr><th>Pre-processing Rule</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Restore Empty Requirement Name</td><td><p>When applied to a requirement with a specified <strong>Name</strong> property, the rule removes the element name upon export.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968298 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 04634: Applying image

- page_id: `243968298`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968298/Applying+image
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-10T10:07:27.241+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Ways to apply image on a symbol

###### The Image Library tab

To apply an image on a symbol using the**Image Library** tab

1. From the Window menu, select Image Library. The Image Library tab opens on the right side of the window.
2. Select the image you want to apply from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Image Library tab' space=''] tree.
3. Drag the image directly on the symbol. The image is applied on a symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Image dialog

To apply an image on a symbol using **Select Image** dialog

1. Open the **Select Image** dialog by doing one of the following: Only mentioned elements support the **Image Library** button on the smart manipulator by default. You can customize the smart manipulator of any other element.
  - Open the Specification window of any element, select **Image**(**Icon**for Stereotype) property cell and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Select Class, Actor, Port, Block, Part, Node, Performer, Concept Role, Stereotype, or Text Box ((HTML Text) on the diagram pane and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
2. Select the image you want to apply.
3. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The image is applied on a symbol.

###### The external resources

To apply an image on a symbol from the external resources

1. Select an image from any external resource (e.g. web browser, email). Image formatsYou can apply images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.
2. Drag it directly on a symbol. 
The image is applied on a symbol. If the the image that you applied on a symbol is large, you can resize that symbol to a preferred size automatically.To resize a symbol to a preferred sizea. Select the symbol with an image applied. 
b. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] smart manipulator that appears at the right bottom side of the shape’s horizontal border.

##### Applying image to Property or its type

When applying image to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED']shape (which type is specified) by draggingor selecting an image from the[Image Library](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab) or [other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources), and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting port and part types' space='']**Type Selection Mode** is switched on,the image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default. For example, if you drag an image on the *workplace* Property shape that type is *Workplace* Class, the image will be applied to the Class but not to the Property.

#### WARNING: Exceptions

Exceptions

- If the Property already has the image applied, the new image replaces it and is set to the Property but not to it's type.
- If the Property type already has an image applied, you cannot replace it with the new one by dragging it on Property shape.

To apply image to Property type

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''], switch on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting port and part types' space='']**Type Selection Mode**.
2. Select an image from the [Image Library](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab) or [other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources).
3. Drag selected image on the Property shape which: 
 - Type is specified. 
 - The Property is without image. 
 - The Property type is without image. 
The image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default. You can specify to apply image on Property by changing the **Apply Image To** property value.

#### NOTE: Symbol display mode

Symbol display mode

You can change the symbol display mode. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']>]]>

To specify the **Apply Image To** property value

1. On the main menu, select the **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the project Options dialog, find **Apply Image To** property.
3. Select one of the following value: 
 - **Type and Notify** (default) - to apply image to Property type and after that show notification message on the right corner of the screen. 
 - **Type** - to apply image to Property type. 
 - **Property** - to apply image to Property.
4. Click **OK**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565101 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04635: Applying industrial symbols

- page_id: `249565101`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565101/Applying+industrial+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:32.417+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Industrial symbols can be applied to the nodes, performers, and concept roles in the OV-1 and NOV-1 diagrams.

If you want to use industrial symbols in your model, you can

- create Performer with Image or Concept Role with Image from the toolbar (1)
- click the Image Library item on the element smart manipulator (2)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two ways of using Image Library

Use the **Select Image** dialog to browse the hierarchy of the industrial symbols. The dialog provides the Simple Search. In the search field, type what you want to find in the hierarchy.

Recently used

You can also apply an industrial symbol to any UAF element by using the element’s specification window, image property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Industrial symbols can be applied to the nodes, performers, and concept roles in the OV-1 and NOV-1 diagrams.</p><p>If you want to use industrial symbols in your model, you can</p><ul><li>create <strong>Performer with Image</strong> or <strong>Concept Role with Image</strong> from the toolbar (1)</li><li>click the<strong> Image Library</strong> item on the element smart manipulator (2)</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="industrial_symbols.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Two ways of using Image Library</h6><p>Use the <strong>Select Image</strong> dialog to browse the hierarchy of the industrial symbols. The dialog provides the Simple Search. In the search field, type what you want to find in the hierarchy.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5835c62e-a8be-49d7-9f88-05f425b41c87"><ac:rich-text-body>You can easily find recently used symbols by opening the <strong>Recently used</strong> tab.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />You can also apply an industrial symbol to any UAF element by using the element’s specification window, image property.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967376 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04636: Applying layout template

- page_id: `243967376`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967376/Applying+layout+template
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-20T17:37:33.116+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you want to use the layout template in another diagram (usually the Composite Structure diagram), the following conditions must be met beforehand:

- The Part must be typed by the Class or subtype of Class in the Composite Structure diagram.
- The appearance of the Part type Class or subtype of Class must be defined in the Class diagram.
- That Class diagram must be set as the layout template.

You can apply layout templates directly from the diagram toolbar or shape's shortcut menu by using the **Apply Template** command. This command provides different suggestions in the following situations:

- Apply Template without drop-down arrow appears when only one layout template is available for the selected element.
- Apply Template with drop-down arrow appears when multiple layout templates are available for the selected element. The multiple layout templates are listed according to the following rules:

If you set the default layout template, it is always the first on the list, and other templates are listed alphabetically. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting layout template as default' space=''].If you have not set the default layout template, all layout templates are listed alphabetically.

The procedures below explain how to apply the layout template to:

- 
- Class and all classifiers
- 

###### Applying a layout template to the Part

There are several different ways you can apply a layout template to a Part. Use one of the workflows below depending on your situation.

To apply a layout template to the Part Property displayed in the Composite Structure Diagram

- Do one of the following:
  - Right click the Part Property shape to which you want to apply the layout template and select Display > Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

To apply a layout template when creating a new Part Property in the Composite Structure diagram

- 
  - Drag the Class that is the type of the Part Property you want to create on the diagram pane and do one of the following:
    - Drop the Class.
    - Hold down the mouse button until the tooltip appears (long drag), then release the mouse button and select the Create Property Symbol and Apply Default Template command.

###### Applying a layout template to the Class and all classifiers

You can apply a layout template for the Class and all classifiers that are set as the layout template, but are represented in other Class diagrams. To do this, follow the procedure below.

To apply a layout template to the Class and all classifiers

1. Select the Class shape or another classifier shape that has the layout template defined.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the shape and select Display > Layout Template > **Apply Template**. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

###### Applying a layout template to the diagram frame

When creating the internal structure for the Class that has a layout template defined, you can keep the same layout and style of its Ports on the Composite Structure Diagram frame.

To apply a layout template to the diagram frame

1. Select the diagram frame.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the diagram and select Display > Layout Template > **Apply Template**. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

The example below illustrates how the appearance of Ports defined in*the Air Transfer System* layout template is applied to the Internal Block Diagram of the *Air Transfer System* Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout template applied on the *Air Transfer System* Internal Block Diagram frame.

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable** which you can download here: *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>If you want to use the layout template in another diagram (usually the Composite Structure diagram), the following conditions must be met beforehand:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2cb52f42-38ff-4324-9698-f8587564a8f6">The Part must be typed by the Class or subtype of Class in the Composite Structure diagram.</li><li data-uuid="92c49f43-9d7c-40dc-8311-b102fdef86e8">The appearance of the Part type Class or subtype of Class must be defined in the Class diagram.</li><li data-uuid="207eb294-d349-46b4-b100-c0d33bf32f22">That Class diagram must be set as the layout template.</li></ul><p>You can apply layout templates directly from the diagram toolbar or shape's shortcut menu by using the <strong>Apply Template</strong> command. This command provides different suggestions in the following situations:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e124ad7f-c24c-4546-893d-a329c99a4d6d"><strong>Apply Template</strong> without drop-down arrow appears when only one layout template is available for the selected element.</li><li data-uuid="e645855a-eb07-4036-85ae-3655c039e2fd"><strong>Apply Template</strong> with drop-down arrow appears when multiple layout templates are available for the selected element. The multiple layout templates are listed according to the following rules:</li></ul><p>If you set the default layout template, it is always the first on the list, and other templates are listed alphabetically. <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting layout template as default" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Click to learn how to set the default layout template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>If you have not set the default layout template, all layout templates are listed alphabetically.</p><p>The procedures below explain how to apply the layout template to:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="bd495392-d8e9-4288-b0d9-9cd9a633b780"><ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a layout template to the Part"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f299de60-cdc0-4929-9411-2fac33b258a4"><ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a layout template to the Class and all classifiers"><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Class and all classifiers</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2fd5f4bb-6f92-4aa0-b3f0-e8cdf97ae789"><ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a layout template to the diagram frame"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Diagram frame]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h4><br />Applying a layout template to the Part</h4><p>There are several different ways you can apply a layout template to a Part. Use one of the workflows below depending on your situation.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template to the Part Property displayed in the Composite Structure Diagram</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="3c4bbfc9-018c-49ce-a1b7-2b166881a110">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right click the Part Property shape to which you want to apply the layout template and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_icon.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template when creating a new Part Property in the Composite Structure diagram</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="7d84cc32-08b9-4b92-99d4-04d5353de364"><ul><li>Drag the Class that is the type of the Part Property you want to create on the diagram pane and do one of the following:<ul><li>Drop the Class.</li><li>Hold down the mouse button until the tooltip appears (long drag), then release the mouse button and select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Apply Default Template </strong>command.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><h4>Applying a layout template to the Class and all classifiers</h4><p>You can apply a layout template for the Class and all classifiers that are set as the layout template, but are represented in other Class diagrams. To do this, follow the procedure below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template to the Class and all classifiers</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="cf22a671-0518-4db0-8eeb-ec1a6bbae13c">Select the Class shape or another classifier shape that has the layout template defined.</li><li data-uuid="54033c18-2af0-4406-83b4-fa1530945cca">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right click the shape and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<span><strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</span></li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_icon.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><h4>Applying a layout template to the diagram frame</h4><p>When creating the internal structure for the Class that has a layout template defined, you can keep the same layout and style of its Ports on the Composite Structure Diagram frame.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template to the diagram frame</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="aea90c63-440d-4574-84a3-0ac0e9d6596f">Select the diagram frame.</li><li data-uuid="0b322b5f-059e-4dd6-a2c2-a2c78904cc49">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right click the diagram and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<span><strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</span></li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_icon.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br />The example below illustrates how the appearance of Ports defined in<em> the Air Transfer System</em> layout template is applied to the Internal Block Diagram of the <em>Air Transfer System</em> Block.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_on_diagram_frame.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The layout template applied on the <em>Air Transfer System</em> Internal Block Diagram frame.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figure of this page is <strong>Modem Cable</strong> which you can download here: <span class="confluence-link"><em> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1481817717837&amp;api=v2">Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip</a>.</em></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967204 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04637: Applying Legend Items

- page_id: `243967204`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967204/Applying+Legend+Items
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:50.641+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends > Specifying Legend Items
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Adorned Properties

1283264726

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Adorned Properties

1283264728

1283264725

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Adorned Properties

1283264727

When you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space=''] a Legend in a diagram or table, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Item adornment' space=''] of its Legend Items isappliedto the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements. You can define the elements to which a Legend Item should be applied by specifying one of its properties in the **Apply To** property group. Legend Items can be applied either to**Specific Elements** or to **Elements By Condition**. If you apply a Legend to a table, you can also specify the **Adorned Properties** property, which defines what cells of the relevant table rows should be adorned.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

You can specify either the **Specific Elements**or the **Elements by Condition** property of a Legend Item, but not both.

##### Applying Legend Items to specific elements

You can apply a Legend Item to one or multiple specific elements as described below.

To apply a Legend Item to specific elements

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item you want to apply.
2. Select the property specification cell of the Specific Elements property, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
3. On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the elements to which you want to apply the Legend Item.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_button_long.png'] to add them to the Selected elements area, and click OK .
5. Close the Specification window.

You can also apply one or multiple Legend Items to an element from its symbol.

To apply Legend Items to an element from its symbol

1. In a diagram, right-click the shape of the element to which you want to apply Legend Items, and in the menu, select Legend Item .
2. In the open dialog, select the Legend Items you want to apply, and click the Apply button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example how to apply Legend Items from an element shape.

##### Applying Legend Items to elements by conditionApplying Legend Items to elements by condition

Applying a Legend Item to **Elements by Condition** ensures that it is consistently applied to all the elements that meet the defined condition, even after you change your model.

**Example**

Let's say that you want to apply a Legend Item to all requirements that have a high-risk level. The quickest way to do it is to create a Property Test operation. Follow the steps below to analyze this specific use case and define the **Elements by Condition** property for the Legend Item:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item.
2. Select the specification cell of the **Elements by Condition** property, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. In the **Element by Condition** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Element Type** box.
4. In the Property Test operation specification area of the **Elements by Condition** dialog, select the **risk** property and set its value to **High**. Default optionsBy default, the **Language** is set to **StructuredExpression,** and the Property Test operation is selected in the **Elements by Condition** dialog.
5. Click OK , and close the Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Elements by Condition** dialog with the Property Test operation defined to test if Requirements have a high-risk level.

After following the above steps, the Legend Item is applied to all Requirements that have the **Risk** property set to **high**. If this use case does not meet your needs, you may define advanced Property Test operation properties. You may also[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='']of a different type, or create several operations for a single condition, as long as it returns a Boolean data type value (*true*or*false*). In addition, you can define a condition using a different**Language**setting.

##### Applying Legend Items to specific cells of a table

When a Legend is used in a table, you can specify not only the elements to which specific Legend Items should apply (the **Specific Elements** or **Elements By Condition** properties) but also the desired properties of these elements. In other words, you can adorn specific cells of a table instead of adorning entire table rows. To do this, use the **Adorned Properties** property of a Legend Item.

#### NOTE: Adorned Properties

Adorned Properties

The **Adorned Properties** property of a Legend Item is only effective if you use the Legend in a table.

**Example**

Let's say you have a Requirements Table with three columns: *Name,* *Text,* and *Risk*., and you want to apply two legends in that table: *Risk* and *Status*. When you create or use a Legend in a table, Legend Item adornments are normally applied to enitre table rows. So in this case, you also have to specify certain element properties (table columns) to which Legend Items of each Legend should be applied. To reproduce this spesific use case, follow these steps:

1. Create a Requirement Table with the Name , Text and Risk columns.
2. In the table, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''] to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend Risk .
3. Create and define the Legend Items for the Risk Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them High , Medium , and Low .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Risk property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Specify **Adorned Properties** property by selecting the **Risk** property as its value.
  6. Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.
4. In the same table, create a legend intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend Status .
5. Create and define the Legend Items for the Status Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. Create four Legend Items indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them Closed , Approved , Disapproved and In Progress .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. Specify the Elements by Condition property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Status property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Specify the Adorned Properties property by selecting the Name and Text properties as its values.
  6. Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.

After following the above steps, you have a Requirements Table with the*Status*and*Risk*Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict because they are applied to different columns of the table, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure shows a Requirement Table with the *Risk* and *Status* Legends applied to it. 
The Legend Items of each Legend are applied to different columns of a table.

##### Selecting adorned symbols

You can select the adorned symbols in the open diagram directly from the shortcut menu or the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.

To select adorned symbols in the diagram

Do one of the following:

1. 
  - Right-click the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click Select Adorned Symbols . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_adorned_symbols.png']
  - Select the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_adorned_symbols_button.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_adorned_symbols_from_smart_manipulator.png']

If the diagram is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space=''], the **Select Adorned Symbols** menu item is greyed out (inactive).

582727279

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Item adornment' space='']

the diagram pane and then click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_adorned_symbols_button.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:height="97" ac:width="258"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_adorned_symbols_from_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43fe7115-03e0-463b-9496-f28c6c8d7f46"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the diagram is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filtered by the selected Legend item(s)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the <strong>Select Adorned Symbols</strong> menu item is greyed out (inactive).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a610a415-f395-41b7-b4fa-1ff7dca197c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">582727279</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Items" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Item adornment" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967221 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04638: Applying Legends

- page_id: `243967221`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967221/Applying+Legends
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:51.760+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Keeping Legend adornments

580023290

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Keeping Legend adornments

580023292

580023289

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Keeping Legend adornments

580023291

You can manage the Legends by choosing to either apply, unapply, suspend, or disable them in the diagrams and/or tables of your project. You can also adorn a Legend in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend itself, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Requirement Table displaying only Legend adornments

##### Applying a Legend in an open diagram or table

To apply a Legend in an open diagram or table

Do one of the following:

- Create a Legend in a diagram or table.
- Drag an existing Legend from the model browser to a diagram.
- Display a Legend via the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='Legends_button.png'] button:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the diagram or table toolbar.
  2. In the drop-down menu, select Apply Legends .
  3. Select the Legend(s) you want to display.
  4. Click the OK button.

Completing one of the actions described above displays the Legend in an open diagram or table, and applies its Legend Item adornments to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.

#### NOTE: Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

You can also temporarily suspend or activate a Legend via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button available next to the Legend name in the **Apply Legends**option. This functionality can be useful for demonstration purposes, working with read-only diagrams/tables, or if you want to preview a diagram/table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes. The changes made via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button are only temporary, i.e., they are not saved upon the saving of a project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Unapplying a Legend from an open diagram or table

To unapply a Legend from an open diagram or table

Do one of the following:

- Delete the Legend shape from a diagram.
- Hover the mouse pointer over a legend in a table, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_edit_button.png'] and select Hide .
- Remove a Legend via the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='Legends_button.png'] button:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the diagram or table toolbar.
  2. In the drop-down menu, select Apply Legends .
  3. Clear the check mark of the Legend(s) you want to remove.
  4. Click the OK button.

The actions described above remove a Legend from an open diagram or table, and Legend Item adornments are no longer applied to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.

#### NOTE: Keeping Legend adornments

Keeping Legend adornments

When you delete a Legend shape in a diagram or select the **Hide** command for a Legend in a table, the question message appears, allowing you to keep Legend Item adornments after the Legend is removed.

##### Displaying an applied Legend in an open diagram or table

You can display an applied Legend that was previously removed from a diagram or table.

To display an applied Legend in an open diagram or table

1. In a table or diagram toolbar, click the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] button.
2. In the drop-down menu, select Display Applied Legends .
3. Select the Legend(s) you want to display.
4. Click the Display button.

##### Disabling/enabling Legends

To disable/enable Legends

1. In the main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General options group, select Legends .
3. To disable/enable selected Legends:
  1. For the **Ignored Legends** option, click the three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The **Select Legend** dialog opens.
  2. Click the **Multiple Selection** button and do one of the following:
    1. To disable Legends: on the left side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to disable, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button and click **OK**.
    2. To enable Legends: on the right side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to enable, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_button_3.png'] button and click OK .
4. To disable/enable all Legends:
  1. Set the Use Legends option to true to enable or false to disable the Legends of the project. Click OK .
5. Click OK .

Once the Legend is disabled, even if it is displayed on the diagram, the Legend cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments. Once all the Legends are disabled, even if they are displayed on the diagram, the Legends cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments.

##### display_adornmentsApplying Legends by Condition

You can apply Legend adornments in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend. To achieve this, define the condition that diagrams should meet for the Legend to be applied (the **Diagrams by Condition** property). Legends with the specified **Diagrams by Condition** property are considered Global Legends, as the Legends' adornments are automatically applied to existing and newly created diagrams that meet the defined condition. Such Legends are marked in the **Apply Legends** and **Display Legends** menus with the *(global)* indication:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

Let's say that you want to apply the adornments of a Legend to all Requirement Diagrams and one specific Requirement Table of your model. To do this, follow these steps:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Select the Diagrams by Condition property specification cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
3. In the Diagrams by Condition dialog, select the Condition radio button.
4. In the Property Test operation specification area, select the Type property and enter the Requirement Diagram as its value. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_diagrams_by_condition.png']
5. Click OK .
6. Select the Specific Diagrams property specification cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
7. In the Select Diagram dialog, select the desired Requirement Table.
8. Click OK .

After completing the above steps, the Legend adornments are applied to all Requirement Diagrams and the selected Requirement Table. By specifying the **Diagrams by Condition** property, you also ensure that the Legend adornments will be automatically applied to newly created Requirement Diagrams as well because they meet the defined condition.

580083820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='']
