<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565104 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04639: Applying military symbols

- page_id: `249565104`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565104/Applying+military+symbols
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-15T13:18:04.887+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['applying-military-symbols', 'using-military-symbols', 'mil-std-2525c-symbology', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Military symbols can be applied to resources in t.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

By default, military symbol libraries will be disabled for the UAF projects.

If you will need to use the military symbols:

- When creating a new project select the needed library version.
- In the Project Options dialog, General > UAF options group, change the Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library option value from None to the needed library version.

To apply military symbol for resource shape

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Select Symbol dialog' space='']**Select Symbol**dialog:
  - Create resources from MIL-STD-2525 Symbology toolbar (in MODAF only).
  - Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar (in MODAF only).
  - Open the Specification window of any UAF element, select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] .
2. Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click **OK**. 
The military symbol is applied for the resource shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Military symbols can be applied to resources in t.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-1_military.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9bf41ba-ceda-42d3-b604-37c4050c745e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, military symbol libraries will be disabled for the UAF projects.</p><p>If you will need to use the military symbols:</p><ul><li><span>When creating a new project </span>select the needed library version.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong> options group, change the <strong>Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library</strong> option value from <strong>None</strong> to the needed library version.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply military symbol for resource shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Select Symbol dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Symbol </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:<br /></span></span><ul><li>Create resources from MIL-STD-2525 Symbology toolbar (in MODAF only).</li><li>Select resource and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png" /></ac:image> on the smart manipulator toolbar (in MODAF only).</li><li>Open the <strong>Specification window</strong> of any UAF element, select <strong>Image</strong> property cell and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png" /></ac:image>.</li></ul><span class="confluence-link"> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Select Symbol dialog" ac:alt="Select Symbol dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_symbol_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The military symbol is applied for the resource shape.<br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973002 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04640: Applying OMG SysML Style

- page_id: `243973002`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973002/Applying+OMG+SysML+Style
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:21:24.045+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can apply the visual style of the OMG SysML Specifications (OMG SysML style) to your SysML v1 model.

To apply the OMG SysML style to a SysML v1 project

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, choose the **Symbol styles** on the left and select the **OMG SysML style** on the right. If you do not see the **OMG SysML style**a. Click the **Import** button. The **Open** dialog opens.b. Open the ***<md.install.dir>/templates/SysML directory*** and select **OMG SysML style.stl**.c. Click **Open**.
3. Click the Make Default button.
4. Click OK . Your SysML project uses OMG SysML style as a default style.

To apply OMG SysML style to a SysML v1 diagram

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, choose the **Symbol styles** on the left and select the **OMG SysML style** on the right.
3. Click the **Apply** button.
4. In the **Select Diagrams** dialog, select a SysML diagram(s) and click **OK**.
5. Click **OK**in the **Project Options** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Applying OMG SysML style to existing SysML diagrams might distort the diagrams. Use the automated layout features that optimize diagram layout for viewing.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Open the <strong><em>&lt;md.install.dir&gt;/templates/SysML directory</em></strong> and select <strong>OMG SysML style.stl</strong>.</p><p>c. Click <strong>Open</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click the <strong>Make Default</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />Your SysML project uses OMG SysML style as a default style.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To apply OMG SysML style to a SysML v1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, choose the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> on the left and select the <strong>OMG SysML style</strong> on the right.</p></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Apply</strong> button.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Select Diagrams</strong> dialog, select a SysML diagram(s) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK </strong>in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Project Options dialog" ac:alt="Project Options dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Project Options_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e5ea953-dd75-4fc0-bf36-8b09cac3607e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Applying OMG SysML style to existing SysML diagrams might distort the diagrams. Use the automated layout features that optimize diagram layout for viewing.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967417 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04641: Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams

- page_id: `243967417`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967417/Applying+or+removing+aspect+for+preexisting+diagrams
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:09:39.808+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581426749

581426751

581426750

You can apply custom or predefined aspects for preexisting diagrams. It is possible to apply multiple aspects for one diagram. The aspects can be applied for all diagrams except [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''] diagrams. The procedure below describes how to apply/remove an aspect for a diagram.

To apply/remove an aspect for a diagram

- From the diagram shortcut menu, point to Aspect, and choose an appropriate aspect from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying an aspect for a diagram. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

581426748

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561452 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 04642: Applying Rollup Pattern Blocks

- page_id: `249561452`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561452/Applying+Rollup+Pattern+Blocks
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:40:11.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Rollup Pattern Wizard
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When trying to calculate total mass, cost, power, or another system dimension based on individual values of all the parts in the model,you need to apply a pattern Block to any number of[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED']by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space=''].It automatically applies pattern Blocks (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='']) recursively, and creates property values.The procedure below describes how to apply the rollup pattern Block.

To apply the rollup pattern Block

1. Right-click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] to which you want to apply the rollup pattern Block.
2. S elect **Tools** > **Apply Rollup Pattern**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']**, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the pattern Block you want to apply. Rollup pattern BlocksYou can select: 
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='']: CostRollupPattern, MassRollupPattern, and PowerRollupPattern. They are created to calculate the total cost, mass, and power of the model elements. 
- Your own created rollup pattern Blocks. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Rollup Pattern Block' space='']]]>
4. Specify options: 
- **Apply Recursively**to apply the pattern Block recursively. 
- **Set Role Name** to set the role names of Part Properties. 
- **Set Subsetted Properties** to set the Subsetted Properties of the Part Properties. 
- **Create Value Properties and Redefine** to create and redefine Value Properties.
5. Click **OK**. 
The pattern Block is applied to the model elements according to selected options. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']

**Webinar**

- Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures of this page is the **Laptop Cost Analysis** that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA']. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969587 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04643: Applying validationRule stereotype

- page_id: `243969587`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969587/Applying+validationRule+stereotype
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:33.047+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

After the Constraint element is created, you must apply:

- «validationRule» stereotype. It is derived from the «invariant » stereotype. It expands the Constraint properties with the ability to specify the following information: severity level (for sorting/filtering), abbreviation string (a short string, for easy recognition) and error message (complete description of error explanation). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the validation rule properties' space='']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2822077-680f-4896-8bb2-d2630465439e"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After the Constraint element is created, you must apply:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">«validationRule» stereotype. <span class="auto-cursor-target">It is derived from the «invariant » stereotype.</span> It expands the Constraint properties with the ability to specify the following information: severity level (for sorting/filtering), abbreviation string (a short string, for easy recognition) and error message (complete description of error explanation). <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying the validation rule properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to specify the validation rules properties >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="776a9247-2826-463c-bff6-db72c9e8b9ba"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966399 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04644: Architectural views

- page_id: `243966399`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966399/Architectural+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:58.183+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML defines 13 diagrams that describe 4+1 architectural views:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Several kinds of diagrams provide a visual notation for the concepts in each view.

##### Use Case view

The use case view represents the functionality and behavior of a system or subsystem as it is perceived by external users. This view is targeted mainly at customers, designers, developers, and testers.

The use case view usually is presented as a number of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MED'] in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']. Occasionally it is used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''].

The use case view is central because the contents drive the development of the other views. It is also used for project planning. Every single use case unit is deemed as a manageable unit during the project execution.

##### Structural view

The structural view represents structural elements for implementing a solution for defined requirements. It identifies all of the business entities and how these entities are related to each other. Usually entities are represented as classifiers and their instances in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object diagram' space=''] in multiple abstraction levels. System decomposition to different layers can be displayed using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='']. A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] can be used to represent the classifier inner structure. The system structural view artifacts are created by software architects and represent the system implementation design solutions.

##### Behavioral view

The dynamic behavior of the system is displayed on the Interaction ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='']), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time diagram' space=''] diagrams. It focuses mainly on the interactions that occur between objects inside a system, activities and work performed by the various parts of a system, and state changes within a particular object or collaboration. Rather than defining the participants of the system, it defines how particular use cases are executed, which provides value for the external user. The dynamic view is concerned about what is happening inside the system and how those actions impact other participants.

##### Implementation view

The implementation view describes the implementation artifacts of logical subsystems defined in the structural view. It can include the intermediate artifacts used in a system construction (code files, libraries, data files, etc.) This view defines dependencies between the implementation components and their connections by the required and provided interfaces. Components and their relationships are displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='']. Inner parts of the component can be represented with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='']. The implementation view helps analyze system parts and their dependencies in a higher component level.

##### Environment view

The environment view represents the physical arrangement of a system, such as computers and devices (nodes) and how they are connected to each other. In contrast to the component view, the deployment view is concerned with the physical structure of the system and the location of the software modules (components) manifested by artifacts within the system.

The environment view is displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>UML defines 13 diagrams that describe 4+1 <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75fcd8db-4b0f-4bc2-97dc-c72c7c2eb7fc">architectural</ac:inline-comment-marker> views:</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Architectural views" ac:alt="Architectural views"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ArchitecturalViews.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Several kinds of diagrams provide a visual notation for the concepts in each view.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Use Case view</h3><p>The use case view represents the functionality and behavior of a system or subsystem as it is perceived by external users. This view is targeted mainly at customers, designers, developers, and testers. </p><p>The use case view usually is presented as a number of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[actors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Case diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Occasionally it is used in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p>The use case view is central because the contents drive the development of the other views. It is also used for project planning. Every single use case unit is deemed as a manageable unit during the project execution. </p><h3>Structural view</h3><p>The structural view represents structural elements for implementing a solution for defined requirements. It identifies all of the business entities and how these entities are related to each other. Usually entities are represented as classifiers and their instances in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Object diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Object diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in multiple abstraction levels. System decomposition to different layers can be displayed using <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can be used to represent the classifier inner structure. The system structural view artifacts are created by software architects and represent the system implementation design solutions.</p><h3>Behavioral view</h3><p>The dynamic behavior of the system is displayed on the Interaction (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Communication diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Communication]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[State]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction Overview]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Time diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Time]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams. It focuses mainly on the interactions that occur between objects inside a system, activities and work performed by the various parts of a system, and state changes within a particular object or collaboration. Rather than defining the participants of the system, it defines how particular use cases are executed, which provides value for the external user. The dynamic view is concerned about what is happening inside the system and how those actions impact other participants.</p><h3>Implementation view</h3><p>The implementation view describes the implementation artifacts of logical subsystems defined in the structural view. It can include the intermediate artifacts used in a system construction (code files, libraries, data files, etc.) This view defines dependencies between the implementation components and their connections by the required and provided interfaces. Components and their relationships are displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Component diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Inner parts of the component can be represented with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The implementation view helps analyze system parts and their dependencies in a higher component level.</p><h3>Environment view</h3><p>The environment view represents the physical arrangement of a system, such as computers and devices (nodes) and how they are connected to each other. In contrast to the component view, the deployment view is concerned with the physical structure of the system and the location of the software modules (components) manifested by artifacts within the system. </p><p>The environment view is displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" /></ac:link>.</p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Architecture Management** (Am) domain captures meta-data relevant to the entire architecture. It provides information pertinent to the entire architecture and presents supporting information rather than architectural models.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Architecture Management</strong> (Am) domain captures meta-data relevant to the entire architecture. It provides information pertinent to the entire architecture and presents supporting information rather than architectural models.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563838 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04648: Architecture Management Information

- page_id: `249563838`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563838/Architecture+Management+Information
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:24.879+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Architecture Management
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832800706

832800708

832800707

**Description**

The **Architecture Management Information**(Am-If) view contains definitions of terms used in the given architecture. It consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons).

**Implementation**

An Architecture Management Information****(Am-If) viewpoint is represented by:

- Dictionary table . It contains the definitions of terms used in the given architecture. The table also consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons). This table provides a central reference for a given architecture’s data and metadata. It enables the set of architecture descriptions to stand alone, with minimal reference to outside resources.
- Reports : - Dictionary (.docx). - Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx). - Dictionary Tabular (.docx).

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

832800704

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Definition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alias' space='MED']

It consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>An Architecture Management Information<strong> </strong>(Am-If) viewpoint is represented by:</p><ul><li><strong>Dictionary table</strong>. It contains the definitions of terms used in the given architecture. The table also consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons). This table provides a central reference for a given architecture’s data and metadata. It enables the set of architecture descriptions to stand alone, with minimal reference to outside resources.</li><li><strong>Reports</strong>:<br />- Dictionary (.docx).<br />- Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx).<br />- Dictionary Tabular (.docx).</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:title="Dictionary" ac:alt="Dictionary"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dictionary.png" /></ac:image><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70a992ae-d97a-449a-bf46-820311b74ab9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">832800704</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Definition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Alias" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Same As" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563790 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04657: Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint

- page_id: `249563790`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563790/Architecture+Meta-Data+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:04.848+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0
- labels: ['architecture-meta-data-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

831912465

831912467

831912466

The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint provides information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

831912464

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970864 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04658: Architecture templates

- page_id: `243970864`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970864/Architecture+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:00.834+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are five architecture report templates that come with the report engine:

#### Behavioral Report

A Behavioral report is a standard report for the Behavioral view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Sequence, Communication, State Machine, and Activity diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of the specification of messages in the Sequence diagram, the specification of lifelines in the Communication diagram, the specification of states in the State Machine diagram, and the actions in the Activity diagram. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

#### Environment Report

An Environment report is a standard report for the Environment view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Deployment diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of nodes, interfaces, components, and artifacts which are ordered by the element’s name.

#### Implementation Report

An Implementation report is a standard report for the Implementation view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Component diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of a list of interfaces, components, and artifacts. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

#### Structural Report

A Structural report is a standard report for the Structural view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of a Class diagram. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of interfaces, classes, and enumerations which are ordered by the element’s name.

#### Use Case Report

A Use Case report is a standard report for the Use Case view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Use Case diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of actors and use case. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are five architecture report templates that come with the report engine:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9e3af78-c96a-412c-9208-449c0d7e292d" /></p><h2>Behavioral Report</h2><p>A Behavioral report is a standard report for the Behavioral view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Sequence, Communication, State Machine, and Activity diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of the specification of messages in the Sequence diagram, the specification of lifelines in the Communication diagram, the specification of states in the State Machine diagram, and the actions in the Activity diagram. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p><h2>Environment Report</h2><p>An Environment report is a standard report for the Environment view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Deployment diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of nodes, interfaces, components, and artifacts which are ordered by the element’s name.</p><h2>Implementation Report</h2><p>An Implementation report is a standard report for the Implementation view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Component diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of a list of interfaces, components, and artifacts. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p><h2>Structural Report</h2><p>A Structural report is a standard report for the Structural view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of a Class diagram. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of interfaces, classes, and enumerations which are ordered by the element’s name.</p><h2>Use Case Report</h2><p>A Use Case report is a standard report for the Use Case view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Use Case diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of actors and use case. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970811 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04659: Array tool

- page_id: `243970811`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970811/Array+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:59.209+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050323630

2050323641

2050323628

**On this page**

43

2050323631

Use $array to create an Array or a HashSet instance.

###### **$array.createArray()**

Create an empty ArrayList.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An instance of ArrayList. |

****

###### **$array.createArray(collection)**

Construct an ArrayList containing elements of the specified collection, in the order they are returned by the collection's iterator. Return a zero size ArrayList if the given collection is null.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection of instances whose elements are to be placed into a list. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An ArrayList containing elements of the specified collection. |

****

###### **$array.subList(list, size)**

Create an ArrayList of a portion of the list in the given size.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | java.util.List | An original list. |
|  | size | int | A view size of a given list. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An ArrayList of a specified view size within a given list. |

****

###### **$array.addCollection(parent, child)**

Add a child list into a parent collection. It helps the template handle the case where the child is null.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | parent | java.util.Collection | A parent collection. |
|  | child | java.util.Collection | A child collection. |
| Return | - | void |  |

****

###### **$array.createHashSet()**

Create a HashSet instance.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.Set | An instance of HashSet. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 1

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Artifacts provide modelers with the capability to show additional information about a process.

This information is not directly related to a sequence or message flow of the process.

Types of Artifacts are as follows:

## PAGE 04661: Assessment

- page_id: `249566892`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566892/Assessment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:21.335+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Supportive Diagrams Concepts > Business Motivation Diagram > Assessment Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1986176031

1986176034

1986176033

**Description**

An Assessment is a judgment of some Influencer that affects the ability of organization to employ its Means or achieve its Ends. In other words, an Assessment expresses a logical connection or fact type between Influencers and the Ends and/or Means of the business plans. In this way, an Assessment indicates which Influencers are relevant to which Ends and/or Means.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1986176029

**Related elements**

- Assessment Concepts
- Assessment Concept Relationships

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd69a8ca-6700-4d37-bbb9-ec44bde3fda4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176031</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecf05752-f415-44cf-a7ea-076c69488801"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176034</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e408bb4-28d4-4d4a-822f-274a72367f41"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176033</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>An Assessment is a judgment of some Influencer that affects the ability of organization to employ its Means or achieve its Ends. In other words, an Assessment expresses a logical connection or fact type between Influencers and the Ends and/or Means of the business plans. In this way, an Assessment indicates which Influencers are relevant to which Ends and/or Means.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assesment.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d8dde51-c042-4a2e-8c00-d3ed72db966b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176029</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Assessment+Concepts">Assessment Concepts</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Assessment+Concept+Relationships">Assessment Concept Relationships</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Business+Motivation+Diagram">Business Motivation Diagram</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566901 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04662: Assessment Concept Relationships

- page_id: `249566901`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566901/Assessment+Concept+Relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:22.057+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Supportive Diagrams Concepts > Business Motivation Diagram > Assessment Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1986268114

1986268116

1986268115

**Description**

The following table provides the relationships that exist between element types and their meaning.

| Relationship | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Identifies | This link connects to a or . Meaning that the Assessment points out some possible Potential Reward or Risk that is/are significant to that Assessment. Each possible consequence serves as an appraisal of the worth, value, or quality of some aspect of the Assessment in specific terms, types, or dimensions. |  |
| ProvidesImpetus | This link connects to a or . Meaning that the Assessment is the driving force for the Business Policy or Business Rule. |  |
| AffectsAchievements | This link connects to a , , or . Meaning that the Assessment has an impact on the End outcomes. |  |
| AffectsEmployment | This link connects to a , , , or . Meaning that the Assessment has an impact on the use of Means. |  |
| JudgementFor | This link connects to a Influencer ( or ). Meaning that the Assessment is the Influencer’s judgment that influences the organization’s ability to carry out its Means or achieve its Ends. |  |

1986268113

**Related element**

- Assessment Concepts

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

There are three types of Assessment elements:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973836 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04664: Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro

- page_id: `243973836`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973836/Assigning+a+keyboard+shortcut+to+a+Macro
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T19:18:21.815+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Macro engine > Macro keyboard shortcuts
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new keyboard shortcut to a macro

1. Open either the (i) Environment Options or (ii) Macro Information dialog.
2. Type a keyboard shortcut in the [ATTACHMENT filename='press-new-shortcut-key.png'] box.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to assign the keyboard shortcut to a macro.
4. Click OK .

If a keyboard shortcut key entered in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box has already been assigned to another macro, the following message will appear under the **Press new shortcut key**box: **Currently assigned to <the command name>**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To add a new keyboard shortcut to a macro</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open either the (i) <strong>Environment Options</strong> or (ii) <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog.</li><li>Type a keyboard shortcut in the<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png" /></ac:image> box.</li><li>Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png" /></ac:image></strong> to assign the keyboard shortcut to a macro.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8dec5360-bd93-40ea-87e2-08069e3accb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a keyboard shortcut key entered in the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png" /></ac:image> box has already been assigned to another macro, the following message will appear under the <strong>Press new shortcut key </strong>box: <strong>Currently assigned to &lt;the command name&gt;</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563375 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04666: Assigning resources in NSV-8 chart

- page_id: `249563375`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563375/Assigning+resources+in+NSV-8+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:24.481+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-8 System Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before assigning resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the resource does not have milestones specified, you will not be able to see it in the NSV-8 chart.

To assign a resource

1. Right-click the Version of Configuration and from the shortcut menu, select Resources . The Select Resources dialog opens.
2. Select the desired resource and click OK .

- You can assign a resource in a simple way. Just select a resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration.
- Double-click the resource bar to see internal diagram of the resource.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before assigning resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the resource does not have milestones specified, you will not be able to see it in the NSV-8 chart.</p><p><br /></p><p>To assign a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Version of Configuration and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Resources</strong>. The <strong>Select Resources</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the desired resource and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b87682b7-f108-4928-a253-2b77f879af40"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can assign a resource in a simple way. Just select a resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration.</li><li>Double-click the resource bar to see internal diagram of the resource.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965812 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04667: Assigning shortcut keys

- page_id: `243965812`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965812/Assigning+shortcut+keys
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:46.565+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686262

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686264

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686263

#### EXCERPT: Restoring shortcut keys

INLINE

Most commonly used commands in a modeling tool have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Most common shortcut keys' space=''], allowing you to access the commands quicker. However, you can assign custom shortcut keys to modeling tool commands and change or remove already existing shortcut keys.

To assign a new shortcut key to a command

1. From the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. From the option group list on the left side of the dialog, select Keyboard .
3. Search for the command you need to assign a shortcut key to. You can also search by the shortcut keys.
4. In the Press new shortcut key box, press the shortcut you want to assign. Do not type the shortcut, but press the keys as you would when executing the command.
5. Click the Assign button. The assigned shortcut appears in the Current keys box.
6. Click OK .

The shortcut key is then assigned to the selected command and you can start using it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Assigning a shortcut key.

#### TIP: Usage information

Usage information

- You can assign multiple shortcut keys to one command.
- If you enter a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, a notification appears under the Press new shortcut key box.
- If you assign a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, the shortcut key is automatically unassigned from that command and assigned to the new one.

##### Removing shortcut keys

You can remove both default and custom shortcut keys.

To remove a shortcut key

1. From the main menu of the modeling tool, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. From the option group list on the left side of the dialog, select Keyboard .
3. Search for the command you need to assign a shortcut key to. You can also search by the shortcut keys.
4. In the Current keys box, select the shortcut key you want to remove.
5. Click the Remove button. To remove all shortcut keys assigned to the selected command, click the Remove All button.
6. Click OK .

#### NOTE: Restoring shortcut keys

Restoring shortcut keys

To restore all shortcut keys to their default values, open the **Environment Options** dialog, select **Keyboard**, and click **Reset to Defaults**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39e42f79-fce5-4994-bd45-35b4557aa30f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1192686262</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2175f79-eaa6-4f40-be74-bfa5df01eb5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1192686264</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea4738b-2a67-47bb-8a2f-a2a101880d6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1192686263</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49f2a791-7fa0-4587-88b5-dc7e175149e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Most commonly used commands in a modeling tool have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Most common shortcut keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shortcut keys]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, allowing you to access the commands quicker. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968222 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 04669: Assigning value specification as property value

- page_id: `243968222`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968222/Assigning+value+specification+as+property+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:53.536+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Cases of advanced editing
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some properties whose values can be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED'].

Examples of properties whose values are value specifications: *Default Value*, *Value*.

To assign a value specification

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Show Shortcut Menu button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. From the shortcut menu, select **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED']**.
4. Select a value specification from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change an assigned value specification

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Show Shortcut Menu button (see the highlighted button in the preceding figure).
3. From the shortcut menu, select **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED']**> **Delete**<value specification>.
4. Assign a new value specification.

## PAGE 04670: Association

- page_id: `249566460`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566460/Association
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:04.812+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > Common BPMN Elements > Artifacts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

148680820

148680821

148680821

**Description**

An Association link shows that particular classes are related to each other. Association links may have names defined – names help to read diagrams. You can also specify multiplicity at the end of an association.

**Example**

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

148680819

**Related element**

- Class

**Related diagram**

- Business Data Diagram

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966026 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04671: Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files

- page_id: `243966026`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966026/Attaching+reattaching+and+detaching+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:26.448+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Projects with file attachments
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

304082935

304082937

304093561

**On this page**

304082936

When a file is attached to a project, such file becomes a model element called Attached File.

Attached Files are displayed in the Model Browser. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Attaching files

You can attach files in one of the following ways:

44

###### Dragging the file icon to the diagram pane

To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the diagram pane

1. In the file system, select the icon of the file you need to attach.
2. Drag it to the diagram pane.

The shape of the newly created Attached File element appears on the diagram pane. The diagram owner becomes the owner of the file as well.

###### Dragging the file icon to the Containment tree

To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the Containment tree

1. Drag the file icon onto an element in the Containment tree .
2. In the shortcut menu, select **Create****Attached File**.

A new Attached File element appears in the Containment tree. The element on which you dragged the file icon becomes the owner of the file.

###### Using a command from the shortcut menu of the element selected in the Containment tree

To attach a file by using a command from the shortcut menu of an element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a possible owner for the file to be attached to.
2. In the shortcut menu, s elect Create Element > Attached File .
3. In the Select File to Attach dialog, select the file and click Attach .

A new Attached File element with its name in the edit mode appears in the Containment tree. If you do not need to change the file name, press Enter. Otherwise, type a new name and then press Enter.

Regardless of the way used to create an Attached File element, it has the same default properties, such as **Attached****At**, **Modified****At**, **Size**, **Author**, and **File**. You can see these properties, their descriptions, and values in the **Specification** window of the Attached File element. If you need to replace the attached file, you can do this using the **File** property value editor.

##### Reattaching files

To reattach the file

1. In the Containment tree, select the attached file you need to replace.
2. Press Enter to open its **Specification** window.
3. In the**Quick Filter** box, type *file*.
4. Click the cell of the **File** property value, then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
5. In the **Select File to Attach** dialog, select the new file to attach, and click **Attach**.
6. In the **Specification** window, click **Close**.

The file is reattached, and the **Modified At** property value of the appropriate Attached File element is updated. 
 
If you no longer need the attached file, you can simply remove it from the model just like any other element. Select it in the Containment tree and press Delete. If you have its shape on the diagram, select the shape and press Ctrl+D.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff40ca63-6ef0-4d35-88a8-9adbca174c0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304082935</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df3dd097-79eb-4eae-b26e-48be5bff6eb1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304082937</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13aeb866-a4b8-48e2-9c00-06c3b7af5fc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304093561</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ddd5f86-9958-4b37-91f3-04cc7d5491e8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="858560f3-9563-4d2e-ac70-2f154303f2de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304082936</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a file is attached to a project, such file becomes a model element called Attached File.</p><p>Attached Files are displayed in the Model Browser.<br /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:title="Attached file in Containment tree" ac:alt="Attached file in Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="attached_file_in_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Attaching files</h3><p>You can attach files in one of the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbdd2802-e48d-48ec-9e88-664d55b7a3b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4>Dragging the file icon to the diagram pane</h4><p>To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the file system, select the icon of the file you need to attach.</li><li>Drag it to the diagram pane.</li></ol><p>The shape of the newly created Attached File element appears on the diagram pane. Select it in the Containment tree and press Delete. If you have its shape on the diagram, select the shape and press Ctrl+D.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567433 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04672: Attribute

- page_id: `249567433`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567433/Attribute
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:00.173+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

- XML schema attribute maps to UML Attribute with stereotype XSDattribute.
- default maps to initial UML Attribute or AssociationEnd value.
- annotation – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd documentation.
- name – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd name.
- type or content simpleType – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd type.

#### PANEL: Other attributes or elements maps to corresponding tagged values

Other attributes or elements maps to corresponding tagged values

```text
<attribute
```

```text
default = string
```

```text
fixed = string
```

```text
form = (qualified | unqualified)
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
name = NCName
```

```text
ref = QName
```

```text
type = QName
```

```text
use = (optional | prohibited | required ) optional
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace ...} >
```

```text
Content: (annotation?,(simpleType?))</attribute>
```

Example

```text
]]>
```

Ref value is generated from ref or refString TaggedValue. One of ref or name must be present, but not both.

If ref is present, then all of <simpleType>, form and type must be absent. Type and <simpleType> must not both be present.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Example of attribute UML model (1).

<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "[http://nomagic.com](http://nomagic.com)" xmlns:xs = ["http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema) targetNamespace =["http://nomagic.com"](http://nomagic.com/)

#### PANEL: Example of attribute UML code

Example of attribute UML code

```text
<xs:attribute name = "name" type = "xs:string" default = "minde"
```

```text
fixed = "fixed_value" form = "qualified" use "optional" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >name attribute documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

```text
</xs:attribute>
```

```text

```

```text
"qualified" use = "optional" >  		<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >surname attribute
```

```text
documentation‹/xs:documentation>		</xs:annotation> 		<xs:simpleType >
```

```text
<xs:restriction base = "xs:string" />
```

```text
‹/xs:simpleType> 	</xs:attribute>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "surname" type    "xs:string" />
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "attr_group" >
```

```text

```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >reference
```

```text
documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

```text
</xs:attribute>
```

```text
<xs:attribute ref = "nm:surname" />
```

```text
</xs:attributeGroup> </xs:schema>
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567454 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04673: AttributeGroup

- page_id: `249567454`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567454/AttributeGroup
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:00.465+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Attribute Group maps to a simple UML Class with the stereotype XSDattributeGroup.

- name – to UML Class name
- annotation – to UML Class documentation
- attribute – to inner UML Attribute or AssociationEnd with XSDattribute
- stereotype.
- attributeGroup - the inner attributeGroup must only be reference. This reference maps to Attribute or AssociationEnd with the type of referenced attributeGroup. The opposite Association End kind must be aggregated and it must be navigable.
- anyAttribute – to inner UML Attribute with stereotype XSDanyAttribute .

If the reference is generated, a name is not generated.

When an **<attributeGroup>**appears as a daughter of **<schema> or <redefine>,**it corresponds to an attribute group definition, shown below. When it appears as a daughter of **<complexType> or <attributeGroup>,**it does not correspond to any component as such.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### attributeGroup UML model example.

#### PANEL: attributeGroup UML model example with associated XML

attributeGroup UML model example with associated XML

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com"
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "global_attr_group">
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "address" type = "xs:string" />	</xs:attributeGroup>
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "attr_group_name" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:documentation>attribute group documentation</xs:documentation>		</xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "surname" type = "xs:string"/>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "name" type = "xs:string" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >name attribute documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>		</xs:attribute>
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup ref = "nm:global_attr_group2" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >reference documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>		</xs:attributeGroup>		<xs:anyAttribute />
```

```text
</xs:attributeGroup>
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "global_attr_group2" >		<xs:attribute name = "city" type = "xs:string"/>	</xs:attributeGroup>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Attribute Group maps to a simple UML Class with the stereotype XSDattributeGroup.</p><ul><li>name – to UML Class name</li><li>annotation – to UML Class documentation</li><li>attribute – to inner UML Attribute or AssociationEnd with XSDattribute</li><li>stereotype.</li><li>attributeGroup - the inner attributeGroup must only be reference. This reference maps to Attribute or AssociationEnd with the type of referenced attributeGroup. The opposite Association End kind must be aggregated and it must be navigable.</li><li>anyAttribute – to inner UML Attribute with stereotype <em>XSDanyAttribute</em>.</li></ul><p>If the reference is generated, a name is not generated.</p><p>When an <strong><u>&lt;attributeGroup&gt;</u> </strong>appears as a daughter of <strong> <u>&lt;schema&gt;</u> or <u>&lt;redefine&gt;,</u> </strong>it corresponds to an attribute group definition, shown below. When it appears as a daughter of <strong><u>&lt;complexType&gt;</u>  or <u>&lt;attributeGroup&gt;,</u> </strong>it does not correspond to any component  as such.</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 61 attributeGroup UML Model Example.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">attributeGroup UML model example.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b1f1691-7cda-4088-acd4-5f288d2da77f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">attributeGroup UML model example with associated XML</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;xs:schema xmlns:nm = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.comn">http://nomagic.com&quot;</a> xmlns:xs = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLScheman">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema&quot;</a> targetNamespace = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;global_attr_group&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;address&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot; /&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;attr_group_name&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:documentation&gt;attribute group documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;surname&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;name&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:documentation &gt;name attribute documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:attribute&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attributeGroup ref = &quot;nm:global_attr_group2&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:documentation &gt;reference documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;<br />		&lt;xs:anyAttribute /&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;global_attr_group2&quot; &gt;<br />		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;city&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot;/&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966230 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04674: Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `243966230`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966230/Authentication+with+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:44:08.920+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

The **3D**EXPERIENCE menu groups all available integrations with the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='IL'] supporting the integration with the platform, you can access the related functionalities in the **3D**EXPERIENCE menu.

For some functionalities to work, you need to log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform as described below. Note that you can log in to the platform deployed either on-premises or on the cloud server.

You can manage all secure connections, including self-signed certificates, using the [Server Certificates](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Server+Certificates) tool.

To log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform from the modeling tool

1. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Login .
2. In the Server box of the open dialog, enter the server address. If the 3D EXPERIENCE platform is deployed on the cloud, select the The 3DEXPERIENCE platform is hosted on the cloud check box. [ATTACHMENT filename='login_dialog.png']
3. Click OK .
4. In the respective boxes of the open dialog, enter your 3D EXPERIENCE username and password. [ATTACHMENT filename='log_in_to_platform.png']
5. Click Log in .

Once you log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can use all the functionalities of the installed plugins without canceling the session.

3D

##### Switching credentials

Once you log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can switch to a different platform, the platform's collaborative space, or a different access role without terminating your session.

To switch to different **3D**EXPERIENCE platform credentials

1. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Switch Credentials .
2. In the **Credentials**drop-down list box of the open dialog, select the credentials you want to switch to and click**OK**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Credentials** drop-down list box displays only the platforms, collaborative spaces, and roles that you have access to. They are displayed in the following format: Platform • Collaborative Space• Access Role.

##### Logging out

When you finish working on a project in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can terminate your session by logging out of the platform.

To log out of the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

- In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Logout .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971401 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04675: Auto importing template tool

- page_id: `243971401`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971401/Auto+importing+template+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:13.471+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard can automatically import a Template Tool to a template without having to declare the #import statement in a template code manually (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing custom tool to template' space='']*).

To enable Report Wizard to automatically import a Template Tool, you have to use the existing template variable inside “*template.xml*” and “*report.xml*”.

The current template variable syntax:

```text
Variable Name
  Variable Description
  
  Variable Value
]]>
```

Add "com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool" to a type value where the name is a variable name and the value is a full-qualified template tool class, for example:

To automatically import TextTool

1. Edit the built-in.xml file in the ...\data\reports\Tutorial 24 - Text Tool\reports .
2. Add a variable as shown in the following example.

```text
text
  A text tool
  com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool
  com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool
]]>
```

The result is a template code that can access com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool from $text.

| $text.noLineBreak($diagram.name) |
| --- |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once you have edited a template variable, you have to restart the application prior to generating a report through the modeling tool.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard can automatically import a Template Tool to a template without having to declare the #import statement in a template code manually (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing custom tool to template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing Custom Tool to Template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>).</p><p>To enable Report Wizard to automatically import a Template Tool, you have to use the existing template variable inside “<em>template.xml</em>” and “<em>report.xml</em>”.</p><p>The current template variable syntax:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e62d12b4-085e-45ac-80ff-df405fa3a4ae"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<variable>
  <name>Variable Name</name>
  <description>Variable Description</description>
  <type/>
  <value xml:space="preserve">Variable Value</value>
</variable>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Add &quot;com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool&quot; to a type value where the name is a variable name and the value is a full-qualified template tool class, for example:</p><p><br /></p><p>To automatically import TextTool</p><hr /><ol><li>Edit the built-in.xml file in the <em>...\data\reports\Tutorial 24 - Text Tool\reports</em>.</li><li>Add a variable as shown in the following example.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c01b07d8-75a2-4127-840a-5fcea178f72b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<variable>
  <name>text</name>
  <description>A text tool</description>
  <type>com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool</type>
  <value xml:space="preserve">com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool</value>
</variable>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The result is a template code that can access com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool from $text.</p><table><tbody><tr><td><pre>$text.noLineBreak($diagram.name)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table-4.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94bc0efd-71ea-4d5a-8742-94117ad9f534"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Once you have edited a template variable, you have to restart the application prior to generating a report through the modeling tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968794 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04676: Automated Package Structure Creation

- page_id: `243968794`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968794/Automated+Package+Structure+Creation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:29:01.191+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of copy-pasting an existing package structure, you can create one automatically. You can do that by creating your own package structure configurations first and then applying them to your model via the **Create Package Structure** action.

##### Creating a package structure configuration

To create a package structure configuration

1. Create a configuration element, which can be any named element (e.g. a Package named 'SubsystemPackages').
2. Right-click the configuration element. In the shortcut menu, click Stereotype > select «package StructureConfiguration » from the menu, and click Apply .
3. Right-click the configuration element and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
5. Under «package StructureConfiguration » select applyToElements and click the Create Value button.
6. In the **Select Elements** dialog, select the element types for which the **Create Package Structure** action will be available. Use the **Multiple Selection**option to specify multiple element types.
7. Under «package StructureConfiguration » select CreateElements and click the Create Value button.
8. In the **Value** box, create alist of packages that will be created. The packages' nesting structure should be defined using spacing as indents (see the image below). If, in addition to spacing, any indent symbols (e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.)are used to define the structure, see **Step 9**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. (Optional) If the structure defined in the **CreateElements** tag is specified using any indent symbols (e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.)that you do not want to include in the created packages' names, select **RemoveIndentSymbols**, click the **Edit Value**button and set it to *true*. By default, the **RemoveIndentSymbols**tag is set to *false*.
10. Click Close .

##### Creating a package structure automatically

To create a package structure automatically

1. Right-click an element for which you want to create the package structure defined in the configuration. In the shortcut menu, click **Tools**> **Create Package Structure**. The **Select Package Structure**dialog opens. The **Create Package Structure**action is only available for elements specified in the **applyToElements**tag of the package structure configuration.
2. In the Select Package Structure dialog, select the package structure configuration element that defines the package structure you want to create. Click OK . The selected package structure is created for the element.

MagicGrid project templates contain two package structure configurations included by default. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using packages' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

