<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966149 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04677: Automated project usages

- page_id: `243966149`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966149/Automated+project+usages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:54.248+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418314237

418314240

418314239

**Automated usages of other projects in the main project**are the usages fully managed by our modeling tool. For example, it can create, remove, and rearrange as necessary without user intervention.

##### Version 17.0.3 and later

Version 17.0.3 introduces a new type of project usages, **automated project usages**. Our modeling tool fully manages them. It creates, removes, and rearranges as necessary without user intervention. **Automated project usages** are not shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']**Used Project** dialog as only **user-defined usages** are shown there.

##### Before version 17.0.3

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, there was only one type of project usage. The user created these **user-defined usages** and they carried additional parameters; i.e., whether the used project was used in read-only or read/write mode, the used project loading options, which used project version was required, etc.

When modifying projects with complex composition, always keep the following condition in mind:

*Whenever there are model level references from elements in resource A (either a used project or the main project) to elements in resource B, there is a used project usage A → B.*

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could see this condition in action when working with used projects in read/write mode. Whenever you modified the contents of a used project so that it started to refer to another used project, and then you attempted to save it, our modeling tool would add an additional usage. You would then see the messages: “*Used project B was directly attached to A*” or “*Mounts were updated ...*”.

However, in some cases, this system behavior caused undesirable consequences, as shown in the following pages:

418321539

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project partitioning' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3fd32ca-0dc5-4b7d-8714-4b3f96e6297e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418314237</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="914c53c5-eb35-41a6-9e9c-772e495b2c55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418314240</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="299ed167-6c37-4b4c-9cc5-b38b9dfa127a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418314239</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Automated usages of other projects in the main project </strong>are the usages fully managed by our modeling tool. For example, it can create, remove, and rearrange as necessary without user intervention.</p><h3>Version 17.0.3 and later</h3><p>Version 17.0.3 introduces a new type of project usages, <strong>automated project usages</strong>. Our modeling tool fully manages them. It creates, removes, and rearranges as necessary without user intervention. <strong>Automated project usages</strong> are not shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Project</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> as only <strong>user-defined usages</strong> are shown there.</p><h3>Before version 17.0.3</h3><p>In earlier versions of our modeling tool, there was only one type of project usage. The user created these <strong>user-defined usages</strong> and they carried additional parameters; i.e., whether the used project was used in read-only or read/write mode, the used project loading options, which used project version was required, etc.</p><p>When modifying projects with complex composition, always keep the following condition in mind:</p><p style="text-align: left;margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Whenever there are model level references from elements in resource A (either a used project or the main project) to elements in resource B, there is a used project usage A → B.</em></p><p style="text-align: left;">In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could see this condition in action when working with used projects in read/write mode. Whenever you modified the contents of a used project so that it started to refer to another used project, and then you attempted to save it, our modeling tool would add an additional usage. You would then see the messages: “<em>Used project B was directly attached to A</em>” or “<em>Mounts were updated ...</em>”.</p><p>However, in some cases, this system behavior caused undesirable consequences, as shown in the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d7aea0e8-3a0a-4ea3-abac-46cfb553328d" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48be605c-1e90-4dc8-81d5-c339bc12456a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418321539</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing Used Projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project partitioning" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Partitioning]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561341 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04678: Automatic delegation

- page_id: `249561341`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561341/Automatic+delegation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-26T13:09:23.444+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''], the automatic delegation functionality:

- Creates a connector from the port to the nearest border.
- Creates connectors with relevant ports between the ports of the nested parts (delegate).

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via port

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via port

1. Select a port. To create connectors with relevant ports for ports within a nested ports structure, make sure to invoke the **Delegate Port(s)** action either from the part or the port enclosing a nested ports structure. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the port, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed port and connector are created to the nearest border:

- If a port is enclosed within a part that is not nested within another part, a connector is created to the nearest border - the diagram frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_1.png']
- If a port is enclosed within a part that is nested within another part, a connector is created to the nearest border - the enclosing part. [ATTACHMENT filename='Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_2.png']

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via part

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via part

1. Select a part containing a port.
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the part, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed port and connector are created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via connector

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via connector

1. Select a connector between two nested parts.
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the connector, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed ports and connectors are created automatically. If the connector has a flow, that flow is also applied to the delegated connectors (as shown in the figure below). Some adjustments might be necessary. They will be indicated by the validation rules.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Autowire Parts' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow management' space='']

the enclosing part.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_2.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Delegating ports and connectors automatically via part</h3><p>To delegate ports and connectors automatically via part</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b99663d9-e405-4629-8b88-4248a4f127c0">Select a part containing a port.</li><li data-uuid="d5f66534-b640-4fd8-8172-ac5bd7608aa6">In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools_button.png" /></ac:image>Tools &gt; <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the part, and in its shortcut menu, select <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol><p>The needed port and connector are created automatically.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_1.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Delegating ports and connectors automatically via connector</h3><p>To delegate ports and connectors automatically via connector</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3471b65d-a94c-4d24-af21-90bddd1f7d5a">Select a connector between two nested parts.</li><li data-uuid="a1bfe42c-b146-4841-ac55-8e330c95f6d6">In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools_button.png" /></ac:image>Tools &gt; <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the connector, and in its shortcut menu, select <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol><p>The needed ports and connectors are created automatically. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968732 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 04679: Automatic Instantiation Wizard

- page_id: `243968732`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968732/Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:04:19.685+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts.

While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypesto the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. Previously, this feature had been supported by the **SysML v1 plugin only**.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** analyzes the structure of the selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). All this information is presented in the first step of the wizard.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** evaluates collected information and provides a suggestion of all possible instances to be created.

If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation wizard,** you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. But if you want to change default values, create several different configurations, see the **Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard** and the **Case study:** sections on how to do that.

The **Automatic Instantiation Wizard** consists of three steps:

- Step #1. Selecting parts to be instantiated
- Step #2. Selecting a package
- Step #3. Creating a diagram (optional)

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the elements for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

**Related pages**

- 
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Link' space='MED']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED']
  - Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard
  - Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study
  - Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML Plugin

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968745 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04680: Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study

- page_id: `243968745`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968745/Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard+Case+Study
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:09.715+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Automatic Instantiation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Consider this example:**

Let’s say we have three classes named **Administrator**, **Librarian**, and **Personnel**. These classes are connected in the following way:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The multiplicity in the association end near the **Librarian** is 4. In this case, the **Automatic Instantiation wizard** suggests four parts.

In the following figure, these values are displayed as **[1]**, **[2]**, **[3]**, and **[4]**. Each of them have a set of properties inherited from the type of the parts. In this case, we cannot add more parts because the multiplicity points to the particular number of available parts. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.

In this case, class **Librarian** has a sub-class **Personnel**, so the type of **part [1]** can be changed to **Personnel** as you can see in the following figure.

Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:

1. Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.
2. Create a new package Instances of the Administrator .
3. Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.
4. After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the Finish button.
5. See the result in the following figure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Consider this example:</strong></p><p><span>Let’s say we have three classes named </span> <strong>Administrator</strong><span>, </span> <strong>Librarian</strong><span>, and </span> <strong>Personnel</strong><span>. These classes are connected in the following way:</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_classes.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><p>The multiplicity in the association end near the <strong>Librarian</strong> is 4. In this case, the <strong>Automatic Instantiation wizard</strong> suggests four parts.</p><p>In the following figure, these values are displayed as <strong>[1]</strong>, <strong>[2]</strong>, <strong>[3]</strong>, and <strong>[4]</strong>. Each of them have a set of properties inherited from the type of the parts. In this case, we cannot add more parts because the multiplicity points to the particular number of available parts. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.</p><p>In this case, class <strong>Librarian</strong> has a sub-class <strong>Personnel</strong>, so the type of <strong>part [1]</strong> can be changed to <strong>Personnel</strong> as you can see in the following figure.</p><p>Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:</p><ol><li>Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.</li><li>Create a new package <strong>Instances of the Administrator</strong>.</li><li>Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.</li><li>After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the <strong>Finish</strong> button. </li><li>See the result in the following figure. </li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561480 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 04681: Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML v1

- page_id: `249561480`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561480/Automatic+Instantiation+wizard+in+SysML+v1
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-09T09:58:18.813+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard analyzes the structure of a selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). It evaluates collected information and suggests all possible instances to be created.

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts. For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypes to the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

The wizard consists of the following areas:

- Steps area: displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.
- Element tree: displays the entire internal structure of the selected element.
- *Selection area*: allows you to select the elements to be instantiated.
- Instantiated properties area: allows you to change the default values and create several different sets of instances .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Automatic Instantiation wizard of the *Vehicle* Block with the main areas highlighted: Steps area, Element tree, Selection area, and Instantiated properties area.

##### Using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

To create and display instance specifications of Blocks in the diagram by using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the Block for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance (see the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='create_instance_command.png']
3. Follow the steps of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='']How to work in each step of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard >> If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard, you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. To change default values or create several different configurations, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space=''].
4. Click Finish when you are done. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] and a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] are created for the selected Block (see the figure below). All part instances are grouped under their owners which allows you to visually identify the structure of nested instances in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Block Definition Diagram with instances of the vehicle Block is created by using the Automatic Instantiation wizard.

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the**VehicleStructure**sample model. To open this sample, download*[ATTACHMENT filename='VehicleStructure.mdzip'].*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Automatic Instantiation </strong>wizard analyzes the structure of a selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). It evaluates collected information and suggests all possible instances to be created. </span> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts. For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. While creating instances automatically, you can:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="ec6425de-cb12-4086-8191-0629f8d668ee"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select properties for which the instances will be created.</span></li><li data-uuid="10a404fa-80e0-408c-85a8-a60750229a5b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Change types of the instantiated properties. <br /></span></li><li data-uuid="8fd62bce-a2c7-442a-843f-2edf4dacab97"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Change names of the instantiated properties. </span></li><li data-uuid="274012b3-2f63-4e93-9a7d-5b10391cdcf8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Apply custom stereotypes to the instantiated properties. </span></li><li data-uuid="a306b893-f676-45af-b5a2-4accc30b6eb2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Assign default values for the instantiated properties.</span></li></ul><p>The wizard consists of the following areas:</p><ul><li data-uuid="300800b6-ca08-42ce-be16-b681a6806de6"><em>Steps area:</em> displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.</li><li data-uuid="343dfb07-844d-42b9-adbf-6ea9aed57e8b"><em>Element tree:</em> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span>displays the entire internal structure of the selected element.</span> </span></li><li data-uuid="f8f51e83-f139-45cf-b542-f7b5f5d4f323"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span> <em>Selection area</em>: allows you to select the elements to be instantiated. </span> </span></li><li data-uuid="790f7399-0b29-44be-b67b-1d071be7eb27"><em>Instantiated properties area:</em> allows you to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">change the default values and create several different </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">sets of instances</span>.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatic_instantiation.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Automatic Instantiation wizard of the <em>Vehicle</em> Block with the main areas <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">highlighted</span>: Steps area, Element tree, Selection area, and Instantiated properties area.</h6><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Using the <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create and display instance specifications of Blocks in the diagram by using the <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6430a0bd-24bd-4de1-abc5-e268df4764f8"><span>Select the Block for which you want to create instances.</span></li><li data-uuid="7969ec94-edea-4c23-97b1-0561690f6b4b">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Instance</strong> (see the following figure).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_instance_command.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4d1a3f88-c264-44b6-b490-2dc00f7589cc"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Follow the steps of the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <strong>Automatic Instantiation </strong>wizard</span></span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard" /><ac:link-body>How to work in each step of the <strong>Automatic Instantiation </strong>wizard &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="648d4921-120b-4ec6-9bac-43292178a13d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the <strong>Automatic Instantiation </strong>wizard, you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. To change default values or create several different configurations, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard" /></ac:link>.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="38301f70-59bb-4bbe-8b7c-ebea7da4ae9e">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Instance Specifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and a new<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SysML Block Definition Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are created for the selected Block (see the figure below). All part instances are grouped under their owners which allows you to visually identify the structure of nested instances in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="displayed instances of the vehicle structure.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Block Definition Diagram with instances of the vehicle Block is created by using the Automatic Instantiation wizard.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p><span>The model used in the figures of this page is the </span><strong>VehicleStructure</strong><span> sample model. To open this sample, download</span><span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="VehicleStructure.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</em></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564897 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04682: Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF

- page_id: `249564897`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564897/Automatic+Instantiation+wizard+in+UAF
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:24.679+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004562780

1004562782

1004562781

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard is used to automatically instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

truenone

##### Selecting parts

Select elements and their internal parts to instantiate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting a package

Select, create or clone the package to store the created actual structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a diagram

Specify diagram name, select diagram type to visualize the actual structure and the package to store the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004562777

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968433 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04683: Automatic numbering

- page_id: `243968433`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968433/Automatic+numbering
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:27.189+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism > Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space=''] are already created.

With an automatic numbering feature, you can number the elements of the selected type upon the element creation or modification. You can specify the number display location after the elements are numbered.

To enable automatic element numbering

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the options group list, select or expand the General option and click Numbering either in the options list or in the options group list. The numbering properties opens.
3. Set the Use Element Auto-numbering property value to true .
4. Click OK .

To specify the element number display location

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Symbol Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens.
  - From the main menu, select Options > Project . Then, in the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog , select the Symbol styles > Default group.
2. Select one of the following **Element Numbering Display Mode** property value: Property valueVisualization on the element shapeAbove the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Before the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Do not display on symbol[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click OK after you have selected.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manual numbering' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can specify the number display location after the elements are numbered.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable automatic element numbering</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> opens.</li><li>In the options group list, select or expand the <strong>General</strong> option and click <strong>Numbering </strong>either in the options list or in the options group list. The numbering properties opens.</li><li>Set the <strong>Use Element Auto-numbering </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To specify the element number display location</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. The <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. Then, in the opened <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> &gt; <strong>Default</strong> group.</li></ul></li><li><p>Select one of the following <strong>Element Numbering Display Mode</strong> property value:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property value</th><th>Visualization on the element shape</th></tr><tr><td>Above the element name</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Numbering above the element name" ac:alt="Numbering above the element name"><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering_above_name.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td>Before the element name</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Numbering before the element name" ac:alt="Numbering before the element name"><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering_before_name.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Do not display on symbol</span></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Do not display a number on the element" ac:alt="Do not display a number on the element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering_DND.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> after you have selected.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf456959-0ef0-452c-8133-b5e57d689aa4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manual numbering" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[edit automatically applied element numbers]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965888 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04684: Autosave

- page_id: `243965888`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965888/Autosave
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:48.250+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you stop working with the modeling tool,an idle time passes and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. 
If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.

To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use

1. Open the Environment Options dialog.
2. In the General pane, select the Save/Load group, and then set the Save Project Recovery Data on Idle option to true .
3. Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the Idle Time to Activate Recovery Save box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After you stop working with the modeling tool,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> an idle time passes</span> and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> pane, select the <strong>Save/Load</strong> group, and then set the <strong>Save Project Recovery Data on Idle </strong>option to <em>true</em>. </li><li>Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the <strong>Idle</strong> <strong>Time</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Activate</strong> <strong>Recovery</strong> <strong>Save</strong> box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561331 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 04685: Autowire Parts

- page_id: `249561331`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561331/Autowire+Parts
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:09:07.547+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['automatic-connections', 'automatic-connectors', 'connect-parts', 'autowire', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Autowire Parts** capability analyzes and finds compatible ports/interfaces, and automatically creates [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'] between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']. The figure below demonstrates the *Thermal View* Internal Block diagram before and after Autowire Parts when the entire diagram is selected. This functionality provides a quick and easy way to create multiple connectors simultaneously.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']and ports are displayed or created in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''], the parts can be related to one another via [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='']Connectors. You can manually create these connections by using the **Connector** button from the part's/port's shape smart manipulator toolbar or from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='']. You can also do this automatically by using the **Autowire Parts** command from the Tools menu ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) in the diagram toolbar, if the following conditionsconditions are satisfied:

- Parts and ports are displayed on the Internal Block diagram pane.
- The displayed ports are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'].
- Ports are typed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] that have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] defined.
- Types and directions of these flow properties are compatible.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''] do not exist in the model.
- Only one-to-one connection is supported.
- Multiple interfaces are not supported.

See an example [ATTACHMENT filename='Autowire-Parts.mdzip']

The following procedure describes how to use the **Autowire Parts** function. This functionality works for an entire diagram or individually selected parts and ports.

To connect Part Properties via compatible ports automatically

1. Select any number of part or port shapes, or the diagram pane.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Tools > **Autowire Parts**, or press Ctrl+Alt+W. InformationIf new connectors are not created after clicking the **Autowire Parts**, check if all required are satisfied.
3. In the opened Messages window, you can specify two options: [ATTACHMENT filename='autowiring_message.png']
  - Highlight new connections - leave the check box selected, if you want the newly created Connectors to be highlighted on the diagram pane, and be able to confirm or delete them manually.
  - Show this message next time - leave the check box selected, if you want this message to appear the next time you click the Autowire Parts command.
4. Click OK .
5. The Connector(s) between Part Properties via compatible interfaces are created in the model. If you left the Highlight new connection check box selected in the Messages window (step 3, first bullet), you must review the newly created Connectors in the following way (see the figure below): a. Select the Connector. b. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connector.png'] and choose: - Confirm Connector - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Delete Connector - removes the Connector from the model. - Ignore - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Select In Validation Results - opens the Active Validation Results table. [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connection.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> capability analyzes and finds compatible ports/interfaces, and automatically <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">creates <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> between <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>. The figure below demonstrates the <em>Thermal View</em> Internal Block diagram before and after Autowire Parts when the entire diagram is selected. This functionality provides a quick and easy way to create multiple connectors simultaneously.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Thermal View Internal Block diagram before and after Autowiring." ac:alt="The Thermal View Internal Block diagram before and after Autowiring."><ri:attachment ri:filename="autowiring_ibd.png" /></ac:image></p><p>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[parts ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and ports are displayed or created in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link>, the parts can be related to one another via <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Connector</span>s</ac:link-body></ac:link>. You can manually create these connections by using the <strong>Connector</strong> button from the part's/port's shape smart manipulator toolbar or from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can also do this automatically by using the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> command from the Tools menu (<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools.png" /></ac:image>) in the diagram toolbar, if the following <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c4ff79e-dd59-454d-96cd-b0abd4201673"><ac:parameter ac:name="">conditions</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>conditions are is supported. </li><li>Multiple interfaces are not supported.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a49c8f77-3b0b-4c0e-a699-7e3c5f097604"><ac:rich-text-body><p>See an example <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Autowire-Parts.mdzip" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br />The following procedure describes how to use the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> function. This functionality works for an entire diagram or individually selected parts and ports.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect Part Properties via compatible ports automatically</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select any number of part or port shapes, or the diagram pane.</p></li><li><p>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools.png" /></ac:image> Tools &gt; <strong>Autowire Parts</strong>, or press Ctrl+Alt+W.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1da4ecc1-043b-41d5-87e2-788412402bd8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If new connectors are not created after clicking the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong>, check if all required<ac:link ac:anchor="conditions"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ conditions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are satisfied.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>In the opened Messages window, you can specify two options:</p><ul><li>Highlight new connections - leave the check box selected, if you want the newly created Connectors to be highlighted on the diagram pane, and be able to confirm or delete them manually.</li><li>Show this message next time - leave the check box selected, if you want this message to appear the next time you click the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> command.</li></ul><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="autowiring_message.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>The Connector(s) between Part Properties <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">via compatible interfaces</span> are created in the model. If you left the <strong>Highlight new connection</strong> check box selected in the Messages window (step 3, first bullet), you must review the newly created Connectors in the following way (see the figure below):<br />       a. Select the Connector.<br />       b. Click <ac:image ac:title="Confirm connection!"><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm_connector.png" /></ac:image> and choose:<strong><br />             - Confirm Connector </strong>- removes the highlight from the Connector.<br /><strong>             - Delete Connector </strong>- removes the Connector from the model.<strong> </strong><br />            <strong> - Ignore</strong> - removes the highlight from the Connector.<strong> </strong><br />             <strong>-</strong> <strong>Select In Validation Results</strong> - opens the Active Validation Results table.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Confirming newly created Connector after Autowiring function when the Highlight new connection check box was selected in the Messages window." ac:alt="Confirming newly created Connector after Autowiring function when the Highlight new connection check box was selected in the Messages window."><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm_connection.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562471 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04687: AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `249562471`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562471/AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:46.168+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-1-overview-and-summary-information', 'av-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229302728

229302730

229302729

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.

The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:

- In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.
- When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.

The AV-1 may be used to:

- Scope the architecture effort.
- Provide context to the architecture effort.
- Define the architecture effort.
- Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.
- Assist search within an architecture repository.

**Implementation**

The AV-1 can be represented:

- Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='av_1.png']
- Using the following (.docx) reports: - AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. - AV-1 DARS.

The AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.

The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.

229302727

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Description' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Reference' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architecture Metadata' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating DARS report' space='']

The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d928db-eff2-4732-bb69-65fec83182d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229302727</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architectural Description" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architectural Reference" /></ac:link></span><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architecture Metadata" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating DARS report" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating DARS template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562474 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04688: AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `249562474`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562474/AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:46.255+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-2', 'av-2-integrated-dictionary', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229449780

229449782

229449781

**Description**

The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).

An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.

It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:

- Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.
- Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.
- Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.
- Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- An AV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.

229449779

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Definition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alias' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Same As' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).</p><p>An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.</p><p>It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:</p><ul><li>Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.</li><li>Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.</li><li>Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.</li><li>Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-2 can be represented:</p><ul><li>An AV-2 table.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" ac:alt="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary"><ri:attachment ri:filename="av_2.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fabe6b99-9e45-45b7-8985-b2e5fc71e744"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229449779</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Definition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Alias" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Same As" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968666 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04689: Available Transformations

- page_id: `243968666`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968666/Available+Transformations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:06.413+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:

- Any to Any . This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.
- Profile Migration . Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).

UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).

Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:</p><ul><li><strong>Any to Any</strong>. This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Profile Migration</strong>. Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d03b38f1-c9ac-4094-9c27-5adee5b78388"><ac:rich-text-body><p>UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83a24d82-c890-4d54-9bba-50ac5c213ddd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967199 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04690: Avoiding Conflicting Legend items

- page_id: `243967199`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967199/Avoiding+Conflicting+Legend+items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:24.746+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends > Specifying Legend Items
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you use multiple Legends in one diagram, you should specify a different set of adornment properties for Legend Items of each Legend. This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using **Fill Color** in one Legend and **Icon** in another. If you want to use the **Icon** property in multiple Legends, then specify different **Icon Position on Shape**/**Path** for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the **Pen Color**, **Line Width,** and **Line Style** properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.

#### TIP: Legend Item priority

Legend Item priority

If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the **Priority** property for each Legend Item. It defines which Legend Item should be applied in case several Legend Items conflict.

##### Example

Let's say you want to use two Legends in a Requirements Diagram: the *Status* Legend indicating the status of each Requirement and the *Risk* Legend showing the risk level of Requirements. It is likely that Legend Items from both Legends will be applied to the same Requirement. For example, a single Requirement can have both status and risk values specified. Let's analyze this specific use case by creating the Legends mentioned above and defining their Legend Item adornment properties so that Legend Items would not conflict when applied to the same Requirement:

1. Open the desired Requirements Diagram.
2. In the diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''] intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend Status .
3. Create and define the Legend Items for the Status Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them Closed , Approved , Disapproved, and In Progress .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='']Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Status property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.
4. In the same diagram, create a Legend to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend Risk .
5. Create and define the Legend Items for the Risk Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. Create three Legend Items indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them High , Medium , and Low .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired **Icon** and **Icon Position on Shape**. Specifying IconWhen specifying the **Icon** property:Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select an image from the Image Library of your modeling tool.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select a custom image from the local file system.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to specify the URL of an image.
  4. Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the **Risk** property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.

If you followed the above steps, you have a Requirements Diagram with the *Status* and *Risk* Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict when applied to the same shape, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure displays a Requirement shape with two Legend Items applied to it. 
The Legend Items do not conflict due to different adornment properties used to define them.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you use multiple Legends in one diagram, you should specify a different set of adornment properties for Legend Items of each Legend. This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using <strong>Fill Color</strong> in one Legend and <strong>Icon</strong> in another. If you want to use the <strong>Icon</strong> property in multiple Legends, then specify different <strong>Icon Position on Shape</strong>/<strong>Path</strong> for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the <strong>Pen Color</strong>, <strong>Line Width,</strong> and <strong>Line Style</strong> properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ade7b350-1fd3-44cc-9bdc-bf32eb39765d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Legend Item priority</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the <strong>Priority</strong> property for each Legend Item. It defines which Legend Item should be applied in case several Legend Items conflict.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Example</h3><p>Let's say you want to use two Legends in a Requirements Diagram: the <em>Status</em> Legend indicating the status of each Requirement and the <em>Risk</em> Legend showing the risk level of Requirements. It is likely that Legend Items from both Legends will be applied to the same Requirement. For example, a single Requirement can have both status and risk values specified. Let's analyze this specific use case by creating the Legends mentioned above and defining their Legend Item adornment properties so that Legend Items would not conflict when applied to the same Requirement:<br /><br /></p><ol><li>Open the desired Requirements Diagram.</li><li>In the diagram, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a legend]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend <em>Status</em>.</li><li>Create and define the Legend Items for the <em>Status</em> Legend by completing the following steps:<br /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and managing Legend Items" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create four Legend Items]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them <em>Closed</em>, <em>Approved</em>, <em>Disapproved,</em> and <em>In Progress</em>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.</li><li>Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired <strong>Fill Color</strong>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" /><ac:link-body>Specify the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> property</ac:link-body></ac:link> by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the <strong>Status</strong> property value the Legend Item represents.</li><li>Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.</li></ol></li><li>In the same diagram, create a Legend to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend <em>Risk</em>.</li><li>Create and define the Legend Items for the <em>Risk</em> Legend by completing the following steps:<br /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link">Create three Legend Items</span> indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them <em>High</em>, <em>Medium</em>, and <em>Low</em>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired <strong>Icon</strong> and <strong>Icon Position on Shape</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cac0a3ad-7d37-47b0-861c-5ee8c2841e48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Specifying Icon</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When specifying the <strong>Icon</strong> property:</p><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_library.png" /></ac:image> to select an image from the Image Library of your modeling tool.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_image.png" /></ac:image> to select a custom image from the local file system.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="url.png" /></ac:image> to specify the URL of an image.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the <strong>Risk</strong> property value the Legend Item represents.</p></li><li>Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.</li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you followed the above steps, you have a Requirements Diagram with the <em>Status</em> and <em>Risk</em> Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict when applied to the same shape, as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_adornment.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The figure displays a Requirement shape with two Legend Items applied to it.</span><br style="text-align: center;" /><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The Legend Items do not conflict due to different adornment properties used to define them.</span></h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965836 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04691: Background Task Manager

- page_id: `243965836`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965836/Background+Task+Manager
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:21:50.601+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427371678

1427371681

1427371679

Low-priority or routine tasks are moved to separate threads and are performed as background tasks. These tasks do not stop a modeling tool from running, and you can work with your model as usual. Background tasks are displayed in the Background Task Manager at the bottom right corner of the modeling tool window. For example, when a search process is running, the progress bar is displayed in the Background Task Manager, shown in the following figure.

If a background task runs for too long, you can cancel it as described below.

To end an ongoing process

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='end_process.png'] in the Background Task Manager status bar to cancel the selected process. If you cancel the last of several tasks (or only one existing process), the Background Task Manager is turned off.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Background Task Manager showing the progress of a searching task.

1427371677

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space='']

These tasks do not stop a modeling tool from running, and you can work with your model as usual. Background tasks are displayed in the Background Task Manager at the bottom right corner of the modeling tool window. For example, when a search process is running, the progress bar is displayed in the Background Task Manager, shown in the following figure.</p><p>If a background task runs for too long, you can cancel it as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To end an ongoing process</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="end_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Background Task Manager status bar to cancel the selected process. If you cancel the last of several tasks (or only one existing process), the Background Task Manager is turned off.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="progress_bar.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Background Task Manager showing the progress of a searching task.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6114c90-5668-43eb-a1ec-3efcad6b5ae9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427371677</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Improving performance" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory allocation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation period" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 04692: Backward traceability – specification

- page_id: `243968925`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968925/Backward+traceability+%E2%80%93+specification

- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Traceability > Predefined traceability rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Property Name | Description | Applied For: | Reference Through: | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested in Artifacts | The property shows which components are manifested in an artifact. | Artifact | Relationships: Manifestation | Component | Example |
| Specifying Class | The property shows the classes representing the component specification from the Design model. | Component | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Specifying Component | The property shows the components that are realized by classifiers through component realization. | Classifier | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Component | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases (from the Requirements model) representing the class specification. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases that specify the given use case in the higher level of abstraction. For example, the Business Use Case specifies the Requirements Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realized Interface | The property shows the interfaces specifying the contract, in which the related classifier conforms to. | Classifier | Relationships: Interface Realization | Interface | Example |
| Specifying Element, All Specifying ElementsAvailable in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Specifying Element property gathers specifying elements from the upper abstraction level. The All Specifying Elements property transitively gathers specifying elements from all upper abstraction levels. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |
